Financial aid for those who cannot afford premiums after subsidies and with insufficient MediSave, and have limited family support

Open to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents living overseas

title: Additional Premium Support for MediShield Life and CareShield Life
tagline: Provides financial support for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who are unable to afford their MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums (where applicable) even after Government subsidies and MediSave use and have limited family support.
text:
Eligibility for APS is based on:

The financial means of the applicant and his/her immediate family members

You will not be eligible for APS if you have sufficient MediSave to pay for your MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums or have purchased an Integrated Shield Plan and/or ElderShield/CareShield Life Supplements

If you are granted APS and purchase an Integrated Shield Plan and/or ElderShield/CareShield Life Supplement afterwards, you will stop receiving APS

title: Additional Premium Support for MediShield Life and CareShield Life
tagline: Provides financial support for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who are unable to afford their MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums (where applicable) even after Government subsidies and MediSave use and have limited family support.
text:
You will be invited to apply for APS if you have insufficient MediSave and have limited family support to help you pay for your MediShield Life premiums

The invitation will include an application form with details on the application process, which must be submitted along with the relevant supporting documents

There is no need for you to apply for APS ahead of time

Applications may take up to 20 working days to be assessed, and applicants will receive a notification on the application outcome

Financial aid for those who cannot afford premiums after subsidies and with insufficient MediSave, and have limited family support

Open to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents living overseas

Covers MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums (where applicable) for the next 2 policy years upon approval.

Eligibility for APS is based on:

The financial means of the applicant and his/her immediate family members

You will not be eligible for APS if you have sufficient MediSave to pay for your MediShield Life and/or CareShield Life premiums or have purchased an Integrated Shield Plan and/or ElderShield/CareShield Life Supplements

If you are granted APS and purchase an Integrated Shield Plan and/or ElderShield/CareShield Life Supplement afterwards, you will stop receiving APS

You will be invited to apply for APS if you have insufficient MediSave and have limited family support to help you pay for your MediShield Life premiums

The invitation will include an application form with details on the application process, which must be submitted along with the relevant supporting documents

There is no need for you to apply for APS ahead of time

Applications may take up to 20 working days to be assessed, and applicants will receive a notification on the application outcome

tagline: Provides educational financial support for eligible Singapore Citizens' post-secondary education.
text:
Funds can be used for approved programmes, repay government loans, and financial schemes
No application needed

title: Post-Secondary Education Account (PSEA)
tagline: Provides educational financial support for eligible Singapore Citizens' post-secondary education.
text:
PSEA Funds can be used to pay:

one's own or siblings’ approved education programmes at approved institutions

government education loans and financial schemes

PSEA balances earn interest at the same rate as the CPF Ordinary Account (CPF-OA), currently 2.5% per annum

Unused PSEA funds will be transferred to the CPF-OA when the account holder turns 31, subject to CPF-OA usage rules

title: Post-Secondary Education Account (PSEA)
tagline: Provides educational financial support for eligible Singapore Citizens' post-secondary education.
text:
You must be a Singapore Citizen with a balance in either:

the Child Development Account (CDA) when turning 13 years old

the Edusave Account when turning 17 years old or is no longer studying in a Ministry of Education (MOE)-funded school, whichever is later

The PSEA will also be opened for Singapore Citizens who are:

eligible for PSEA top-ups by the Government

recipient of the first payment of the National Service Housing, Medical and Education (NS HOME) Awards

recipient of the annual education bursary under the Home Ownership Plus Education (HOPE) Scheme

title: Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief for eligible working mothers, up to 25% of earned income per child. Encourages married women to stay in the workforce after having children, and rewards families with children who are Singapore Citizens.

title: Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief for eligible working mothers, up to 25% of earned income per child. Encourages married women to stay in the workforce after having children, and rewards families with children who are Singapore Citizens.
text:
Relief of up to 25% of working mother’s earned income per child or $12,000 per child
Receive cumulative tax relief for multiple children, up to 100% of your earnable income

title: Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief for eligible working mothers, up to 25% of earned income per child. Encourages married women to stay in the workforce after having children, and rewards families with children who are Singapore Citizens.
text:
Up to 25% of the mother's earned income can be claimed as tax relief for each child

Relief amount is cumulative for multiple children, capped at 100% of the mother's earned income

Relief may be claimed alongside the Qualifying Child Relief (QCR) or Child Relief (Disability) on the same child

The total relief cap is $50,000 per child for all 3 combined

title: Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief for eligible working mothers, up to 25% of earned income per child. Encourages married women to stay in the workforce after having children, and rewards families with children who are Singapore Citizens.
text:
To be eligible for WMCR, you must fulfil all the following conditions in the previous year :

You are a working mother who is married, divorced or widowed

You have taxable earned income from employment, through pensions, trade or business, or via profession or vocation

You have cared for a child who was a Singapore Citizen as at 31 December of the previous year and qualifies for QCR / Child Relief (Disability)

You may read more on the QCR and Child Relief (Disability) schemes and their criteria here)

You may still claim WMCR even for children who have passed away in the previous year

The amount of WMCR you may claim is based on child order. 

Child order

	

WMCR amount from YA 2025

	

For a Qualifying Singaporean child born/adopted before 1 Jan 2024^

	

For a Qualifying Singaporean

child born/adopted

on or after 1  Jan 2024

1st

	

15% of mother's earned income

	

$8,000

2nd

	

20% of mother's earned income

	

$10,000

3rd and beyond

	

25% of mother's earned income

	

$12,000

^ WMCR percentages are added together if you claim for more than one child and the total is capped at 100% of the mother's earned income.

You may claim still WMCR even if you and / or your husband or ex-husband has already claimed the QCR / Child Relief (Disability) schemes on the same child. However, you must note the following conditions:

QCR / Child Relief (Disability) claims must be utilised first (claims may be made by you or your spouse)

The total relief cap (i.e. QCR / Child Relief (Disability) plus WMCR) is $50,000 per child

The WMCR is limited to the remaining balance after the QCR / Child Relief (Disability) claim is allowed

title: Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief for eligible working mothers, up to 25% of earned income per child. Encourages married women to stay in the workforce after having children, and rewards families with children who are Singapore Citizens.
text:
For e-Filing:

	

For paper filing:

If you previously claimed WMCR last year and your circumstances remain unchanged, the relief amount will be pre-filled and automatically granted

If claiming the relief for the first time:

Log in to myTax Portal using your Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA)

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Child”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Yes” under “Claim Working Mother’s Child Relief”

Select “Update”

	

Complete the section on “Child Relief” in Form B1 & B

If it’s your first time claiming WMCR, indicate 'Y' under the “Claim for WMCR” column and fill in your child's details in Appendix 2 as well

The WMCR amount will be computed and applied automatically based on your eligibility when your Income Tax Return is processed.

title: CPF Education Loan Scheme
tagline: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).
text: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).

title: CPF Education Loan Scheme
tagline: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).
text:
Allows for the use of OA savings to pay for the government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs)
Applications will only be processed after the application period for the approved educational institution has closed
Processing of application generally takes 3 working days upon receipt of the student's enrolment confirmation from the approved educational institution

title: CPF Education Loan Scheme
tagline: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).
text:
Allows the use of OA savings to pay for own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs)

The student (i.e., borrower) would need to start repaying the principal amount withdrawn plus the accrued interest in cash, to the lender’s CPF OA, one year after graduating from or leaving the course of study, whichever occurs earlier

Note:

OA savings can be used up to the Available Withdrawal Limit under the CPF Education Loan Scheme

The Available Withdrawal Limit is either 40% of your accumulated OA savings, or your remaining OA balance, whichever is lower

The amount that can be used is also subject to the tuition fees payable

If you are 55 or older, you must set aside the Full Retirement Sum (FRS) in your Retirement Account before using the remaining OA savings, up to the Available Withdrawal Limit.

You may wish to check how much tuition fees can be paid using OA savings.

title: CPF Education Loan Scheme
tagline: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).
text:
CPF members/lenders with OA savings are eligible to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, or relative’s government subsidised tuition fees using OA savings, up to the Available Withdrawal Limit

The Available Withdrawal Limit is up to 40% of accumulated OA savings, or the remaining OA balance, whichever is lower

The CPF member can check the Available Withdrawal Limit through the CPF website using Singpass

Only full-time diploma / degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs) are covered under this loan scheme

For the list of approved courses, you may refer to approved courses under this scheme

Here are the alternative education financing options:

MOE Tuition Fee Loan

Other financing options offered by the institutions

Post-Secondary Education Account funds

title: CPF Education Loan Scheme
tagline: A loan scheme that allows CPF member to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to pay for their own, children’s, spouse’s, siblings' or relatives' government subsidised tuition fees for full-time diploma/degree courses at Approved Educational Institutions (AEIs).
text:
Apply online through “Forms and e-applications" at CPF website with your Singpass

You must read the accompanying Terms & Conditions, and Procedures & Guidelines before you complete the online form

Before you submit your application online under the CPF Education Loan Scheme:

Check the application period for the approved educational institution you are applying to use CPF savings to pay your tuition fees

Ensure you are accepted by the institution and confirm that it is an approved educational institution under the CPF Education Loan Scheme

Establish whether you are a new (e.g. fresh intake for AY2024/2025) or existing student (e.g. 2nd year student) under the institution

You are required to submit 1 (one) application form for each CPF lender, for the entire course of study, unless you enrol into a different AEI

Please note the application period for use of CPF savings under CPF Education Loan Scheme differs for each AEI

You may refer to their schedules here

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings for Immediate Needs from Age 55
tagline: CPF members can make withdrawals from their CPF savings starting from age 55.
text: CPF members can make withdrawals from their CPF savings starting from age 55.

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings for Immediate Needs from Age 55
tagline: CPF members can make withdrawals from their CPF savings starting from age 55.
text:
CPF members can make withdrawals from their CPF savings starting from age 55
Generally, members who turn 55 can withdraw at least $5,000 or any amount in excess after setting aside their Full Retirement Sum (FRS)
Members who own an eligible property have the flexibility to set aside their FRS with a mixture of property and cash, and withdraw their Retirement Account savings above the Basic Retirement Sum

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings for Immediate Needs from Age 55
tagline: CPF members can make withdrawals from their CPF savings starting from age 55.
text:
Generally, upon turning 55, members can withdraw at least $5,000 or any amount in excess after setting aside their Full Retirement Sum (FRS).  

Members born in 1958 and after can withdraw an additional amount of up to 20% of their retirement savings when they turn 65.

Property owners with remaining lease that lasts them until age 95 or older, have the flexibility to set aside their FRS with a mixture of property (up to half the FRS, which is half the Basic Retirement Sum (BRS)) and cash, and withdraw their Retirement Account savings above their BRS.

title: CAREbuddy
tagline:
CAREbuddy is a supportive service for caregivers, provided by trained volunteers (CAREbuddy) who are also caregivers to dependents with disabilities.

CAREbuddy programme is 6-months long during which the CAREbuddy provides emotional support, shares resources, and helps caregivers progress in their caregiving journey.
text:
CAREbuddy is a supportive service for caregivers, provided by trained volunteers (CAREbuddy) who are also caregivers to dependents with disabilities.

CAREbuddy programme is 6-months long during which the CAREbuddy provides emotional support, shares resources, and helps caregivers progress in their caregiving journey.

title: CAREbuddy
tagline:
CAREbuddy is a supportive service for caregivers, provided by trained volunteers (CAREbuddy) who are also caregivers to dependents with disabilities.

CAREbuddy programme is 6-months long during which the CAREbuddy provides emotional support, shares resources, and helps caregivers progress in their caregiving journey.
text:
Caregivers will be matched with a CAREbuddy of a similar profile, according to availability.
The CAREbuddy supports the Caregiver to work through personal and family goals, in ways that bring about positive changes.
97% of caregivers felt connected to their CAREbuddy and enjoyed good rapport / communication.

title: CAREbuddy
tagline:
CAREbuddy is a supportive service for caregivers, provided by trained volunteers (CAREbuddy) who are also caregivers to dependents with disabilities.

CAREbuddy programme is 6-months long during which the CAREbuddy provides emotional support, shares resources, and helps caregivers progress in their caregiving journey.
text:
CAREbuddy program consists of a network of trained volunteers who are caregivers to persons with dependents with disabilities themselves.  These trained volunteers befriend caregivers on a one-to-one basis, lend a listening ear, and journey with caregivers over a six-month period with at least 4 touchpoints to bring about positive changes in their caregiving journey.

 

Caregivers will be paired with a CAREbuddy who shares a similar profile, based on availability.  

The dates and timing of the meet ups will be arranged according to both the CAREbuddy’s and Caregiver’s convenience.

 

CAREbuddy volunteers understand the challenges and experiences of caring for dependents with special needs or disabilities, as they are caregiver themselves.

 

Kindly note that CAREbuddy programme does not provide respite and emergency support. CAREbuddy volunteer is not a mental health or special needs professional and will not be able to support the caregiver in areas where professional knowledge and skills are required.  CAREbuddy volunteers do not provide 24 hours hotline, SOS service, child-minding or child-buddy service.

title: CAREbuddy
tagline:
CAREbuddy is a supportive service for caregivers, provided by trained volunteers (CAREbuddy) who are also caregivers to dependents with disabilities.

CAREbuddy programme is 6-months long during which the CAREbuddy provides emotional support, shares resources, and helps caregivers progress in their caregiving journey.
text:
CAREbuddy program is a highly subsidised program made affordable to all caregivers of persons with special needs.

 

Interested caregivers can contact CaringSG to find out the eligible subsidies available to them.

Kindly note that CAREbuddy programme does not provide respite and emergency support. CAREbuddy volunteer is not a mental health or special needs professional and will not be able to support the caregiver in areas where professional knowledge and skills are required.  CAREbuddy volunteers do not provide 24 hours hotline, SOS service, child-minding or child-buddy service.

CAREbuddy program is a highly subsidised program made affordable to all caregivers of persons with special needs.

title: Enhanced Climate Friendly Households Programme (Climate Vouchers)
tagline: Encourage households to be more efficient in their energy and water consumption by helping to defray the upfront cost of adopting such energy and water-efficient appliances.
text:
Expansion of eligible products to include 5-tick clothes dryers and induction stoves
Top-up of $100 to existing $300 Climate Vouchers quantum for HDB households
$400 Climate Vouchers extended to all Singaporean private property households

title: Enhanced Climate Friendly Households Programme (Climate Vouchers)
tagline: Encourage households to be more efficient in their energy and water consumption by helping to defray the upfront cost of adopting such energy and water-efficient appliances.
text:
Each Singaporean and Permanent Resident (PR) HDB household is entitled to a total of $400 Climate Vouchers comprising $300 Climate Vouchers from April 2024 and an additional $100 Climate Vouchers from 15 April 2025

Each Singaporean household living in a private residential property is entitled to a total of $400 Climate Vouchers from 15 April 2025

The list of eligible products will be expanded to include 5-tick clothes dryers and induction stoves from 15 April 2026

Multiple families or tenants living in the same registered address are collectively entitled to one set of Climate Vouchers for the same registered address or flat

text: Empower lower-income families to achieve stability, self-reliance, and social mobility.

title: ComLink+
tagline: Empower lower-income families to achieve stability, self-reliance, and social mobility.
text:
Family coaches from MSF's Social Service Offices and case workers from Family Service Centres journey with families and support them as they work towards their long-term goals
ComLink+ families will receive financial top-ups through ComLink+ Progress Packages when they take active steps towards their longer-term goals in the areas of preschool education, employment, debt clearance, and home ownership

title: ComLink+
tagline: Empower lower-income families to achieve stability, self-reliance, and social mobility.
text:
Under ComLink+ Progress Packages, families that have taken active steps to improve their circumstances can receive: 

[New – from Q3 2026] Partnership Payout of $500 per quarter for continued engagement with their family coaches or case workers at Family Service Centres 

[Revised – from Q3 2026] Up to $250 per quarter for regular preschool attendance

[Revised – from Q3 2026] Up to $1,250 per quarter for stable employment (based on CPF records)

1:1 matched debt repayment up to a limit of $5,000 in total debt repaid

2:1 matched CPF contribution to help families save up for purchase of flat

[Revised - from Q3 2026] Total payouts across all ComLink+ Progress Packages are subject to a lifetime cap of $50,000

title: ComLink+
tagline: Empower lower-income families to achieve stability, self-reliance, and social mobility.
text:
Families are eligible for ComLink+ if they have children under the age of 21 and are: 

Living in public rental housing (PRH); or 

Not living in PRH, but are eligible for KidSTART or have school-going children at risk of long-term absenteeism

title: CPF Housing Scheme
tagline: Enables CPF members to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to buy properties and pay for various housing purchase-related costs if they are owners of the property.
text: Use CPF Ordinary Account (OA) savings to buy properties or pay for housing purchase-related costs

title: CPF Housing Scheme
tagline: Enables CPF members to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to buy properties and pay for various housing purchase-related costs if they are owners of the property.
text:
Enables members to own properties in Singapore

OA savings can also be used to cover downpayments, housing loans, stamp and legal fees and Home Protection Scheme premiums (for HDB flats)

title: CPF Housing Scheme
tagline: Enables CPF members to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to buy properties and pay for various housing purchase-related costs if they are owners of the property.
text:
The amount of OA savings members can use depends on several factors, including the leasehold of the property, the loan you’re taking, the age of the youngest owner using CPF, and the number of properties you are using CPF for

For detailed information on the terms and conditions, please refer to the terms and conditions for use of CPF savings under the CPF Housing Scheme

Upon sale, transfer or disposal of the property (during your lifetime), you are required to refund the CPF principal amount withdrawn together with the accrued interest to your CPF account(s)

If you are aged 55 or above and had pledged the property to make up your retirement sum, you will also be required to refund the pledged amount

title: CPF Housing Scheme
tagline: Enables CPF members to use their Ordinary Account (OA) savings to buy properties and pay for various housing purchase-related costs if they are owners of the property.
text:
To utilise your OA savings for the property, HDB or the lawyer acting for you in the purchase must submit an application to the CPF Board with your CPF withdrawal details, along with any required documentation

Know how much OA you can use

Before your OA savings can be released, you will need to pay any cash difference, which is the purchase price less housing loan (if any) and the lump sum payment from your CPF account

Note that the savings withdrawn are paid to the property developer, HDB, sellers, banks, lawyers or such other entities on your behalf

To adjust or stop your OA savings for your loan payments, login to your HDB Infopage if you have an HDB loan or myCPF if you have a bank loan

title: Assistive Technology Fund (ATF)
tagline: Provides subsidies for persons with disabilities (PWDs) to purchase assistive technology devices to enable independent living. The devices should aid early intervention, education, training, employment, therapy, rehabilitation and/or independence in daily living for PWDs.
text:
Subsidy of up to 90% of the cost of the required assistive technology device (lifetime cap of $40,000)

Subsidy amount is determined according to the applicant's monthly household income

Funds can be used to buy, replace, upgrade, or repair assistive technology devices

The Fund does not support consumables. The Fund will cover medical devices (e.g. oxygen concentrators and suction pumps) and spectacles for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents at or above age 60, who remain served by ATF.

title: Assistive Technology Fund (ATF)
tagline: Provides subsidies for persons with disabilities (PWDs) to purchase assistive technology devices to enable independent living. The devices should aid early intervention, education, training, employment, therapy, rehabilitation and/or independence in daily living for PWDs.
text:
Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

A hearing impairment: Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Undergone qualified assessor’s assessment to determine the need and type of device(s)

Monthly Per Capita Household Income of $4,800 and below

Annual Value (AV) of residence reflected on NRIC of $21,000 and below for households with no income

Note for Singaporean and Permanent Resident Seniors:

Subject to eligibility,

(a) Singaporean and Permanent Resident seniors who have not tapped on ATF before the age of 60 will be supported by the Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund (SMF). Please apply to SMF.

(b) Singaporean and Permanent Resident seniors who have tapped on ATF before the age of 60 will be supported by ATF throughout their lifetime.

title: Assistive Technology Fund (ATF)
tagline: Provides subsidies for persons with disabilities (PWDs) to purchase assistive technology devices to enable independent living. The devices should aid early intervention, education, training, employment, therapy, rehabilitation and/or independence in daily living for PWDs.
text:
Approach a therapist or a social worker from public hospitals or social service agencies to assess and assist you to submit the application

If you are not currently under the care of any public hospitals or social service agencies, you may approach a polyclinic to obtain a referral letter to a public hospital for an assessment and thereafter submit the application.

Before meeting your touchpoints, all applicants are encouraged to check if your disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere with your Singpass. If you do not have a verified disability status, the touchpoint's registered healthcare professional will be able to guide you with the disability verification.

Means testing is required and this will be assisted by the social worker at the public hospitals or social service agencies.

title: Baby Bonus Cash Gift
tagline: Help parents defray child-raising costs through regular cash payouts in their child's early years.
text:
Baby Bonus Cash Gift enhanced by $3,000 for eligible Singaporean children
Enhancements will apply to eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023
Apply online on LifeSG app/website

title: Baby Bonus Cash Gift
tagline: Help parents defray child-raising costs through regular cash payouts in their child's early years.
text:
Eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023 will qualify for the enhanced Baby Bonus Cash Gift announced at Budget 2023.

Eligible Singaporean children in the first and second birth order will receive $11,000 while children in the third and subsequent birth orders will receive $13,000.

Cash Gift will be disbursed on a regular basis, every six months until the child is six-and-a-half years old.

The Baby Bonus Cash Gift will be paid into the new Child Savings Account (CSA), which is a joint savings account that opens automatically at the same time with the Child Development Account (CDA) when a child is enrolled for the Baby Bonus Scheme. With a savings account, it will be easier for the child to receive Government payouts, like the Baby Bonus cash gift, education awards and financial assistance.

title: Adoption Leave for Mothers (AL)
tagline: Provides up to 12 weeks of leave for eligible adoptive mothers to care for their adopted child.
text:
Up to 12 weeks of Adoption Leave for eligible adoptive mothers
Government reimbursement capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000 for the first and second child orders
For the third and subsequent child orders, the government will reimburse up to $30,000

title: Adoption Leave for Mothers (AL)
tagline: Provides up to 12 weeks of leave for eligible adoptive mothers to care for their adopted child.
text:
Up to 12 weeks of Adoption Leave (AL) for mothers to care for their adopted infant.

For the first and second child, the employer pays for the first 4 weeks; the government reimburses the 5th to 12th week, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000 per child order.

For the third and subsequent child, the government reimburses all 12 weeks, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $30,000 per child order.

Option to share up to 4 weeks of Adoption Leave (AL) with the adoptive father under the Government-Paid Shared Parental Leave scheme.

title: Adoption Leave for Mothers (AL)
tagline: Provides up to 12 weeks of leave for eligible adoptive mothers to care for their adopted child.
text:
You are not the natural mother of the child

Adopting a child in accordance with any Singapore law relating to the adopting of children.

Child is below 12 months old on your Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date

FIA date on or after 1 July 2017

You are employed or self-employed for at least 3 months before FIA date

If child is not a Singapore citizen on adoption application date, he/she must have a Dependent's Pass (DP) and either parent must have been a Singapore citizen since the issue date

title: Adoption Leave for Mothers (AL)
tagline: Provides up to 12 weeks of leave for eligible adoptive mothers to care for their adopted child.
text:
For employees:

Notify your employer about your leave arrangement early.

Submit declaration form (AL1) to your employer with necessary documents at least one week before starting your Adoption Leave (AL), or within one week after starting your AL.

Apply for AL as per your employer's leave application procedures.

For self-employed:

Record your AL dates

Submit your claim online via the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal after starting the government-paid portion of your AL

Claims should be submitted within 3 months after the last day of your AL

For employers:

Obtain and verify the declaration form (AL1) and supporting documents from your employee

Keep the declaration form or records for 5 years from the last AL date

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal after your employee has taken the government-paid portion of AL

Claims should be submitted within 3 months after the last date of the employee’s AL

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text: GOAL+ is a sponsorship scheme that aims to help caregivers save and provide for their dependant with disability. SG Enable Case Managers will invite eligible caregivers to apply for GOAL+ during the trust set-up process.

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text:
How does GOAL+ work?

GOAL+ runs from 1 April 2026 to 31 March 2031. Eligible caregivers whose PWDs do not have a Special Needs Trust (SNT) account can receive $5,000 from Community Chest to set up one.

Eligible caregivers of PWDs with verified disability can qualify for matching top-ups of up to $10,000 from the Government.

GOAL+ allows for a five-year top-up window for eligible SNT accounts, so that caregivers can plan contributions via cash top-up to the trust account in a more sustainable manner. Applicants are strongly encouraged to top up the trust account as soon as practicable. Any contribution made beyond the five years will not be entitled to matching top-ups from the Government.

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text:
To qualify for GOAL+, PWD must meet the following criteria: 

A Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident 

The family has a per capita household income (PCHI) of ≤ $3,600 (P64), or annual value (AV) of property ≤ $21,000 for households with a PCHI of $0. 

Has a verified disability

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text:
Before applying for GOAL+, all applicants are encouraged to:

Check if the PWD’s disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere with your Singpass. If PWD is above 21 years old and does not have Singpass, you may check his/her disability status by calling the Operator (Bizlink) at 64366635 or email to msfdisability@bizlink.org.sg.

If PWD does not have a verified disability status, he/she must get a Disability Verification Form (DVF) completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional*.

*Please note that a submitted DVF does not mean that PWD’s disability status has been verified.

 

Please contact SG Enable for an appointment with a Case Manager to:

(i)     Find out how the Special Needs Trust works

(ii)    Assess eligibility for GOAL+ sponsorship

title: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+)
tagline: Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.
text:
Gift Of A Lifetime Plus (GOAL+) aims to provide eligible caregivers of person with disability (PWDs) with an initial capital of $5,000 to set up trust accounts with Special Needs Trust Company Pte Ltd (SNTC). Under GOAL+, the Government sponsors matching donations of up to $10,000 for low- and middle-income families who contribute to the trust account within a five-year window period.

GOAL+ is a sponsorship scheme that aims to help caregivers save and provide for their dependant with disability. SG Enable Case Managers will invite eligible caregivers to apply for GOAL+ during the trust set-up process.

How does GOAL+ work?

GOAL+ runs from 1 April 2026 to 31 March 2031. Eligible caregivers whose PWDs do not have a Special Needs Trust (SNT) account can receive $5,000 from Community Chest to set up one.

Eligible caregivers of PWDs with verified disability can qualify for matching top-ups of up to $10,000 from the Government.

GOAL+ allows for a five-year top-up window for eligible SNT accounts, so that caregivers can plan contributions via cash top-up to the trust account in a more sustainable manner. Applicants are strongly encouraged to top up the trust account as soon as practicable. Any contribution made beyond the five years will not be entitled to matching top-ups from the Government.

To qualify for GOAL+, PWD must meet the following criteria: 

A Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident 

The family has a per capita household income (PCHI) of ≤ $3,600 (P64), or annual value (AV) of property ≤ $21,000 for households with a PCHI of $0. 

Has a verified disability

Before applying for GOAL+, all applicants are encouraged to:

Check if the PWD’s disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere with your Singpass. If PWD is above 21 years old and does not have Singpass, you may check his/her disability status by calling the Operator (Bizlink) at 64366635 or email to msfdisability@bizlink.org.sg.

If PWD does not have a verified disability status, he/she must get a Disability Verification Form (DVF) completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional*.

*Please note that a submitted DVF does not mean that PWD’s disability status has been verified.

 

Please contact SG Enable for an appointment with a Case Manager to:

(i)     Find out how the Special Needs Trust works

(ii)    Assess eligibility for GOAL+ sponsorship

title: CAREconnect
tagline: CAREconnect offers inclusive social events for family bonding and connection to fellow caregivers and volunteers, through mobilising support groups, organising inclusive, social, and/or respite events, and webinars.
text: CAREconnect offers inclusive social events for family bonding and connection to fellow caregivers and volunteers, through mobilising support groups, organising inclusive, social, and/or respite events, and webinars.

title: CAREconnect
tagline: CAREconnect offers inclusive social events for family bonding and connection to fellow caregivers and volunteers, through mobilising support groups, organising inclusive, social, and/or respite events, and webinars.
text:
CAREconnect goes beyond just offering services. It’s a community movement because it aims to build a network of support for caregivers within their local neighborhoods.
CAREconnect conducts inclusive events for caregivers. 92% of caregivers agreed that the events helped them to feel more included in their neighbourhood.
The monthly webinars focus on a variety of topics and resources that would help caregivers make informed decisions and provide some answers to questions and difficulties they may have. 82% of caregivers at CAREconnect Webinars agreed that the sessions were helpful in learning to care for their child better.

title: CAREconnect
tagline: CAREconnect offers inclusive social events for family bonding and connection to fellow caregivers and volunteers, through mobilising support groups, organising inclusive, social, and/or respite events, and webinars.
text:
CAREconnect offers a variety of events to cater to different needs and preferences:

Informative Webinars: CAREconnect offers online webinars that provide valuable caregiving resources and information, accessible from the comfort of home. 

Engaging Physical Events: CAREconnect organizes face-to-face events, in collaboration with volunteers and grassroots organizations, offering caregivers the opportunity to connect, share experiences, and receive emotional support.

By offering both online and in-person events, along with strong community ties through volunteers and grassroots organizations, CAREconnect builds an inclusive and accessible support system for caregivers.

Beyond just offering services, CAREconnect is a community movement focused on creating a support network for caregivers within their local neighborhoods. Partnership with grassroots enhances CAREconnect’s ability to reach a broader audience and address the unique challenges caregivers face.

Up to full waiver of school and miscellaneous fees, depending on household income

Students with Average Gross Household Income of up to $4,000, or Average Per Capita Income of up to $1,000 will also receive:

Secondary students: Free textbooks and school attire, $21 transport credit per month for those who take public transport, and 10 meals per school week

title: Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)
tagline: Subsidy for caregivers to offset the training fees of approved courses to equip themselves with the necessary skills in caring for the physical and emotional needs of their loved ones.
text:
$200 annual subsidy
Contact the service provider at least two weeks before the course starts

title: Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)
tagline: Subsidy for caregivers to offset the training fees of approved courses to equip themselves with the necessary skills in caring for the physical and emotional needs of their loved ones.
text:
Annual subsidy of $200 that lets caregivers attend approved courses to better care for their loved ones

The $200 subsidy is provided for each care recipient every financial year (from April to March the following year)

If multiple caregivers of the same care recipient wish to attend training, the $200 would need to be shared among the family members

From 1 April 2024, eligible beneficiaries will start off with a $400 grant. Each year, there will be a top up to the care recipient’s Caregivers Training Grant by $200, and unused monies can be carried forward to the following year, capped at $400 per year.

title: Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)
tagline: Subsidy for caregivers to offset the training fees of approved courses to equip themselves with the necessary skills in caring for the physical and emotional needs of their loved ones.
text:
Care recipient must be:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Aged 65 and older, OR have a disability assessed by a qualified healthcare practitioner

Caregiver must be:

Person in charge of caring for the care recipient (this can include family members and foreign domestic workers).

title: Caregivers Training Grant (CTG)
tagline: Subsidy for caregivers to offset the training fees of approved courses to equip themselves with the necessary skills in caring for the physical and emotional needs of their loved ones.
text:
Choose a course from the e-calendar

Contact the training provider to find out more about the course

Their details are available in the calendar

Register for the course

Tell the trainer you want to apply for CTG at least two weeks before the course starts

Your trainer will give you the application form to fill out, and will submit the CTG application form for you

You may also download the CTG application form

title: Community Care Apartments
tagline: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.
text: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.

title: Community Care Apartments
tagline: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.
text:
Homes with pre-installed fittings and senior-friendly design features
Access to services like 24 hour emergency response and other optional paid care services
Apply via the HDB Flat Portal

title: Community Care Apartments
tagline: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.
text:
Flexibility to choose a lease ranging from 15 to 35 years (in 5 year increments) as long as it lasts the applicant(s) and their spouse(s) (if any) until at least age 95.

Eligible first-timers may apply for the Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families or Singles) for the flat purchase.

Each unit comes fully furnished and fitted with senior-friendly features including:

Wide and wheelchair-friendly main door with a build-in bench at the side

Wheelchair accessible bathroom fitted with grab bars and slip-resistant flooring

Built-in wardrobe and cabinets

Furnished kitchen (applicances not provided)

Easy-to-slide partitions between rooms for privacy

All residents must subscribe to the Basic Service Package, which includes the help of a community manager who can arrange:

Simple home fixes

Activities at the communal spaces

Basic health checks

24-hour emergency response

Add-on care and support services (at additional costs), e.g. shared caregiving service, medical escort and transport etc.

title: Community Care Apartments
tagline: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.
text:
Applicant must be a Singapore Citizen (SC)

If you are applying with your spouse, or parent(s)/child(ren), at least one of them must be a SC or Permanent Resident (PR)

If your spouse is not an SC or PR, they must be included as a core occupier

All applicant(s) and their spouse(s) (if any) must be at least 65 years old at the time of HFE letter application

Chosen lease must last all applicant(s) and their spouse(s) (if any) until at least age 95, computed as at the time of HFE letter application.

Monthly household income should not exceed $14,000

Other conditions

If you or any persons listed in the application owns or has an interest in any HDB flat or private residential property, you must dispose of the flat within 6 months from the completion of the flat purchase.

You or any listed applicant(s) and their spouse(s) (if any) are not eligible to buy a Community Care Apartment if you had previously enjoyed 2 or more subsidised housing, one of which was a Studio Apartment or a short lease 2-room Flexi flat or Community Care Apartment.

Additional amount payable

Some applicants will have to make an additional payment on top of the flat price e.g. resale levy. Visit HDB InfoWeb for more details

title: Community Care Apartments
tagline: Senior-friendly housing integrated with care services to support seniors who are 65 and above to age independently within the community.
text:
Check your eligibility by applying for an HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter

Apply online via the HDB Flat Portal during sales launch or open booking of flats, and receive ballot results in up to 2 months' time after applications close

From 4 weeks after the release of ballot results, you will be invited to book a flat and sign the Basic Service Package Agreement

From 6 months after booking a flat, you will be invited to sign the Agreement for Lease

When your booked flat is ready, you will be invited to collect the keys

text: Refunds ABSD for eligible seniors who have purchased their second property

title: ABSD Concession for Single Singapore Citizen Seniors
tagline: Provides single Singapore Citizen seniors (aged 55 and above) a refund for the Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) on their second residential property purchase.
text: Fully refunds the ABSD for eligible seniors on their second residential property purchase.

title: ABSD Concession for Single Singapore Citizen Seniors
tagline: Provides single Singapore Citizen seniors (aged 55 and above) a refund for the Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) on their second residential property purchase.
text:
Eligibility for the ABSD refund is subject to the following conditions:

You must be a single Singapore Citizen senior (aged 55 and above) who purchased a second residential property on or after 16 February 2024

You have paid the ABSD on the second property

Your first residential property is solely owned by you (single Singapore Citizen aged 55 and above) / co-owned by you and your immediate family members^ (all are single Singapore Citizens aged 55 and above)

The owners of the first residential property must also be owners of the second

Any additional owners purchasing the second residential property with the owners of each first residential property must also be single Singapore Citizens aged 55 and above who are immediate family members

The buyer(s) can only own up to 1 residential property each when purchasing the second property

All buyers must not have purchased or acquired any other residential property since the purchase of the second residential property

The first residential property must be sold or disposed of within 6 months of either:

the date of purchase of the second property (for completed property)

the date of issue of the Temporary Occupation Permit / Certificate of Statutory Completion whichever is earlier (for incomplete property)

No change in ownership of the second property at the time of the first property's sale

The value^^ of the second property must be less than each of the first properties that were sold

The application for ABSD refund must be made within 6 months after the date of sale of the first property

^ Immediate family members refer to one's parent, child, or sibling. 

^^ The “value” refers to the higher of the property’s purchase price or market value. The second property’s value is calculated as at its date of purchase. The first property is as at its date of sale.

title: ABSD Concession for Single Singapore Citizen Seniors
tagline: Provides single Singapore Citizen seniors (aged 55 and above) a refund for the Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) on their second residential property purchase.
text:
Apply for the ABSD refund online through the e-Stamping Portal with these steps:

Select "Request" then "Apply for Assessment / Appeal"

Select "Assessment (including remission and penalty)"

If the document is stamped, select "Yes" and enter the Document Ref No., then click “Continue” to fill in the rest of the details and submit the form

If the document has not been stamped, please stamp it and pay the ABSD before applying for the ABSD Concession

To stamp the document, select “Stamping” > “Sale & Purchase – Purchase/Acquisition” then select the relevant form (e.g. For purchase of residential property, select the “Sale and Purchase Agreement” or “Acceptance to Option to Purchase” document description and fill in the details)

At the “Supporting Documents” page, under “Additional Information”, please key in “ABSD Concession for Single SC Seniors”

Please also submit both the following supporting documents with your application:

Copy of the exercised Option to Purchase / Sale and Purchase Agreement (if applicable) for the purchase of the second residential property

Copy of the exercised Option to Purchase / Sale and Purchase Agreement (if applicable) for the sale of the first residential properties

For successful applicants, the refund will be made to the party liable for payment of stamp duty.

title: ABSD Concession for Single Singapore Citizen Seniors
tagline: Provides single Singapore Citizen seniors (aged 55 and above) a refund for the Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) on their second residential property purchase.
text:
Provides single Singapore Citizen seniors (aged 55 and above) a refund for the Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) on their second residential property purchase.

Refunds ABSD for eligible seniors who have purchased their second property

Fully refunds the ABSD for eligible seniors on their second residential property purchase.

Eligibility for the ABSD refund is subject to the following conditions:

You must be a single Singapore Citizen senior (aged 55 and above) who purchased a second residential property on or after 16 February 2024

You have paid the ABSD on the second property

Your first residential property is solely owned by you (single Singapore Citizen aged 55 and above) / co-owned by you and your immediate family members^ (all are single Singapore Citizens aged 55 and above)

The owners of the first residential property must also be owners of the second

Any additional owners purchasing the second residential property with the owners of each first residential property must also be single Singapore Citizens aged 55 and above who are immediate family members

The buyer(s) can only own up to 1 residential property each when purchasing the second property

All buyers must not have purchased or acquired any other residential property since the purchase of the second residential property

The first residential property must be sold or disposed of within 6 months of either:

the date of purchase of the second property (for completed property)

the date of issue of the Temporary Occupation Permit / Certificate of Statutory Completion whichever is earlier (for incomplete property)

No change in ownership of the second property at the time of the first property's sale

The value^^ of the second property must be less than each of the first properties that were sold

The application for ABSD refund must be made within 6 months after the date of sale of the first property

^ Immediate family members refer to one's parent, child, or sibling. 

^^ The “value” refers to the higher of the property’s purchase price or market value. The second property’s value is calculated as at its date of purchase. The first property is as at its date of sale.

Apply for the ABSD refund online through the e-Stamping Portal with these steps:

Select "Request" then "Apply for Assessment / Appeal"

Select "Assessment (including remission and penalty)"

If the document is stamped, select "Yes" and enter the Document Ref No., then click “Continue” to fill in the rest of the details and submit the form

If the document has not been stamped, please stamp it and pay the ABSD before applying for the ABSD Concession

To stamp the document, select “Stamping” > “Sale & Purchase – Purchase/Acquisition” then select the relevant form (e.g. For purchase of residential property, select the “Sale and Purchase Agreement” or “Acceptance to Option to Purchase” document description and fill in the details)

At the “Supporting Documents” page, under “Additional Information”, please key in “ABSD Concession for Single SC Seniors”

Please also submit both the following supporting documents with your application:

Copy of the exercised Option to Purchase / Sale and Purchase Agreement (if applicable) for the purchase of the second residential property

Copy of the exercised Option to Purchase / Sale and Purchase Agreement (if applicable) for the sale of the first residential properties

For successful applicants, the refund will be made to the party liable for payment of stamp duty.

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

title: Career Conversion Programme (CCP) for Individuals
tagline: Support mid-career new hires or existing employees seeking to transition into growth job roles with good longer-term prospects.
text:
Mid-career individuals should meet the following criteria: 

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Aged 21 or above

Graduated or completed National Service, for at least 2 years

Career conversion is required:

New Hire: New job role trained for under the CCP should be substantially different from previous job role before joining CCP

Existing Employee: New job role should be growth job roles identified under the Jobs Transformation Maps (JTMs)

Able to commit to full-time employment or attachment in a participating company

Beyond the general eligibility criteria, different CCPs may have additional course-specific entry requirements, and employers may apply their own -specific shortlisting process. In general, individuals applying for CCPs should also not be shareholders, immediate ex-staff, or related to owners/decision-makers of the CCP participating company or its related entities.

title: Child Development Account
tagline: Special savings account for the child, which can be used for approved areas of child-raising expenditure, including healthcare and preschool fees.
text:
The Child Development Account (CDA) is a special savings account for the child, which can be used for defray child-raising costs. The First Step Grant is automatically deposited after the CDA is opened, and subsequent savings will be co-matched by the Government, up to a cap.

Eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023 will qualify for the following enhanced CDA benefits announced at Budget 2023.

Child Development Account First Step Grant:

$5,000 automatically credited into the CDA upon account opening.

Government co-matching of CDA savings:

Eligible Singaporean children are entitled to Government co-matching up to a cap of $4,000 for the first child, $7,000 for the second child, $9,000 for the third and fourth child, and $15,000 for the fifth child and beyond.

title: Child Development Account

Child is a Singapore Citizen

Children born on or after 14 Feb 2023

Parents are lawfully married

Children of unwed parents may be eligible for CDA benefits if they are born on or after 1 September 2016.

title: ComCare Assistance
tagline: Provides financial support for individuals and families facing varied adversities, ensuring timely aid for both short-term and long-term needs.
text:
ComCare Interim Assistance   

Provides urgent and temporary financial assistance for lower-income individuals or families.   

ComCare Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance (SMTA) 
Provides temporary financial assistance for lower-income individuals or families who are temporarily unable to work, seeking a job or earning a low income. 

ComCare Long Term Assistance (LTA) 
Provides long-term assistance to individuals or families who are permanently unable to work  due to age, illness, or disability with inadequate family support or savings.

title: Special Needs Trust
tagline:
Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) is a not-for-profit trust company and a registered charity. As a fully owned subsidiary of SG Enable, SNTC provides affordable trust services to safeguard monies set aside by caregivers to fund the long-term care needs of their beneficiaries who are persons with disability.

The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).
text:
Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) is a not-for-profit trust company and a registered charity. The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).

title: Special Needs Trust
tagline:
Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) is a not-for-profit trust company and a registered charity. As a fully owned subsidiary of SG Enable, SNTC provides affordable trust services to safeguard monies set aside by caregivers to fund the long-term care needs of their beneficiaries who are persons with disability.

The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).
text: Sponsorship schemes eg GOAL+ are available to enable lower-income families set up the Special Needs Trust with $5,000.

title: Special Needs Trust
tagline:
Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) is a not-for-profit trust company and a registered charity. As a fully owned subsidiary of SG Enable, SNTC provides affordable trust services to safeguard monies set aside by caregivers to fund the long-term care needs of their beneficiaries who are persons with disability.

The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).
text:
After the Special Needs Trust (SNT) is set up, SNTC works with caregivers to put in place proper plans and arrangements that will enhance the financial well-being of PWDs (Beneficiary).

The SNT pathway is as per the following:

Caregiver opens an SNT account with an initial deposit of 5,000

Beneficiary receives gifts under the caregiver’s will, CPF cash and insurance nominations

Disburse funds to the beneficiary upon the caregiver’s passing or incapacity according to latter’s wishes set out in the Letter of Intent.

The trust fund is managed by the Public Trustee and principal value is guaranteed by the Government. SNTC fees are 90-100% subsidised by the Ministry of Social and Family Development.

Reviews are conducted periodically to check on the beneficiary's well-being.

Case Managers will advise caregiver on the future care needs of the beneficiary through a personalised care plan.

title: Special Needs Trust
tagline:
Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) is a not-for-profit trust company and a registered charity. As a fully owned subsidiary of SG Enable, SNTC provides affordable trust services to safeguard monies set aside by caregivers to fund the long-term care needs of their beneficiaries who are persons with disability.

The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).
text:
Caregivers (Settlor) setting up the SNT should be:

21 years and above

Has mental capacity

Not an undischarged bankrupt

 

Persons with Disability (Beneficiary) must be:

A Singaporean or Permanent Resident

Residing in Singapore

Has a verified disability

text:

The Special Needs Trust (SNT) is a legal arrangement for SNTC to hold monies for persons with disability (PWD).

Sponsorship schemes eg GOAL+ are available to enable lower-income families set up the Special Needs Trust with $5,000.

After the Special Needs Trust (SNT) is set up, SNTC works with caregivers to put in place proper plans and arrangements that will enhance the financial well-being of PWDs (Beneficiary).

The SNT pathway is as per the following:

Caregiver opens an SNT account with an initial deposit of 5,000

Beneficiary receives gifts under the caregiver’s will, CPF cash and insurance nominations

Disburse funds to the beneficiary upon the caregiver’s passing or incapacity according to latter’s wishes set out in the Letter of Intent.

The trust fund is managed by the Public Trustee and principal value is guaranteed by the Government. SNTC fees are 90-100% subsidised by the Ministry of Social and Family Development.

Reviews are conducted periodically to check on the beneficiary's well-being.

Case Managers will advise caregiver on the future care needs of the beneficiary through a personalised care plan.

Caregivers (Settlor) setting up the SNT should be:

21 years and above

Has mental capacity

Not an undischarged bankrupt

 

Persons with Disability (Beneficiary) must be:

A Singaporean or Permanent Resident

Residing in Singapore

Has a verified disability

title: Company-Led Training (CLT) Programme
tagline: Deepen your tech and media skills through on-the-job training in emerging tech and media areas such as artificial intelligence, cyber security, data analytics and virtual production.
text:
Participating organisations must:

Be a Singapore-registered company.

Employ professionals in tech and media job roles that are in demand by the industry.

Have the capability and capacity to deliver training programmes, with a good track record in human resource development.

Commit resources including practical projects and qualified mentors to support trainees’ learning.

Provide structured career progression pathways based on skills and competencies.

Ensure that Singapore Permanent Residents (SPRs) do not exceed 15% of total trainees who have completed training.

title: ComCare Long Term Assistance (LTA)
tagline: Provides long-term support to those permanently unable to work due to old age, illness or disability, and have inadequate family support or savings to rely on for daily needs.
text:
Watch this ComCare Financial Assistance Application Instructions video which provides applicants with information on the requirements of the ComCare application process and applicants' responsibilities while on assistance.

You can apply at any Social Service Office.

You'll need to provide:

Updated bank account passbook(s) / statement(s) of applicant and all household members, showing clearly the latest transaction details of at least 1 month.

Other supporting documents, if relevant:

Your identity card or birth certificate (of applicant and all household members) 

(Note: NRIC / FIN of main applicants will not be required for online applications as the login will be via Singpass. If applicants / household members had provided these identification documents in earlier ComCare applications, they will not be required again.)

Latest utility bill(s) (e.g. Singapore Power (SP), private electricity retailers)

Latest Medical Certificate (with duration and fitness for work status)

Pay-slips / Employment contract (for employment without CPF contributions)

Latest employment / termination letter (for recent changes in employment status)

Insurance Letter (for lump-sum / regular payouts by private insurer)

Any other supporting documents

title: ComCare Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance (SMTA)
tagline: Provides temporary financial assistance for lower-income individuals or families who are temporarily unable to work, looking for a job or earning a low income and require assistance.
text:
Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident (at least one immediate family member in the same household must be a Singapore Citizen).

Have little or no family support, savings or assets to rely on for your daily needs.

Looking for work or temporarily unable to work due to illness or have to care for children, elderly or other dependants.

Monthly household income per capita of $800 and below (each applicant’s specific financial circumstances will be assessed).

Even if your income exceeds these guidelines, you can still approach a Social Service Office (SSO) for assessment if you face financial difficulties. The SSOs will assess each household’s needs, such as employment, family and finances, to ensure that the assistance we provide is holistic.

title: ComCare Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance (SMTA)
tagline: Provides temporary financial assistance for lower-income individuals or families who are temporarily unable to work, looking for a job or earning a low income and require assistance.
text:
Watch this ComCare Financial Assistance Application Instructions video which provides applicants with information on the requirements of the ComCare application process and applicants' responsibilities while on assistance.

To apply:

Online via SupportGoWhere

Alternatively, visit your nearest Social Service Office to apply in person.

If you need help with your application, please refer to this step-by-step video guide.

Each SSO administers ComCare financial assistance and helps applicants to access other forms of assistance they may need, such as employment assistance or family services.

You'll need to provide:

Updated bank account passbook(s) / statement(s) of applicant and all household members, showing clearly the latest transaction details of at least 1 month

Other supporting documents, if relevant:

Your identity card or birth certificate (of applicant and all household members)
(Note: NRIC / FIN of main applicants will not be required for online applications as the login will be via Singpass. If applicants / household members had provided these identification documents in earlier ComCare applications, they will not be required again.)

Latest utility bill(s) (e.g. Singapore Power (SP), private electricity retailers)

Latest Medical Certificate (with duration and fitness for work status)

Pay-slips / Employment contract (for employment without CPF contributions)

Latest employment / termination letter (for recent changes in employment status)

Insurance Letter (for lump-sum / regular payouts by private insurer)

Any other supporting documents

The collection, use or disclosure of personal data of relevant individuals was applied in the development of policies and services for lower-income households. In addition to the Personal Data Protection Act, there exists strict policies, processes and safeguards in place to ensure the proper use and protection of personal data by the Government.

title: CPF Housing Grant for Executive Condominiums (ECs)
tagline: Provides up to $30,000 subsidy for eligible first-timer applicants buying an Executive Condominium (EC).
text:
Housing subsidy of up to $30,000 for eligible first-timer recipients purchasing an EC from a property developer

The Grant can be used to offset the EC’s purchase price or reduce the required housing loan for it

title: CPF Housing Grant for Executive Condominiums (ECs)
tagline: Provides up to $30,000 subsidy for eligible first-timer applicants buying an Executive Condominium (EC).
text:
Your core family nucleus must include at least 1 first-timer applicant who has not received any housing subsidies before

Applicants must be Singapore Citizens at least 21 years old

The household must include at least 1 other Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

All applicants and occupiers must have no ownership or interest in private property locally or overseas in the last 30 months

All persons listed in the application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property

Grant amount eligibility will be determined by your household’s average monthly household income (includes all applicants and occupiers listed in the EC application)

All members of the core family nucleus will receive equal shares of the grant, fully credited into their CPF Ordinary Accounts

Only core applicants can use their grants to pay for the EC unit purchase

title: Car Park Label Scheme
tagline: Provides two types of labels that allow drivers (Class 1) and passengers (Class 2) with medically certified mobility challenges to park at accessible spaces, so they can board or alight from their vehicles safely.
text:
Car park labels allow access to accessible lots for easier boarding and alighting for drivers and passengers with mobility impairment

Each label can be used by the label holder only. Car park labels are not applicable to motorcycles

Option for Social Service Agencies (SSAs) to obtain labels for vehicles providing dedicated transport to passengers with mobility impairment

title: Car Park Label Scheme
tagline: Provides two types of labels that allow drivers (Class 1) and passengers (Class 2) with medically certified mobility challenges to park at accessible spaces, so they can board or alight from their vehicles safely.
text:
For Class 1: Drivers who are certified by Singapore registered medical professionals to require the use of bulky mobility aids and need to open the vehicle doors fully to board or alight from the vehicle.

For Class 2: Passengers who are certified by a Singapore registered medical professionals as to require the use of bulky mobility aids and need to open the vehicle doors fully to board or alight from the vehicle.

For Class 2 SSA: Social Service Agencies registered as NCSS Members providing dedicated transport to passengers with mobility impairment.

title: Car Park Label Scheme
tagline: Provides two types of labels that allow drivers (Class 1) and passengers (Class 2) with medically certified mobility challenges to park at accessible spaces, so they can board or alight from their vehicles safely.
text:
Provides two types of labels that allow drivers (Class 1) and passengers (Class 2) with medically certified mobility challenges to park at accessible spaces, so they can board or alight from their vehicles safely.

Eligible drivers and passengers with mobility impairment can apply for a Car Park Label to use accessible lots

Car park labels allow access to accessible lots for easier boarding and alighting for drivers and passengers with mobility impairment

Each label can be used by the label holder only. Car park labels are not applicable to motorcycles

Option for Social Service Agencies (SSAs) to obtain labels for vehicles providing dedicated transport to passengers with mobility impairment

For Class 1: Drivers who are certified by Singapore registered medical professionals to require the use of bulky mobility aids and need to open the vehicle doors fully to board or alight from the vehicle.

For Class 2: Passengers who are certified by a Singapore registered medical professionals as to require the use of bulky mobility aids and need to open the vehicle doors fully to board or alight from the vehicle.

For Class 2 SSA: Social Service Agencies registered as NCSS Members providing dedicated transport to passengers with mobility impairment.

For more information on the application process, please visit Enabling Guide.

SG Enable Hotline: 1800-8585-885

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

contactus@sgenable.sg

Enabling Village 
20 Lengkok Bahru
#01-01, Singapore 159053

title: Life Insurance Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.
text: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.

title: Life Insurance Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.
text: Life Insurance Relief up to $5,000

title: Life Insurance Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.
text:
Tax relief for your life insurance premiums

If you are a married man, you may also claim the life insurance premium paid for your wife

The amount of relief is capped between the lower of:

Difference between $5,000 and your CPF contribution; or

Up to 7% of the insured value of your own / your wife's life, or the amount of insurance premiums paid

title: Life Insurance Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.
text:
To claim Life Insurance Relief, you must meet all the following conditions:

You have paid, and are claiming, for your own life insurance policy

Married men may also claim for premiums paid on their wife’s life insurance policy

The insurance company has an office or branch in Singapore

The Singapore office of the insurance company should perform similar activities as the overseas company where the life insurance policy was purchased

Your / your wife’s total CPF contributions for the following were less than $5,000 in the previous year:

Compulsory employee's CPF contribution

Compulsory MediSave or voluntary CPF contribution for self-employed individuals

title: Life Insurance Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,000 for individuals who paid annual insurance premiums on their own life insurance policies.
text:
If you were previously approved for this relief last year (and your circumstances have not changed), the amount will be pre-filled and automatically granted. Please refer to the process for first-time applicants below to update your claim if there are changes.

For e-Filing:

	

For paper filing:

If this is your first time claiming this relief:

Log in with your Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Life Insurance”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Update”

	

For Form B1 & B, fill in the “Life Insurance Relief” section

If claiming for the first time, provide details of the premiums paid in Appendix 2
