tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.
text: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.

title: Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.
text: Claim $3,000 for Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR)

title: Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.
text:
Eligible working mother can claim relief of $3,000

Caregiver may be the subject of other relief claims e.g. Parent Relief, Spouse Relief

title: Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.
text:
To be eligible for GCR, you must meet all the following conditions:

Working mother who is married, divorced or widowed

Caregiver resides and lives in Singapore^

Caregiver is a parent / grandparent / parent-in-law / grandparent-in-law (in-laws include that of ex-spouses')

As of last year, caregiver has been looking after a Singapore Citizen child aged 12 and below / an unmarried child with disabilities

Caregiver's annual income did not exceed $8,000 from any trade, business, profession, vocation and / or employment in the previous year 

No one else has claimed GCR on the same caregiver. Relief is limited to 1 claim for 1 parent / grandparent i.e. a single claim of $3,000

^ Dependant has been staying in Singapore on a permanent basis. Foreign dependants must have stayed in Singapore for at least 8 months in the previous year.

title: Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law as caregivers for their children.
text:
For e-Filing:

If claiming the relief for the first time:

Log in with Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Grandparent Caregiver”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Update”

If you have previously claimed GCR, the amount will be pre-filled and granted to you automatically. You may refer to the above steps if you would like to make changes to your relief

For paper filing:

For Form B1 & B, fill in the “Grandparent Caregiver Relief” section.

Include your dependant's details in Appendix 2 as well, if it’s your first time claiming GCR

title: Integrated Shield Plan (IP)
tagline: A private medical insurance plan, offered by private insurers, that provides optional additional coverage on top of what you are already covered for under MediShield Life.
text:
IPs offers optional additional coverage on top of MediShield Life, targeted at covering unsubsidised care in public hospitals or private hospital bills
The additional private insurance component of your Integrated Shield Plan premium is payable by MediSave, up to the Additional Withdrawal Limits

title: Integrated Shield Plan (IP)
tagline: A private medical insurance plan, offered by private insurers, that provides optional additional coverage on top of what you are already covered for under MediShield Life.
text:
MediShield Life component:

Fully payable by MediSave

Sized for subsidised care (e.g B2 / C wards) in public hospital

Additional private insurance component:

Payable by MediSave, but only up to the Additional Withdrawal Limits

Covers unsubsidised care (e.g. A, B1 wards) in public hospitals or private hospital bills

IPs offers optional additional coverage on top of MediShield Life, targeted at covering unsubsidised care in public hospitals or private hospital bills
The additional private insurance component of your Integrated Shield Plan premium is payable by MediSave, up to the Additional Withdrawal Limits

MediShield Life component:

Fully payable by MediSave

Sized for subsidised care (e.g B2 / C wards) in public hospital

Additional private insurance component:

Payable by MediSave, but only up to the Additional Withdrawal Limits

Covers unsubsidised care (e.g. A, B1 wards) in public hospitals or private hospital bills

Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents

There are a variety of IPs with different levels of coverage

When deciding whether to buy an IP, you should consider what you would like to be covered for and whether you can afford the plan, especially in the long term

You may refer to MOH, Comparison of Integrated Shield Plans or Health Insurance Planner for a comparison of IP benefits and long-term IP premiums across different plans

Healthcare Hotline: 1800-222-3399

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: By appointment only

title: Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP)
tagline: ADAP supports caregivers of children without mental capacity to apply to be court-appointed deputies, through the simplified deputyship process. As a deputy, the caregiver can make legal decisions for the child when he/she turns 21.
text:
Programme provides guidance/facilitation, for caregivers of persons with no mental capacity to manage their own matters in adulthood, to apply to Family Justice Courts to be appointed as legal deputies for their child. This is a simplified process that is also more affordable, compared to application for deputyship under standard route.

Courts may accept mental capacity assessment reports prepared by psychologists from Special Education Schools (SPED Schools), selected Day Activity Centres (DACs), Sheltered Workshops (SWs), or the MSF-appointed ADAP Agency in lieu of a medical report. This applies only when the child is enrolled in a SPED school, DAC, or SW that is onboard the ADAP programme. In all other cases, the mental capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical doctor or psychiatrist.

title: Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP)
tagline: ADAP supports caregivers of children without mental capacity to apply to be court-appointed deputies, through the simplified deputyship process. As a deputy, the caregiver can make legal decisions for the child when he/she turns 21.
text:
Your child is:

between 18 and 21 years old as well as in the graduating cohort in a Special Education (SPED) school; or

above 18 years old and enrolled in an MSF-funded Day Activity Centre (DAC) or Sheltered Workshop (SW).

You agree to use the standard list of powers/orders available under the Integrated Family Application Management System (iFAMS) when filing a simplified Deputyship application.

All family members and any other caregivers agree for you to make the application (i.e. it is an uncontested case).

title: Assisted Deputyship Application Programme (ADAP)
tagline: ADAP supports caregivers of children without mental capacity to apply to be court-appointed deputies, through the simplified deputyship process. As a deputy, the caregiver can make legal decisions for the child when he/she turns 21.
text:
Caregivers can approach the relevant staff in the SPED school, Day Activity Centre or Sheltered Workshop to indicate interest to apply for Deputyship. The school or centre will work with an MSF-appointed ADAP Agency to guide the parent/caregiver in their Deputyship application.

For Caregivers whose child with disability is not enrolled in a service, please contact ADAP Agency directly via phone or email for enquiries on deputyship application.

ADAP supports caregivers of children without mental capacity to apply to be court-appointed deputies, through the simplified deputyship process. As a deputy, the caregiver can make legal decisions for the child when he/she turns 21.

Guidance provided for simplified deputyship applications for caregivers of persons with disabilities and without mental capacity
Acceptance of mental capacity assessment reports from SPED Schools, DACs, SWs, or the MSF-appointed ADAP Agency

Programme provides guidance/facilitation, for caregivers of persons with no mental capacity to manage their own matters in adulthood, to apply to Family Justice Courts to be appointed as legal deputies for their child. This is a simplified process that is also more affordable, compared to application for deputyship under standard route.

Courts may accept mental capacity assessment reports prepared by psychologists from Special Education Schools (SPED Schools), selected Day Activity Centres (DACs), Sheltered Workshops (SWs), or the MSF-appointed ADAP Agency in lieu of a medical report. This applies only when the child is enrolled in a SPED school, DAC, or SW that is onboard the ADAP programme. In all other cases, the mental capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical capacity assessment must be conducted by a medical doctor or psychiatrist.

Your child is:

between 18 and 21 years old as well as in the graduating cohort in a Special Education (SPED) school; or

above 18 years old and enrolled in an MSF-funded Day Activity Centre (DAC) or Sheltered Workshop (SW).

You agree to use the standard list of powers/orders available under the Integrated Family Application Management System (iFAMS) when filing a simplified Deputyship application.

All family members and any other caregivers agree for you to make the application (i.e. it is an uncontested case).

text:
Subsidised fibre broadband, with monthly payment over 36 months

Subsidised laptop or tablet, with one-time payment

title: DigitalAccess@Home
tagline: Provides subsidised broadband and laptops/tablets to lower-income households to support their lifestyle needs, including work and social activities.
text:
To qualify for DigitalAccess@Home, you must meet all of the following criteria:

Staying in a HDB flat

At least one member of your household is a Singapore Citizen

With a monthly household income (MHI) or per capita income (PCI) meeting either of the following conditions:

	

Monthly household income (MHI)

	

Monthly income per capita (PCI)

No primary school student or person with disabilities in your household

	

≤ $1,900

	

≤ $650

If there is a primary school student or person with disabilities in your household

	

≤ $3,400

	

≤ $900

You are encouraged to check your eligibility with the online self-assessment tool before applying.

title: DigitalAccess@Home
tagline: Provides subsidised broadband and laptops/tablets to lower-income households to support their lifestyle needs, including work and social activities.
text:
Only one application form per household is needed.

Online submission

Step 1: Visit IMDA's Digital Access Programme site. Click on ‘For Applicant’ at the top menu bar

Step 2: Log in with Singpass. The form will be prefilled with your information from our records. Complete the form and submit.

Step 3: Household members have to give their consent for data collection via Singpass upon receipt of letter/SMS/email

Hardcopy submission

Step 1: Download the hardcopy or obtain a copy from your nearest SG Digital community hub

Step 2: Complete the form. Household members have to give their consent for data collection

Step 3: Post completed form to: Info-communications Media Development Authority, 10 Pasir Panjang Road #03-01, Mapletree Business City, Singapore 117438

You will receive an update on your application via SMS/email in 10 working days. Alternatively, you can check your application status by logging into www.digitalaccess.gov.sg with your SingPass.

4% annual interest
Grant can cover MediShield Life premiums, vaccinations, hospitalisation and outpatient treatments

title: MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN)
tagline: All Singapore Citizen newborns are eligible for the MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN) of up to $5,000.
text:
Up to $5,000 credited to each Singapore Citizen newborn's CPF MediSave account

Accounts are opened automatically for newborns

Covers MediShield Life premiums, recommended childhood vaccinations, hospitalisation, and approved outpatient treatments

title: MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN)
tagline: All Singapore Citizen newborns are eligible for the MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN) of up to $5,000.
text:
All Singapore Citizen newborns born on or after 26 August 2012 are eligible for MGN

Children who are Singapore Citizens at birth and born before January 1, 2015, are entitled to MGN of up to $3,000

For those born on or after January 1, 2015, they are eligible for MGN of $4,000

For those born on or after April 1, 2025, they are eligible for MGN of $5,000

Children who are born overseas can qualify for MGN upon registering their birth with Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) or through a Singapore Overseas Mission

The MGN amount will depend on the date that the child becomes a Singapore Citizen

Children who obtain Singapore Citizenship after the age of 2 are not eligible for MGN.

title: MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN)
tagline: All Singapore Citizen newborns are eligible for the MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN) of up to $5,000.
text:
No application required

The grant is automatically credited into your newborn’s MediSave account within 1 week from birth registration / acquiring Singapore citizenship

Once the MGN has been credited, you will receive a notification on the MGN amount

You may also check your child’s CPF statement online by logging into your CPF with your Singpass.

Premium subsidies of up to 60%
Pioneer and Merdeka Generation subsidies

title: MediShield Life Premium Subsidies
tagline: To keep MediShield Life premiums affordable, the Government provides several premium support measures, such as Premium Subsidies, Pioneer Generation Subsidies and Merdeka Generation Subsidies.
text:
Eligibility criteria for Premium Subsidies:

Premium Subsidies of up to 60% for eligible lower-to middle-income households

Pioneer Generation Subsidies of between 40-60%, depending on age

Merdeka Generation Subsidies of between 5%-10%, depending on age

As of 1 Apr 2025,

Household Monthly Income Per Person

	

Subsidy Range (tiered by age)

$0 - $1,500

	

25% - 60%

> $1,501 - $2,600

	

20% - 55%

> $2,601 - $3,600

	

15% - 40%

Note: The above subsidy rates apply to those living in property with an AV of $21,000 or less. Properties with an AV between $21,001 and $31,000 receive 10% less subsidies. Permanent Residents (PRs) receive half the subsidy rate applicable to Singapore Citizens (SCs). Those who live in residences with an AV above $31,000 or own multiple properties will not receive these subsidies.

Eligibility for Pioneer Generation Subsidies:

Applicable for Pioneers, regardless of their household monthly income or the AV of their residence

Eligibility for Merdeka Generation Subsidies:

Applicable for Merdeka Generation seniors, regardless of their household monthly income or the AV of their residence, on top of the Premium Subsidies that the seniors may be eligible for

Please click here for more information on Premium Subsidies, Pioneer Generation Subsidies and Merdeka Generation Subsidies.

Living in property with an Annual Value (AV) of $31,000 and below; and

Not owning more than 1 property

title: Dependant's Protection Scheme (DPS)
tagline: Term life insurance scheme, solely administered by Great Eastern Life that provides basic financial protection for you and your family in the event of death, terminal illness or total permanent disability.
text:
Provides cash payout to get through the first few years should the insured member meet an untimely death, suffer from terminal illness or total permanent disability
Processing of claim application generally takes 7 working days upon the Great Eastern Life's receipt of requested documents

title: Dependant's Protection Scheme (DPS)
tagline: Term life insurance scheme, solely administered by Great Eastern Life that provides basic financial protection for you and your family in the event of death, terminal illness or total permanent disability.
text:
Insured CPF Members up to age 60

Maximum sum assured of up to $70,000

Insured CPF Members above age 60 and up to age 65

Maximum sum assured of up to $55,000

title: Dependant's Protection Scheme (DPS)
tagline: Term life insurance scheme, solely administered by Great Eastern Life that provides basic financial protection for you and your family in the event of death, terminal illness or total permanent disability.
text:
Automatic inclusion of DPS coverage for Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident between age 21 and 65 upon a valid CPF working contribution.

DPS claim payout is made to:

Next-of-kin/DPS nominee of policyholder who is deceased, or

DPS policyholder who is certified by a registered medical practitioner under the Medical Registration Act to be suffering from:

Terminal illness: an illness that is likely to result in the death of the insured CPF member within 12 months

Total permanent disability: the inability to take part in any employment permanently, or the total permanent loss of physical functions of any of the following:

Both eyes, or

Two limbs, or

One eye and one limb

title: Dependant's Protection Scheme (DPS)
tagline: Term life insurance scheme, solely administered by Great Eastern Life that provides basic financial protection for you and your family in the event of death, terminal illness or total permanent disability.
text:
For CPF members between age 16 and 65 and have not been automatically covered by DPS, you can apply to join DPS with Great Eastern Life directly.

Claim on death grounds

Next-of-kins/DPS nominee of policyholder can contact Great Eastern Life directly for details of claim application forms and supporting documents required.

Claim on medical grounds

DPS and either Home Protection Scheme (HPS) or withdraw CPF due to reduced life expectancy, or both: 

Submit your application to the CPF Board directly via the CPF website. The claim application for DPS will be forwarded to Great Eastern Life for their concurrent assessment. 

DPS only:

Contact Great Eastern Life directly for details of claim application forms and supporting documents required.

Terminal illness: an illness that is likely to result in the death of the insured CPF member within 12 months

Total permanent disability: the inability to take part in any employment permanently, or the total permanent loss of physical functions of any of the following:

Both eyes, or

Two limbs, or

One eye and one limb

For CPF members between age 16 and 65 and have not been automatically covered by DPS, you can apply to join DPS with Great Eastern Life directly.

Claim on death grounds

Next-of-kins/DPS nominee of policyholder can contact Great Eastern Life directly for details of claim application forms and supporting documents required.

Claim on medical grounds

DPS and either Home Protection Scheme (HPS) or withdraw CPF due to reduced life expectancy, or both: 

Submit your application to the CPF Board directly via the CPF website. The claim application for DPS will be forwarded to Great Eastern Life for their concurrent assessment. 

DPS only:

Contact Great Eastern Life directly for details of claim application forms and supporting documents required.

CPF Hotline: 1800-227-1188

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Write to us

Visit your nearest branch

Singapore Citizen

Persons with permanent physical disabilities who are medically certified by doctors from the Tan Tock Seng Hospital, Clinic for Advanced Rehabilitation Therapeutics (CART) as permanently disabled and incapable of taking public transport (i.e. bus and/or MRT), but fit to drive

title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
tagline: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
text: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.

title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
tagline: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
text:
Eligible employees, platform workers and self-employed persons can receive cash and CPF payments to supplement their income and grow their CPF savings for retirement, housing and healthcare
Employees can receive up to $4,900 per year
Self-employed persons and Platform Workers can receive up to $3,267 per year

title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
tagline: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
text:
Employees

From Work Year 2025, eligible recipients will receive monthly payments of up to $4,900 per year, comprising 40% in cash and 60% credited to CPF Accounts

Self-employed persons and Platform Workers

From Work Year 2025, eligible recipients will receive annual payments of up to $3,267, comprising 10% cash and 90% credited to MediSave Account

Self-employed persons will receive payments annually, while Platform Workers will receive payments monthly

From Work Year 2029, eligible Platform Workers whose CPF contribution rates fully align with that of employees will receive higher WIS payments at the same level as employees

title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
tagline: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
text:
Eligibility criteria

30 years old and above as at 31 December of the Work Year

Singapore Citizen

Employee, self-employed person and/or platform worker

Gross monthly income of $500* to $3,000 (if married, assessable income of recipient's spouse must not exceed $70,000), for the month worked

Average gross monthly income of not more than S$3,000 in the past 12 months as an employee or platform worker

Annual value of the place of residence (as indicated on NRIC) must not exceed $21,000, as at 31 December of the preceding year

Does not own more than 1 property (if married, recipient and spouse must not own more than 1 property)

*Persons with disabilities, workers who qualify for ComCare Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance and caregivers of care recipients (generally include caregivers residing with care recipients who are medically certified to have permanent moderate to severe disabilities) will qualify for concessionary WIS, even if they earn less than $500.

title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
tagline: Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
text:
Employees

No application required

Your WIS eligibility will be automatically assessed based on the income declared by your employer(s) for work done in that month

Self-employed persons

Declare your net trade income to IRAS and make the required MediSave contributions in full

Platform workers

No application required

Your WIS eligibility will be automatically assessed based on the net earnings declared by your platform operator(s) for work done in that month

Up to 95% subsidy of improvement costs
Respond within 14 working days upon receipt of application
Apply via EASE e-Service or Mobile@HDB

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE)
tagline: Subsidy for Singapore Citizen households with at least one senior who meets age criterion and needs help to install senior-friendly fittings.
text:
Depending on your flat type, the subsidy can cover up to 95% of the total improvement cost.

'Calculate Calculate Cost of HIP/ EASE Improvement Items' e-Service

Choose ‘EASE (Direct Application)’ under the selection type, indicate your flat type and the EASE improvements you wish to have.

If you are a senior living in a rental flat, your preferred EASE improvement items will be fully funded by the Government.

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE)
tagline: Subsidy for Singapore Citizen households with at least one senior who meets age criterion and needs help to install senior-friendly fittings.
text:
Singapore Citizen household with a senior who is aged:

65 and above; or

Between 60 and 64, and requires assistance for one or more of the Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Eating

Bathing

Dressing

Transferring

Toileting

Walking or moving around

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE)
tagline: Subsidy for Singapore Citizen households with at least one senior who meets age criterion and needs help to install senior-friendly fittings.
text:
You can apply:

Apply for Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE) Online via EASE

Mobile@HDB

You are required to submit a Functional Assessment Report if you are a Singapore Citizen flat owner with at least one member in the household aged between 60 and 64 who requires assistance for one or more of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL)

The Functional Assessment Report for the elderly aged between 60 and 64 must be endorsed by a qualified assessor such as a Singapore Medical Council fully registered doctor, and must be submitted together with your application form

title: Edusave Account
tagline: Provides $230 to $290 annually for approved fees and programmes for every Singapore Citizen child, aged 7 to 16 or studying in a primary or secondary school. Ad-hoc top-ups may be made by the Government to the child’s Edusave account periodically.
text:
Annual contributions to Edusave accounts for primary school students: $230

Annual contributions to Edusave accounts for secondary school students: $290

Funds can be used for approved school enrichment programs, miscellaneous fees, approved educational activities and personal learning devices / assistive devices.

Edusave account balances earn interest at a rate pegged to the CPF Ordinary Account (CPF-OA), currently at 2.5%

title: Job Placement and Job Support
tagline: Provides persons with disabilities employment assistance through job matching and job coaching for up to 12 months.
text:
SG Enable works with partner agencies funded by the government to provide Job Placement and Job Support (JPJS) services such as APSN, Autism Resource Centre (Singapore), Movement for the Intellectually Disabled of Singapore (MINDS) and SPD. Persons with disabilities who approach SG Enable for employment assistance will be referred to a suitable partner agency, subject to the respective agency’s eligibility criteria.

 

SG Enable and its appointed partners provide specialised job placement and job support services that include the following:

Assessing the jobseeker’s strengths, job interests and skills, and identifying training opportunities

Helping the jobseeker identify job opportunities and link up with prospective inclusive employers

Assessing the nature of the job and its tasks for suitability

Assessing the built environment for accessibility

Working with the employer to job redesign and recommend workplace modification, making use of assistive devices, if needed

On-site job coaching to help the new employee integrate into the workplace

title: Job Placement and Job Support
tagline: Provides persons with disabilities employment assistance through job matching and job coaching for up to 12 months.
text:
Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who are verified to have a permanent disability of any of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least one of the size Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

Moderate Visual Impairment or worse in the better eye

Moderate Hearing Loss or worse in the better ear

Intellectual Disability

Autism 

^You can check if your disability status is verified on SupportGoWhere or contact us at employment@sgenable.sg.

title: Job Placement and Job Support
tagline: Provides persons with disabilities employment assistance through job matching and job coaching for up to 12 months.
text:
Preparation of documents

The following support documents are required before making an application:

Disability Verification Form (DVF) (To be completed and endorsed by registered healthcare professional) 

Copy of NRIC Front and Back

One Referral Form (for email/physical submissions only)

How to apply

Persons with disabilities seeking employment assistance may apply

1)      Online via SupportGoWhere, or

2)      Download the One Referral Form and email us at employment@sgenable.sg, or

3)      Approach SG Enable’s Information and Career Centre, located within the Enabling Village, for assistance.

Jobseekers with disabilities can also apply for vacancies with our inclusive employers in our Disability Employment Portal.

tagline: Provides discretionary monthly cash payout to lower-income Singapore Citizens with severe disability, who are not able to benefit from Careshield Life, ElderShield and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) and have low MediSave balances and inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs.
text: Eligible Singapore Citizens can receive up to $250 cash payout per month for as long as they remain eligible, and meet the criteria for receipt of payment.

title: ElderFund
tagline: Provides discretionary monthly cash payout to lower-income Singapore Citizens with severe disability, who are not able to benefit from Careshield Life, ElderShield and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) and have low MediSave balances and inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs.
text:
The care recipient:

Is a Singapore Citizen aged 30 and above

Lives in Singapore

Has a monthly household income per capita of $1,500 or less

Has MedisSave balance of less than $10,000

Assessed by an MOH-accredited assessor to be unable to perform 3 out of the following 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Washing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Walking or moving around

Transferring

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield policyholder

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield supplement plan policyholder

No longer an active recipient of ElderShield claims (including supplement claims)

Not eligible for IDAPE, or no longer an active recipient of IDAPE

^Applications from Singaporeans who do not meet the guiding financial criteria but have difficulty meeting their long-term care needs will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

title: ElderFund
tagline: Provides discretionary monthly cash payout to lower-income Singapore Citizens with severe disability, who are not able to benefit from Careshield Life, ElderShield and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) and have low MediSave balances and inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs.
text:
Provides discretionary monthly cash payout to lower-income Singapore Citizens with severe disability, who are not able to benefit from Careshield Life, ElderShield and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) and have low MediSave balances and inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs.

Up to $250 cash per month

Eligible Singapore Citizens can receive up to $250 cash payout per month for as long as they remain eligible, and meet the criteria for receipt of payment.

The care recipient:

Is a Singapore Citizen aged 30 and above

Lives in Singapore

Has a monthly household income per capita of $1,500 or less

Has MedisSave balance of less than $10,000

Assessed by an MOH-accredited assessor to be unable to perform 3 out of the following 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Washing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Walking or moving around

Transferring

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield policyholder

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield supplement plan policyholder

No longer an active recipient of ElderShield claims (including supplement claims)

Not eligible for IDAPE, or no longer an active recipient of IDAPE

^Applications from Singaporeans who do not meet the guiding financial criteria but have difficulty meeting their long-term care needs will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Contact an MOH-accredited severe disability assessor for a severe disability assessment. See list of assessors.

Log in to AIC’s eService portal (eFASS) with your Singpass to submit a claim application

AIC hotline: 1800 650 6060

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit the AIC Link branch nearest to you

tagline: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
text: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
text:
Up to $120,000 Enhanced CPF Housing Grant for first-timer families
The Grant can be used to offset the flat purchase price or reduce the required housing loan
Households with lower household incomes will receive larger grants

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
text:
Up to $120,000 for applicants purchasing a new or resale flat as a family

Up to $60,000 for couples comprising a first-time and second-time applicant

The grant amount is based on the average gross monthly household income, with lower-income households receiving more support

The Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (EHG) can be used to:

Offset the purchase price of the flat

Reduce the housing loan required for the flat purchase

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
text:
Applicants forming a core family nucleus, consisting of the core applicant(s) and occupier(s)

Average gross monthly household income of $9,000 or below for first-timer households (including all applicants and occupiers)

For mixed households (first-timer and second-timer), half of the average gross monthly household income must not exceed $4,500

If you are buying a flat with your parents, you and at least 1 parent must be a Singapore Citizen, and both parents must be first-timers and meet all the eligibility conditions

Have worked continuously for at least 12 months, 2 months before the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application, and were employed at the time of the HFE letter application

Flat has a remaining lease of more than 20 years

To qualify for the full grant, flat must have sufficient remaining lease to cover the youngest core applicant and/or core occupier to the age of 95

Must not own, have an interest in or disposed of any private residential property (local or overseas) within the last 30 months

All persons listed in the HFE application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property at HFE letter application and up to 30 months before HFE letter application

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
text:
Obtain a HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal to determine your eligibility for this Grant and others

If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may apply for the grant when submitting your resale application or after you have booked a flat from HDB

The Grant will be credited to the CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible household members

Only core applicants can use their share of the housing grants to pay for the flat purchase

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)

text:
EHG (Singles) Grant of up to $60,000 for eligible first-timer applicants

Households with lower household incomes will receive a larger grant amount

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Singles)
tagline: Provides up to $60,000 in Enhanced CPF Housing Grants (EHG) (Singles) for eligible first-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat on their own, or with their non-resident spouses.
text:
The EHG (Singles) Grant provides up to $60,000 to offset the purchase price of the flat / reduce the required housing loan for the flat (new or resale)

Receive a total Grant of up to $120,000 when jointly purchasing flats with other eligible first-timer single applicants (Limited to 2, up to $60,000 each)

title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Singles)
tagline: Provides up to $60,000 in Enhanced CPF Housing Grants (EHG) (Singles) for eligible first-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat on their own, or with their non-resident spouses.
text:
First-timer applicants aged 35 or above, who are:

buying a HDB flat alone or with other first-timer single citizens

buying a resale flat alone, with other first-timer single citizens or with their parents

First-timer applicants aged 21 or above who are buying a resale flat with their non-resident spouse

If you are buying a flat with your parents, you and at least 1 parent must be a Singapore Citizen, and both parents must be first-timers and meet all the eligibility conditions

Applicants have worked continuously for at least 12 months, 2 months before the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application, and were employed at the time of the HFE letter application

Monthly household income not exceeding $4,500 for singles and $9,000 for joint singles, non-resident spouses or your parents (resale flats only)

Flat has a remaining lease of more than 20 years

To qualify for the full EHG grant, flat must have sufficient lease for the coverage of the youngest core applicant and occupier to the age of 95

Must not own, have an interest in or have disposed of any private residential property (local or overseas) within the last 30 months

All persons listed in the HFE letter application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property at HFE letter application and up to 30 months before HFE letter application

title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
text: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.

title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
text:
Dollar-for-dollar matching grant on cash top-ups to your RA/SA of up to $2,000 per year
Boost CPF retirement savings and monthly payouts 
Earn CPF interest rates of up to 6% per annum on cash top-ups

title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
text:
The Government will match every dollar of cash top-ups made to the RA/SA of eligible Singaporeans of up to $2,000 per year, with a $20,000 cap over an eligible member’s lifetime

The matching grant will automatically be credited to the RA/SA of the eligible member at the beginning of the following year

Eligible Singaporeans will enjoy interest rates of up to 6% per annum on the cash top-up and matching grant, enhancing their retirement payouts

Cash top-ups that attract the MRSS matching grant will not qualify for tax relief

title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
text:
You must meet the following criteria to be eligible:

Singapore Citizens only

Aged 55 and above as of 31st December of the assessment year, Or below 55 years old with disability status verified with the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF)

Your Retirement Account (RA) savings^ or Ordinary and Special Account (OSA) savings are less than $110,200^^

Average monthly income not more than $4,000

Annual Value of Residence not more than $21,000

Owns not more than 1 property

Your eligibility for the scheme is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year, and you do not need to apply

You can check your eligibility via your Retirement Dashboard

RA savings refers to the RA balances (excluding amounts such as interest earned), plus retirement withdrawals and CPF LIFE premium deducted. If you are below 55 or turning 55 in the assessment year and your RA has not been created at the time we assess your eligibility, your total Special Account and Ordinary Account savings will be used for the eligibility assessment instead.

^^ Current Basic Retirement Sum for 2026.

title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
text:
You do not need to apply as your eligibility is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year

If you are eligible, CPF Board will notify you via email or post. You can check your eligibility via your Retirement Dashboard

To receive the matching grant you need to receive cash top-ups in your RA/SA , within the year that you are eligible for MRSS

You can make a cash top-up here

title: Fresh Start Housing Scheme
tagline: Helps families with children who are currently living in public rental flats to own a 2-room Flexi flat or 3-room flat on a shorter lease.
text:
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant of up to $120,000 for eligible first-timer families
Fresh Start Housing Grant of $75,000 for eligible second-timer families
Resale levy cap of $30,000 for eligible second-timer families
HDB Concessionary Loan is subject to credit assessment

title: Fresh Start Housing Scheme
tagline: Helps families with children who are currently living in public rental flats to own a 2-room Flexi flat or 3-room flat on a shorter lease.
text:
Both first-timer and second-timer families

Purchase a 2-room Flexi or 3-room Standard flat on a shorter lease in HDB sales launches i.e. Build-To-Order (BTO) or Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) exercises, or open booking of flats

HDB Concessionary Loan is subject to credit assessment

First-timer families

Enhanced CPF Housing Grant of up to $120,000 

Second-timer families

Fresh Start Housing Grant of $75,000 ($60,000 will be disbursed into CPF Ordinary Accounts before key collection; $15,000 may be disbursed after key collection into CPF Ordinary Accounts over 5 years)

Resale levy cap of $30,000

title: Fresh Start Housing Scheme
tagline: Helps families with children who are currently living in public rental flats to own a 2-room Flexi flat or 3-room flat on a shorter lease.
text:
Have at least 1 parent and 1 child who are Singapore Citizens

Applicant and spouse (if applicable) must be aged at least 35 and below 55 years old

Child must be aged below 21

Household monthly income of $7,000 and below

Stayed in public rental flat for at least 1 year

Have occupied public rental flat for at least 1 year, without accumulating 3 or more months of rental arrears in the preceding 12 months

Have not received any public rental tenancy discount under Relocation, Sale of Flat to Sitting Tenants, or Rent & Purchase Schemes

Do not own other properties overseas or locally, and have not disposed of any within the last 30 months

text: Remission of Buyer's Stamp Duty (BSD) and Seller's Stamp Duty (SSD) on transfer of HDB flat between immediate family members.

title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Transfer of HDB Flats within Family
tagline: Provides stamp duty remission for eligible transfers of HDB flats within families.
text:
The incoming lessee must be immediate family of any remaining lessee of the flat and a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Repayment goes to the outgoing lessee's Central Provident Fund (CPF) account

At least 1 of the flat’s current lessee remain after the transfer. Also, they must have either:

acquired the flat and paid stamp duty on its initial acquisition

acquired the flat via mutual exchange and paid stamp duty then

acquired the flat by assent, distribution or being pursuant to a will

acquired the flat via the right of survivorship as a joint tenant

title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Transfer of HDB Flats within Family
tagline: Provides stamp duty remission for eligible transfers of HDB flats within families.
text:
Provides stamp duty remission for eligible transfers of HDB flats within families.

Remission on BSD and SSD for HDB flat transfers among immediate family

Remission of Buyer's Stamp Duty (BSD) and Seller's Stamp Duty (SSD) on transfer of HDB flat between immediate family members.

The incoming lessee must be immediate family of any remaining lessee of the flat and a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Repayment goes to the outgoing lessee's Central Provident Fund (CPF) account

At least 1 of the flat’s current lessee remain after the transfer. Also, they must have either:

acquired the flat and paid stamp duty on its initial acquisition

acquired the flat via mutual exchange and paid stamp duty then

acquired the flat by assent, distribution or being pursuant to a will

acquired the flat via the right of survivorship as a joint tenant

Visit the e-Stamping Portal to e-Stamp and obtain the remission certificate

In order to e-Stamp, you must prepare written confirmation from HDB that confirms that the incoming lessee will form a family nucleus with the remaining lessee and is authorised by HDB to stay in the flat

If there is no incoming lessee, HDB confirmation is not required

title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples
tagline: Provides various stamp duty remissions for eligible couples buying new residential property or transferring their current one(s) as part of matrimonial proceedings.
text:
For married couples:

Full ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of their first residential property

Partial ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of a second residential property

Potential remission of BSD, ABSD and SSD on property transfers resulting from matrimonial proceedings

title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples
tagline: Provides various stamp duty remissions for eligible couples buying new residential property or transferring their current one(s) as part of matrimonial proceedings.
text:
Please refer to the following table for the various conditions and scenarios where a couple may receive remissions:

Joint purchase of first residential property by married couple

	

While ABSD will be applicable upon stamping, you may apply for the ABSD remission instead if you meet these conditions:

Married couple with at least 1 Singapore Citizen spouse

Property must be jointly purchased under couple’s names only

Both spouses do not already own any residential properties at the time of purchase

Joint purchase of second residential property by married couple

	

ABSD has already been paid on the second property

Married couple with at least 1 Singapore Citizen spouse

Property must be jointly purchased under couple’s names only

Couple must not own or have an interest in more than 1 residential property each when purchasing second property

Couple’s first property must be sold within 6 months after either:

Date of purchase of the second property (for completed property)

Issue date of the Temporary Occupation Permit (TOP) / Certificate of Statutory Completion (CSC), whichever is earlier, if the property was uncompleted at the time of purchase

Couples undergoing matrimonial proceedings

	

Transfer of property is due to either:

Court-ordered division of matrimonial assets (e.g. a proceeding for a decree of divorce, judicial separation or nullity of marriage under the law or religion)

Result of divorce proceedings

Property must be transferred from 1 party to another (both involved in the matrimonial proceedings)

Property may also be transferred by any child or children of the marriage / former marriage of the parties

title: Stamp Duty Remissions for Married and Divorced Couples
tagline: Provides various stamp duty remissions for eligible couples buying new residential property or transferring their current one(s) as part of matrimonial proceedings.
text:
For partial ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of a second residential property:

Navigate e-Stamping Portal to "Request" > "Apply for Refund"

For “Sale & Purchase – Purchase/ Acquisition” forms submitted on or after 2 Jul 2023, ABSD will be automatically refunded to the purchasers, within 6 weeks from the stamping of the first property’s sale, without needing a refund application

For stampings before 2 Jul 2023, you must manually provide the Stamp Duties (Spouses) (Remission of ABSD) refund application via the e-Stamping Portal > "Request" > "Apply for Refund"

For full ABSD remission for married couple’s joint purchase of their first residential property:

e-Stamp via the e-Stamping Portal to obtain the remission certificate

Alternatively, a legal representative, where engaged, will assist with the stamping

Applicants who want to purchase a unit from a property developer under the Executive Condominium Housing Scheme must take a housing loan from an FI

You need to obtain a valid Letter of Offer (LO) to confirm the loan offer from an FI before signing Agreement for Lease for a new flat or exercising the Option to Purchase for a resale flat

text:
Discover community programmes and events available in your neighbourhood

Stay healthy with fitness activities, broaden your experiences through outings, tours, and workshops. 

Get to know your neighbours better through localised events such as neighbourhood celebrations and festivities, coffee talks and dialogues. 

Contribute to the community by signing up for volunteering opportunities with like-minded individuals.

title: Home Caregiving Grant (HCG)
tagline: Provides financial support to help with caregiving expenses for households caring for persons with moderate to severe disabilities.
text: Monthly cash payout of $200, $400 or $600

title: Home Caregiving Grant (HCG)
tagline: Provides financial support to help with caregiving expenses for households caring for persons with moderate to severe disabilities.
text:
Care recipient must meet the following criteria:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident with a parent, child or spouse who is a Singapore Citizen

Lives in Singapore

Always requires some assistance on a permanent basis with at least 3 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)

Monthly per capita household income of less than or equal to $4,800 or less  (or property annual value of $21,000 or below for households with no income)

Those with multiple properties will receive the lowest HCG payout of $200 per month

Not in a residential long-term care institution

 

The 6 ADLs are: 

Eating

Bathing

Dressing

Transferring

Toileting

Walking or moving around

title: Home Caregiving Grant (HCG)

Online at the AIC eService Portal

At any of the AIC Links

You'll need to provide:

Completed Functional Assessment Report from your General Practitioner, a registered Nurse or a registered Physio/Occupational Therapist

text: A national strategy focused on preventive care to empower individuals to take steps toward better health, with the support of their Healthier SG clinic and community partners.

title: Healthier SG
tagline: A national strategy focused on preventive care to empower individuals to take steps toward better health, with the support of their Healthier SG clinic and community partners.
text:
Receive dedicated care from your preferred Healthier SG clinic
Prevent or manage chronic conditions and complications
Create your Health Plan with your family doctor
Receive healthcare and medication subsidies and benefits

title: Healthier SG
tagline: A national strategy focused on preventive care to empower individuals to take steps toward better health, with the support of their Healthier SG clinic and community partners.
text:
Benefits after enrolment include: 

Free initial consultation and subsequent annual Health Plan check-ins with your preferred Healthier SG clinic 

Healthpoints (worth $20) through Healthy 365 app after the first consultation and Health Plan creation

Special subsidies for Healthier SG screening tests and vaccinations (for eligible Singapore Citizens)

Healthier SG Chronic Tier subsidy at Healthier SG clinics for CHAS cardholders with high medication requirements (for selected chronic medications at prices comparable to polyclinic prices) 

Full MediSave coverage for treatment of chronic conditions listed under the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP), up to the relevant MediSave withdrawal limit

title: Healthier SG

text: Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents aged 40 years old and above to enrol with a Healthier SG clinic or polyclinic

title: Healthier SG
tagline: A national strategy focused on preventive care to empower individuals to take steps toward better health, with the support of their Healthier SG clinic and community partners.
text:
Self-enrol via HealthHub

If you are eligible, you will be invited via SMS to enrol in Healthier SG. You can do so in three simple steps:

Use the HealthHub app, which can be downloaded from Google Play or Apple App Store.

Choose your preferred Healthier SG clinic from the list of more than 1,000 Healthier SG clinics.

Book an appointment for your fully subsidised first Health Plan consultation.

For a detailed step-by-step guide on how to enrol, click here.

If you have not received an SMS invitation, you can:

Request one by submitting your details at https://go.gov.sg/healthiersg-sms-invite

Contact the MOH hotline at 6325 9220

Enrol with caregiver's assistance

You can grant your caregiver/family member/loved one access to your HealthHub, so they can use their smartphone to help you enrol in Healthier SG. Follow the step-by-step guide at these links:

 Grant your caregiver/family member access

 Assist your family member’s enrolment

Other assisted enrolment

Visit any ServiceSG Centre or AIC Link for enrolment assistance. To find an enrolment station near you, visit HealthierSGGoWhere

title: Housing Loan from HDB
tagline: Provides eligible buyers with financing via housing loan to purchase or take over ownership of a HDB flat.
text:
Eligibility for up to 75% Loan-to-Value (LTV) limit for new and resale flats
Monthly instalments capped at 30% of applicants' monthly income
Concessionary interest rate for HDB housing loans

title: Housing Loan from HDB
tagline: Provides eligible buyers with financing via housing loan to purchase or take over ownership of a HDB flat.
text:
LTV limit of up to 75% for new and resale flats

Monthly instalments capped at 30% of the applicants' monthly income

Concessionary interest rate for HDB housing loans

Option to use CPF savings to pay for the loan

Applicants may also retain their savings or use them to pay for CPF Home Protection Insurance

title: Housing Loan from HDB
tagline: Provides eligible buyers with financing via housing loan to purchase or take over ownership of a HDB flat.
text:
At least 1 applicant must be a Singapore Citizen

The core member(s)* in the HFE letter application must not have previously taken 2 or more housing loans from HDB

Singles aged 35 and above who are buying a 2-room Flexi flat on a 99-year lease in non-mature estates or a 5-room or smaller resale flat

Seniors aged 55 and above who are not applying for a 2-room Flexi flat on a short lease or a Community Care Apartment

Your average gross monthly household income must not exceed:

$14,000 for families

$21,000 for extended families

$7,000 for singles under the Single Singapore Citizen Scheme

All persons listed in the application:

Cannot own or have an interest in any private residential property, local or overseas

Must not have disposed of any private residential property in the last 30 months before the application for an HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter

*Core member(s) refer to the applicant(s) and occupier(s) in an HFE letter application who enable the applicant(s) to qualify for a flat purchase under an eligibility scheme. All core members must remain in the flat application, and physically occupy the flat during the minimum occupation period (MOP) after the flat purchase. Their names cannot be removed.

Second HDB housing loan
You must also meet the following additional requirements:

At Point of Flat Purchase

	

Conditions to Take a Second HDB Housing Loan

You have disposed of the existing flat or private residential property and do not own any HDB flat

	

You and the essential occupier have to use up to 50% of the cash proceeds from the disposal of the last-owned HDB flat or from private residential property disposed of at least 30 months prior to the HFE letter application.

You may retain up to $20,000 in your CPF Ordinary Account (OA) and use the remaining balance to buy the flat.

You are buying a HDB flat before disposing of the existing one

	

You will be charged the commercial interest rate (pegged to the average non-promotional interest rate for HDB flats offered by the 3 local Financial Institutions (FIs)†) for the housing loan to buy the next flat.

The interest rate will be converted to the concessionary rate after you have disposed of the existing flat and used the CPF refund and up to 50% of the cash proceeds from the disposal to reduce the housing loan amount for the next flat. From the CPF monies refunded, you may retain up to $20,000 in your CPF OA.

†The 3 local FIs are DBS Bank Limited, Overseas-Chinese Banking Corporation Limited, and United Overseas Bank Limited.

Please note that your second HDB housing loan amount will be reduced by the full CPF refund and part of the cash proceeds from the disposal of the following residential properties:

Existing or last-owned HDB flat

Last-owned private residential property, disposed of at least 30 months prior to the HFE letter application

The manner of disposal includes the sale, transfer or surrender of a flat or private residential property, or by any other method under the Housing and Development Act or any other written law.

You may read more on how the cash proceeds from the disposal are used on HDB's website.

title: Housing Loan from HDB
tagline: Provides eligible buyers with financing via housing loan to purchase or take over ownership of a HDB flat.
text:
Obtain a HFE letter through the HDB Flat Portal

The HFE letter will inform you of your eligibility for purchasing a flat and the amount of housing loan and grants you can receive

Use the payment plan calculator to estimate your budget and payments required

title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.

title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text:
Payout up to the sum assured will be made to HDB or the mortgagee directly
Processing of claim application generally takes 5 working days upon CPF Board's receipt of requested documents

title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text:
For claims under death grounds

HPS benefits will be paid in a lump sum (based on the share of HPS cover) to HDB or the mortgagee directly to settle the outstanding housing loan. 

Any excess claim amounts will be paid to the insured member's CPF Ordinary Account. 

Thereafter, the deceased member’s CPF savings will be distributed to nominees, or next-of-kins depending on whether there is a valid nomination made. 

For claims admitted under medical grounds

Housing instalments (based on the share of HPS cover) may be paid by HPS for a period of up to two years, before a review of insured member's eligibility to claim the remaining insured sum in full is carried out.

title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text:
CPF members who own a HDB flat and are HPS policyholders paying their monthly housing instalments using CPF savings or cash are eligible to apply for HPS coverage.

HPS claim payout is made to:

Next-of-kin of HPS policyholder who is deceased, or

HPS policyholder who is certified by a registered medical practitioner under the Medical Registration Act to be suffering from:

Terminal illness: an illness that is likely to result in the death of the insured CPF member within 12 months

Total permanent disability: the inability to take part in any employment permanently, or the total permanent loss of physical function of any of the following:

Both eyes, or

Two limbs, or

One eye and one limb

title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text:
Apply online for HPS coverage via the CPF website with your Singpass.

Claim on death grounds

If the insured member has an outstanding housing loan, CPF Board will automatically assess the deceased’s claim eligibility upon Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA)'s notification. 

CPF Board will inform the co-owner(s) or the insured's next-of-kin of the outcome and if additional information is required.

Claim on medical grounds

Submit your application to CPF Board directly via the CPF website

tagline: Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
text:
Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.

Payout up to the sum assured will be made to HDB or the mortgagee directly
Processing of claim application generally takes 5 working days upon CPF Board's receipt of requested documents

For claims under death grounds

HPS benefits will be paid in a lump sum (based on the share of HPS cover) to HDB or the mortgagee directly to settle the outstanding housing loan. 

Any excess claim amounts will be paid to the insured member's CPF Ordinary Account. 

Thereafter, the deceased member’s CPF savings will be distributed to nominees, or next-of-kins depending on whether there is a valid nomination made. 

For claims admitted under medical grounds

Housing instalments (based on the share of HPS cover) may be paid by HPS for a period of up to two years, before a review of insured member's eligibility to claim the remaining insured sum in full is carried out.

CPF members who own a HDB flat and are HPS policyholders paying their monthly housing instalments using CPF savings or cash are eligible to apply for HPS coverage.

HPS claim payout is made to:

Next-of-kin of HPS policyholder who is deceased, or

HPS policyholder who is certified by a registered medical practitioner under the Medical Registration Act to be suffering from:

Terminal illness: an illness that is likely to result in the death of the insured CPF member within 12 months

Total permanent disability: the inability to take part in any employment permanently, or the total permanent loss of physical function of any of the following:

Both eyes, or

Two limbs, or

One eye and one limb

Apply online for HPS coverage via the CPF website with your Singpass.

Claim on death grounds

If the insured member has an outstanding housing loan, CPF Board will automatically assess the deceased’s claim eligibility upon Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA)'s notification. 

CPF Board will inform the co-owner(s) or the insured's next-of-kin of the outcome and if additional information is required.

Claim on medical grounds

Submit your application to CPF Board directly via the CPF website

text:
Adopting a child in accordance with any Singapore law relating to the adopting of children

You are not the natural mother of the child

Child is below 12 months old on Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date

FIA date on or after 1 January 2021

If child is not a Singapore Citizen on FIA date, a Dependent’s Pass has been issued and either you or your husband has been a Singapore Citizen since the Dependent’s Pass issue date

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before FIA date

You do not qualify for the AL scheme Adoption Leave (AL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment

title: Tax Payment Support

text:
Enables longer payment plans for applicants experiencing difficulties in paying their taxes
Grants retirees the Extended Giro Scheme for Residential Property (Retirees), offering longer instalment plans for tax payment

title: Tax Payment Support
tagline: Supports eligible individuals in their tax payments through various payment arrangements such as longer GIRO-based instalment plans.
text:
Longer instalment plans may be made available for individuals or businesses facing financial challenges or other extenuating circumstances

Eligible retirees may apply for the Extended Giro Scheme for Residential Property (Retirees), which provides a longer instalment plan of up to 24 months for property tax payment

title: Tax Payment Support
tagline: Supports eligible individuals in their tax payments through various payment arrangements such as longer GIRO-based instalment plans.
text:
For individuals who experience unexpected or extenuating circumstances due to the following:

Financial issues

High medical expenses

Loss of employment or income

Lawsuits

You may be required to submit documentary evidence to support your request for a longer instalment plan

For retirees:

To be eligible for the Extended Giro Scheme for Residential Property (Retirees), applicants must meet all the following conditions:

You are residing in the property (and taxed at owner-occupier tax rates)

Your assessable income for Year of Assessment 2023 does not exceed $34,000

All property owners, including yourself, are aged 65 or above in 2024

There is outstanding property tax payable for the property

If applicants cease to be eligible, their extended instalment plan will be reverted to the standard instalment plan

title: Tax Payment Support
tagline: Supports eligible individuals in their tax payments through various payment arrangements such as longer GIRO-based instalment plans.
text:
For individuals:

	

Apply for an extended instalment plan via the 'Apply for Payment Plan' digital service through the IRAS myTax Portal

Please note that instalment plans are only available with GIRO

For existing GIRO users, cancel your current plan and set up a new one. You may refer to this guide on cancelling and reactivating GIRO for detailed instructions

For applicants without a GIRO arrangement for Individual Income and Property Tax, you may apply for GIRO (with a DBS / POSB or OCBC bank account) via myTax Portal and set up your new plan online. Refer to this guide for detailed instructions

For businesses without an existing GIRO arrangement for Corporate Income Tax, Property Tax and Goods and Services Tax:

	

Apply for GIRO with a DBS / POSB, OCBC, UOB, Bank of China or Maybank account via the IRAS myTax Portal

Arrange a new plan

Alternatively, you may refer to this guide for the detailed steps

Where you do not have account with any of the participating banks listed above, you may:  

Fill up this form to request for GIRO application form 

View on applying GIRO here

Please refer to IRAS’ site on alternative tax payment plans for more details

text: Provides up to $40,000 in housing grants to eligible families if they had previously received CPF Housing Grant for Singles and now qualify for the CPF Housing Grant for Families.

title: CPF Housing Grant (Families) - Top-Up Grant
tagline: Provides up to $40,000 in housing grants to eligible families if they had previously received CPF Housing Grant for Singles and now qualify for the CPF Housing Grant for Families.
text:
Up to $40,000 in Top-Up grant for eligible families
Grant is directly credited into all eligible Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident applicants’ CPF Ordinary Accounts
Available for those who previously received a Singles Grant

title: CPF Housing Grant (Families) - Top-Up Grant
tagline: Provides up to $40,000 in housing grants to eligible families if they had previously received CPF Housing Grant for Singles and now qualify for the CPF Housing Grant for Families.
text:
Up to $40,000 in Top-Up Grant

The applicant applying for the Top-Up Grant may also apply for other grants (e.g. Enhanced Housing Grant and Proximity Housing Grant), if eligible

If your household is eligible for the grant, all eligible Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident applicants will receive equal shares of the grant, fully credited into their CPF Ordinary Accounts

title: CPF Housing Grant (Families) - Top-Up Grant
tagline: Provides up to $40,000 in housing grants to eligible families if they had previously received CPF Housing Grant for Singles and now qualify for the CPF Housing Grant for Families.
text:
You must be a Singapore Citizen aged 21 and above, and your spouse or child is a Singapore Citizen / Permanent Resident

You have previously received the CPF Housing Grant for Resale Flats (Singles) or bought a 2-room flat (includes Flexi) from HDB as a single, and are now married

Your average gross monthly household income must not exceed HDB’s prevailing income ceiling of $14,000 for resale flat or $7,000 for 2-room Flexi flat from HDB

You and your spouse, as well as other persons listed in the application, must not have ownership or interest in any private residential property, local or overseas, and no disposal of such property within the last 30 months prior to the application

You and your spouse, as well as other persons listed in the application, must not have ownership or interest in more than one non-residential private property, local or overseas, and no disposal of such property within the last 30 months prior to the application

title: CPF Housing Grant (Families) - Top-Up Grant
tagline: Provides up to $40,000 in housing grants to eligible families if they had previously received CPF Housing Grant for Singles and now qualify for the CPF Housing Grant for Families.
text:
You must submit your application within 6 months of the first qualifying event (e.g. marriage to Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident spouse/ spouse or child obtaining Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency / birth of Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident child)

You can do so via the HDB InfoWEB > 'e-Services' > 'Residential' > 'Living in an HDB Flat' > 'Application for Top-Up Grant'

title: Infant and Childcare Subsidies

Working applicants: Basic Subsidy of $600 for infant care and $300 for childcare

Additional Subsidy for working applicants

title: Infant and Childcare Subsidies
tagline: Provides tiered subsidies to help cover infant care and childcare fees for Singapore Citizen children from different income backgrounds.
text:
Working applicants (mother or single father who works at least 56 hours per month)

Basic Subsidy of $600 for infant care and $300 for childcare

Additional Subsidy of up to $710 for infant care and up to $467 for childcare

Non-working applicants

Basic Subsidy of $150 for infant care and childcare

Eligible applicants who are not working or who are non-parent caregivers can apply for Special Approval to receive the higher Basic Subsidy and Additional Subsidy that working applicants receive

title: Infant and Childcare Subsidies
tagline: Provides tiered subsidies to help cover infant care and childcare fees for Singapore Citizen children from different income backgrounds.
text:
Types of subsidies

	

Eligibility criteria

For all subsidies

	

Child must be a Singapore Citizen

Enrolled in an infant care or childcare centre licensed by ECDA

Additional Subsidy

	

Working applicants (mother or single father who works at least 56 hours per month)

Monthly household income of up to $12,000, or monthly household income per capita of up to $3,000 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household

From 1 January 2027, monthly household income thresholds will be raised to $15,000, or $3,400 per capita

Special Approval

	

Main subsidy applicants who are not working due to the following reasons:

Looking for a job

Studying, training, or on course for at least 56 hours a month

Pregnancy

Medically unfit for work due to hospitalisation, long-term illness and/or permanent disability

Taking care of a special needs child

Taking care of a sick family member who requires a full-time caregiver

Caring full-time for a younger child aged 24 months and below who is not enrolled in a preschool

Incarcerated

Non-parent caregivers who are caring for the child such as grandparents, legal guardian, foster parents, and head of a children’s home

Families who are tenants of a rental flat under the HDB’s Public Rental Scheme or are beneficiaries of ComCare Short-To-Medium-Term Assistance (SMTA) or Long-Term-Assistance (LTA) will automatically qualify for maximum preschool subsidies upon application for Special Approval

title: Government-Paid Maternity Benefit (GPMB)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of income support for eligible working mothers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Maternity Leave.
text:
For live births

Your child is a Singapore Citizen or becomes a Singapore Citizen within 12 months from birth

Your child's date of birth/estimated delivery date is on or after 1 Jan 2017

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before the child's birth

Not eligible for Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) or maternity leave under the Employment Act, except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment.

For stillbirths:

Similar conditions apply, with the following additional criteria:

The date of stillbirth is on or after 1 November 2021

You are a Singapore Citizen at the time of stillbirth or the child’s father is a Singapore Citizen and was lawfully married to the mother at some point between conception and stillbirth

tagline: Provides a $10,000 housing subsidy for eligible Singapore Citizen/ Permanent Resident households when a family member becomes a Singapore Citizen.
text:
$10,000 housing subsidy for eligible Singapore Citizen/ Permanent Resident households when a household member acquires Singapore citizenship
Citizen Top-Up is credited into CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible family members
Can be used for repayment or redemption of housing loan, future HDB flat purchase or CPF withdrawal at age 55

title: Housing Subsidy - Citizen Top-Up
tagline: Provides a $10,000 housing subsidy for eligible Singapore Citizen/ Permanent Resident households when a family member becomes a Singapore Citizen.
text:
$10,000 housing subsidy for eligible households when a qualifying member becomes a Singapore Citizen

The subsidy is converted into equal shares and credited fully into the CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible family members

Only core flat owners can use their share of the CPF housing grants to pay for the flat e.g. loan repayment

The Citizen Top-Up can be used for a lump sum repayment or full redemption of an HDB housing loan

For housing loans from financial institutions, you may additionally use it for monthly instalments

You must clear all outstanding housing loan arrears before you can use the grant for the above purposes

If you have any remaining Citizen Top-Up balance and no housing loan, you may use it to buy another HDB flat in the future, or to form part of the CPF funds available for withdrawal under CPF rules at age 55

title: Housing Subsidy - Citizen Top-Up
tagline: Provides a $10,000 housing subsidy for eligible Singapore Citizen/ Permanent Resident households when a family member becomes a Singapore Citizen.
text:
Households that have paid a $10,000 premium when buying a flat from HDB

Households who received a reduced Family Grant for a resale flat, a Design Build and Sell Scheme flat or an Executive Condominium unit

The Citizen Top-Up is granted when a Permanent Resident spouse, parent, child or sibling^ originally listed in the flat application obtains Singapore Citizenship, or when a Singapore Citizen child is born to the Singapore Citizen applicant and spouse originally listed in the flat application

^ For siblings, this qualification only applies to flats purchased as orphaned siblings or for families with a non-resident spouse.

title: CAREwell
tagline: CAREwell is a program that supports caregivers in their caregiving role, emotional and mental wellness, decision- making regarding services, and care management.
text:
CAREwell offers tailored support to caregivers through a combination of approaches:

Trauma-informed emotional support and mental health advisory for caregivers

Professional support on service navigation, care coordination, and service integration for meeting dependent and family’s social and health needs.

Each family is paired with a Keyworker, a trained professional in social work, healthcare, special needs, or related fields. The Keyworker will work with the caregiver to assess needs and identify challenges, establish a family support plan, and implement strategies to support both the caregiver and their dependent.
