title: CAREwell
tagline: CAREwell is a program that supports caregivers in their caregiving role, emotional and mental wellness, decision- making regarding services, and care management.
text:
CAREwell is a program that supports caregivers in their caregiving role, emotional and mental wellness, decision- making regarding services, and care management.

Each family is guided by a transdisciplinary CAREwell Key Worker.
CAREwell Key Workers meet with caregiver for needs analysis and establish family support plan.

CAREwell offers tailored support to caregivers through a combination of approaches:

Trauma-informed emotional support and mental health advisory for caregivers

Professional support on service navigation, care coordination, and service integration for meeting dependent and family’s social and health needs.

Each family is paired with a Keyworker, a trained professional in social work, healthcare, special needs, or related fields. The Keyworker will work with the caregiver to assess needs and identify challenges, establish a family support plan, and implement strategies to support both the caregiver and their dependent.

CAREwell program is a highly subsidised program made affordable to all caregivers of persons with special needs.

Interested caregivers are encouraged to get in touch with us to find out the eligible subsidies available to them.

Interested caregivers or external agency can click on the request link for CAREwell. For caregivers, you will receive an email with a link to ‘CSG Caregiver Needs Form’. Fill in the form and our team will contact you shortly.

Applicants to CAREwell are screened for suitability before being enrolled into the programme.

https://caring.sg/carewell

For natural or adoptive parents who do not qualify for Shared Parental Leave (SPL) due to their employment arrangement.
Eligible parents can receive Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB), capped at $2,500 per week.

title: Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB)
tagline: Support eligible working parents who do not qualify for the Shared Parental Leave (SPL) scheme due to their employment arrangements.
text:
For child born/estimated delivery date/Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, eligible parents can claim up to 6 weeks of income support. 

For child born/estimated delivery date/FIA date on or after 1 Apr 2026, eligible parents can claim up to 10 weeks of income support.  

Government payment capped at $2,500 per week.

title: Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB)
tagline: Support eligible working parents who do not qualify for the Shared Parental Leave (SPL) scheme due to their employment arrangements.
text:
For natural parents with live births: 

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks  

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth 

For adoptive parents: 

You are not the natural father or mother of the child 

Child’s Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks  

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks  

The child must be below 12 months old on FIA date 

If child is not a Singapore Citizen (SC), either adoptive mother or father of child has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 

Applicable to all: 

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before child’s birth 

For fathers: lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and birth or within 12 months of birth 

Not eligible for Shared Parental Leave (SPL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment

title: Take-a-Break (TAB)
tagline:
Take-a-Break (TAB) is a respite care programme to provide caregivers temporary relief from their caregiving responsibilities.

Based on their needs, caregivers may choose from a range of home-based respite care services and determine the frequency of service over a specific duration.

Subsidies are available for eligible caregivers.

title: Renting Out of HDB Flats
tagline: Enables eligible Singapore Citizen flat owners to rent out their HDB flat after meeting the Minimum Occupation Period.
text:
Only Singapore Citizens can rent out HDB flats
Fulfill Minimum Occupation Period (MOP) before renting out (Prime and Plus flats cannot be rented out)
Non-Citizen Quota applies for non-Malaysian tenants
Approval from HDB required before renting out

title: Renting Out of HDB Flats
tagline: Enables eligible Singapore Citizen flat owners to rent out their HDB flat after meeting the Minimum Occupation Period.
text:
Receive rental income through renting out your flat where necessary  

Provide an option to seniors to monetise your flat and move to live with family members

title: Renting Out of HDB Flats
tagline: Enables eligible Singapore Citizen flat owners to rent out their HDB flat after meeting the Minimum Occupation Period.
text:
Owner must be a Singapore Citizen to rent out the HDB flat. Singapore Permanent Residents are not allowed to do so.

Fulfill a Minimum Occupation Period (MOP) of 5 years, or 3 years for non-subsidised flats purchased before 30 August 2010. Non-subsidised flats refer to those purchased from the open market without a housing grant. Owners of Prime and Plus flats are not allowed to rent out the flat even after the 10-year MOP. Owners of 2-room Flexi flats, Studio Apartments and Lease Buyback Scheme (LBS) flats are also not eligible to rent out their flat.

MOP is calculated from the date of purchase or the effective date of transfer of flat ownership.

Do consider the Non-Citizen Quota, if owners are renting to tenants who are non-Malaysian non-citizens (Singapore Permanent Residents or foreigners). Use HDB’s e-Service to check if your flat can be rented out to non-Malaysian non-citizens.

title: Renting Out of HDB Flats
tagline: Enables eligible Singapore Citizen flat owners to rent out their HDB flat after meeting the Minimum Occupation Period.
text:
Flat owners must ensure that they comply with the regulations listed on HDB’s website before renting out their flats. These include the number of tenants and their legal residency status.

'Apply/ Manage Application to Rent Out Flat' e-Service or MyHDB mobile app. An administrative fee of $18 applies for each application. 

Notify HDB of any changes in rental details (e.g. changes to your tenants’ particulars or if your tenants move out of the flat) within 7 days, and obtain approval for new tenants before they move in.

Flat owners must ensure that they comply with the regulations listed on HDB’s website before renting out their flats. These include the number of tenants and their legal residency status.

'Apply/ Manage Application to Rent Out Flat' e-Service or MyHDB mobile app. An administrative fee of $18 applies for each application. 

Notify HDB of any changes in rental details (e.g. changes to your tenants’ particulars or if your tenants move out of the flat) within 7 days, and obtain approval for new tenants before they move in.

Submit an enquiry

title: MOE Tuition Fee Loan (TFL) & Study Loan (SL) Schemes
tagline:
From 1 July 2026, MOE will be implementing a new Higher Education Student Loan (HESL) scheme to replace the existing government loan schemes - Tuition Fee Loan (TFL), Study Loan (SL) and Overseas Student Programme Loan (OSPL), which will be merged under the new HESL scheme. Institutions will stop accepting new TFL, SL and OSPL applications before 1 June 2026.

If you intend to apply for loan before 1 July 2026, please check with your institution regarding the respective cut-off dates for TFL, SL and OSPL application. If you wish to apply for a loan after the cut-off dates, you can apply for the HESL via a new portal from 1 July 2026. For details on the HESL scheme and its application process, please visit MOE website (https://www.moe.gov.sg/financial-matters/government-loan-schemes) or your institution website.

text:
TFL and means-tested SL schemes can cover up to 100% of subsidised tuition fees for eligible Singapore Citizen students
Eligible students who meet the income eligibility criteria can also apply for a living allowance loan to cover their education expenses under the means-tested SL scheme
Loans are interest-free during the course of study; interest and repayment commences only upon a student’s graduation or leaving the institution

tagline:

text:
For TFL

For Polytechnics, LASALLE College of the Arts (LASALLE) or Nanyang Academy of Fine Arts (NAFA) diploma students

	

Loan coverage of up to 75% of subsidised tuition fees payable.

For Autonomous University students, or LASALLE or NAFA undergraduates

	

Loan coverage of up to 90% of subsidised tuition fees payable.

For SL

For Undergraduates

	

Covers up to 10% of the subsidised Singapore Citizen tuition fees payable and a living allowance loan of $3,600 per year

For Diploma Students

	

Covers up to 25% of subsidised tuition fees payable and a living allowance loan of $2,000 per year

Students studying full-time MOE-subsidised diploma courses in any of the local polytechnics.

SC, PR or international students taking full-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate courses in autonomous universities.

SC and PR taking full-time MOE-subsidised undergraduate courses in LASALLE or NAFA.

For Diploma Students

SC or PR taking full-time MOE-subsidised diploma courses at Polytechnics, LASALLE and NAFA.

tagline: Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.
text: Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.

title: Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG)
tagline: Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.
text:

Apply at your child's kindergarten

title: Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG)
tagline: Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.
text:
Subsidies range between $17 and $161 per month

Subsidy amount depends on monthly household income, and will be directly disbursed to the child's kindergarten

Eligible applicants will receive a yearly KiFAS SUG capped at $240 per year to help cover initial costs such as registration fee, deposit, school uniform, insurance, and educational material fees, in addition to the basic subsidies

title: Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG)
tagline: Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.
text:
Child must be a Singapore Citizen

Enrolled in an Anchor Operator or Ministry of Education Kindergarten

Monthly household income of up to $12,000, or monthly household income per capita of up to $3,000 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household

From 1 January 2027, monthly household income thresholds will be raised to $15,000, or $3,400 per capita

Yearly SUG:

Applicants with monthly household income of up to $1,900, or monthly household income per capita of up to $650 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household, can further apply for SUG

Each application is assessed on a case-by-case basis

title: Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up)
tagline: Enhanced tier of Workfare Skills Support (WSS), supporting lower-wage Singaporean workers who go for long-form training.
text: Enhanced tier of Workfare Skills Support (WSS), supporting lower-wage Singaporean workers who go for long-form training.

title: Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up)
tagline: Enhanced tier of Workfare Skills Support (WSS), supporting lower-wage Singaporean workers who go for long-form training.
text:
Provides training allowance of at least $300 per month
Covers a wide catalogue of long-form courses (including Nitec, Higher Nitec, Diplomas, Post-Diplomas, and Undergraduate courses)
Covers multiple bouts of long-form training
Apply online via the Training Allowance System using Singpass

title: Workfare Skills Support (Level-Up)
tagline: Enhanced tier of Workfare Skills Support (WSS), supporting lower-wage Singaporean workers who go for long-form training.
text:
Training Allowance (TA)   

Full-time training: 50% of average income over the latest available 12-month period, with minimum allowance of $300 per month 

Part-time training: $300 per month 

Coverage for multiple rounds of long-form training

Every individual can receive: 

Up to 24 months of monthly TA between ages 30 to 39

Up to another 24 months of monthly TA from age 40. This is a shared cap between WSS (Level-Up) and the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme

For each age period, the cap is shared between both full-time and part-time training

Courses supported include:

SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme courses

Nitec, Higher Nitec, Diplomas, Post-Diplomas, and Undergraduate courses by Institutes of Higher Learning

From 2026 Q4, long-form Workforce Skills Qualifications (WSQ) full qualifications

Training Allowance (TA)   

Full-time training: 50% of average income over the latest available 12-month period, with minimum allowance of $300 per month 

Part-time training: $300 per month 

Coverage for multiple rounds of long-form training

Every individual can receive: 

Up to 24 months of monthly TA between ages 30 to 39

Up to another 24 months of monthly TA from age 40. This is a shared cap between WSS (Level-Up) and the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme

For each age period, the cap is shared between both full-time and part-time training

Courses supported include:

SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme courses

Nitec, Higher Nitec, Diplomas, Post-Diplomas, and Undergraduate courses by Institutes of Higher Learning

From 2026 Q4, long-form Workforce Skills Qualifications (WSQ) full qualifications

Singapore Citizen;

At least 30 years old or older as of 31 December of the work year;

Earn an average gross monthly income of not more than $3,000 for the past 12 months;

Have declared your net trade income and made MediSave contributions in full if you are self-employed;

Live in a property with an annual value of $21,000 and below as at 31 December of the preceding year;

Own not more than one property; and

If you are married:

you and your spouse together not own more than one property; and

the assessable income of your spouse does not exceed $70,000 of the preceding Year of Assessment

The above is similar to the Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) criteria.

You can apply online via the Training Allowance System (TAS) using your Singpass.

WSG Hotline: 1800 536 8333

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

title: Lively Places Fund
tagline: Provides funding of up to $20,000 for community-driven projects that enhance public spaces and foster community bonds.
text: Provides funding of up to $20,000 for community-driven projects that enhance public spaces and foster community bonds.

title: Lively Places Fund
tagline: Provides funding of up to $20,000 for community-driven projects that enhance public spaces and foster community bonds.
text:
Funding of up to $5,000 for small, short-term community projects
Funding of up to $20,000 for eligible larger projects
Traffic redirection support provided for street events

title: Lively Places Fund
tagline: Provides funding of up to $20,000 for community-driven projects that enhance public spaces and foster community bonds.
text:
The funds awarded will depend on the type and scale of the project. Please refer to the below table for a detailed breakdown:

Category

	

Award Sum

	

Description

Project fund

	

Up to $5,000

	

For smaller, short-term action plans or activities for community bonding in HDB neighbourhoods

e.g. block parties, anti-littering campaign, community recycling day

Building Fund

	

Up to $5,000

	

For projects that will involve a physical transformation of existing public spaces and have an implementation period of 6 months or less

e.g. community arts projects and short-term pop-up installations to activate spaces

Up to $20,000

	

For projects that will involve a physical transformation of existing public spaces and have an implementation period of more than 6 months

e.g. conversion of void decks into community living rooms and cafes, longer-term pop-up installations to activate spaces

Street Closure

	

N/A

	

For short-term projects that transform streets into active public spaces for the community

Support provided will be limited to road closure essentials, consisting of safety barriers, signage and security personnel

title: Lively Places Fund
tagline: Provides funding of up to $20,000 for community-driven projects that enhance public spaces and foster community bonds.
text:
Open to all Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents

Individual or team applicants, with at least 1 applicant above 17 years old

Not for projects already funded by other agencies

Co-funding between agencies may be considered for new projects if the project meets the fund’s objectives

Previous awardees must complete existing projects before reapplying

Cannot support projects that duplicate existing programmes

Does not cover projects or studies started before funding approval

All proposals will be reviewed based on the following criteria:

Fund objective

Level of neighbourhood involvment

Level of community match

Outcomes

Refer to the HDB InfoWEB for more information regarding the criteria.

title: Merdeka Generation Package
tagline: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
text: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.

title: Merdeka Generation Package
tagline: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
text: Provides various health-related benefits to support seniors’ ageing, such as additional Outpatient Care subsidies and additional MediShield (MSHL) premium subsidies

title: Merdeka Generation Package
tagline: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
text:
Provides the following benefits to support healthcare for Merdeka seniors:

Additional Outpatient Care Subsidies 

	

Additional subsidies for outpatient care at polyclinics, public Specialist Outpatient Clinics and Community Health Assist Scheme clinics.

Additional MediShield Life Premium Subsidies 

	

Provides an additional 5% subsidy for your annual premiums, increasing to 10% after turning 75 years old.

[Redemption has ended wef. 31 Dec 2023] 
$100 Passion Silver Card Top-Up

	

One-off $100 top-up for use on active ageing programmes, public transport, merchant benefits etc (redemption has ceased wef. 31 Dec 2023).

[Top-Up has ended] 
Annual MediSave top-ups of $200 (2019-2023)

	

$200 Medisave top-ups annually from 2019 to 2023. 

MediSave can be used to cover hospitalisation and treatment costs, pay various medical-related premiums etc.

[Incentives have ceased]
CareShield Life Additional Participation Incentives 

	

Provides participation incentives (totalling $3,000 spread across 10 years) for seniors who join CareShield Life by 31 Dec 2024, in addition to any other premium subsidies seniors may receive.

If you would like more details on the above benefits as well as other useful information about this Package, please refer here.

title: Merdeka Generation Package
tagline: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
text:
Singapore Citizens who were:

born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959; and became citizens on or before 31 December 1996, or

born on or before 31 December 1949; became citizens on or before 31 December 1996; and are not currently receiving the Pioneer Generation Package

You may check your eligibility via the LifeSG app or the Singpass Mobile app

title: Merdeka Generation Package
tagline: Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.
text:
Supporting Merdeka Generation seniors in their silver years by helping them to stay active, healthy and providing peace of mind over future healthcare costs.

Provides various health-related benefits to support seniors’ ageing, such as additional Outpatient Care subsidies and additional MediShield (MSHL) premium subsidies

Provides the following benefits to support healthcare for Merdeka seniors:

Additional Outpatient Care Subsidies 

	

Additional subsidies for outpatient care at polyclinics, public Specialist Outpatient Clinics and Community Health Assist Scheme clinics.

Additional MediShield Life Premium Subsidies 

	

Provides an additional 5% subsidy for your annual premiums, increasing to 10% after turning 75 years old.

[Redemption has ended wef. 31 Dec 2023] 
$100 Passion Silver Card Top-Up

	

One-off $100 top-up for use on active ageing programmes, public transport, merchant benefits etc (redemption has ceased wef. 31 Dec 2023).

[Top-Up has ended] 
Annual MediSave top-ups of $200 (2019-2023)

	

$200 Medisave top-ups annually from 2019 to 2023. 

MediSave can be used to cover hospitalisation and treatment costs, pay various medical-related premiums etc.

[Incentives have ceased]
CareShield Life Additional Participation Incentives 

	

Provides participation incentives (totalling $3,000 spread across 10 years) for seniors who join CareShield Life by 31 Dec 2024, in addition to any other premium subsidies seniors may receive.

If you would like more details on the above benefits as well as other useful information about this Package, please refer here.

Singapore Citizens who were:

born from 1 January 1950 to 31 December 1959; and became citizens on or before 31 December 1996, or

born on or before 31 December 1949; became citizens on or before 31 December 1996; and are not currently receiving the Pioneer Generation Package

You may check your eligibility via the LifeSG app or the Singpass Mobile app

No application required

Eligible individuals will receive the benefits automatically

For general enquiries on Merdeka Generation eligibility, MediSave top-ups and MediShield Life Subsidy: 1800-222-2888

For loss of Merdeka Generation Card, and other healthcare-related enquiries: 1800-650-6060

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

title: Lease Buyback Scheme
tagline: Provides seniors with a steady stream of retirement income when they sell the tail-end of their flat lease to HDB while they continue to occupy the flat for the lease they retained.
text:
Sell part of your flat's lease to HDB and choose to retain the length of lease based on the age of the youngest owner

title: Lease Buyback Scheme
tagline: Provides seniors with a steady stream of retirement income when they sell the tail-end of their flat lease to HDB while they continue to occupy the flat for the lease they retained.
text:
At least 1 owner must be a Singapore Citizen

All owners 65 years and older

Monthly household income not more than $14,000

No concurrent ownership of second property, and no more than 1 non-residential private property

All owners have been living in the flat for at least 5 years, or 10 years for Plus or Prime flats

Flat has at least 20 years of lease to sell to HDB

title: Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS)
tagline: Provides tax relief to encourage individuals to save for retirement, over and above their CPF savings. Investment returns are tax-free before withdrawal and only 50% of withdrawals are taxable at retirement.
text:
Provides equivalent tax relief on contributions to SRS, capped at $15,300 for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents and $35,700 for foreigners

Investment returns on SRS contributions are tax-free before withdrawal, and only 50% of withdrawals will be taxable at retirement

title: Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS)
tagline: Provides tax relief to encourage individuals to save for retirement, over and above their CPF savings. Investment returns are tax-free before withdrawal and only 50% of withdrawals are taxable at retirement.
text:
You can only have 1 SRS account at any point in time

To open an SRS account, you must:

be at least 18 years old.

not be an undischarged bankrupt.

have the mental capacity to make own decisions

not have an existing SRS account (including suspended accounts) with the same or another operator

not have a pending application with another SRS operator.

not have a previous SRS account with all its funds withdrawn, on medical grounds or after reaching the prevailing retirement age (at time of your first contribution)

To qualify for SRS Tax Relief:

You must be a tax resident for the Year of Assessment following the year of SRS contribution

Your SRS account was not suspended on 31 Dec of the year of contribution

You did not withdraw, from your SRS account, the same amount as you contributed in the same year of contribution

Please note that the following conditions / criteria apply to SRS withdrawals:

SRS withdrawals can be made anytime. Withdrawals can be made in cash or in the form of investments

Withdrawals are subject to income tax (based on the time and circumstances of withdrawal) and will be considered part of your taxable income

Foreigners and Permanent Residents who make withdrawals are subject to withholding tax

Withdrawals made before retirement age will be subject to penalties unless they were made due to exceptional circumstances.

You may read up on the additional criteria and details on SRS withdrawals and the relevant taxes here.

title: Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS)
tagline: Provides tax relief to encourage individuals to save for retirement, over and above their CPF savings. Investment returns are tax-free before withdrawal and only 50% of withdrawals are taxable at retirement.
text:
To open an SRS account:

Choose 1 of 3 SRS operators (DBS Group Holdings Ltd, Overseas-Chinese Banking Corporation (OCBC) Ltd, United Overseas Bank (UOB) Ltd.) to set up an SRS account with

Tax relief will be provided automatically, if eligible

For foreigners:

You must complete the SRS Declaration Form

Note: You must update your SRS bank operator if you become a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, even if you have already contributed this year, as your maximum contribution amount will have to be recalculated and may be pro-rated.

Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents who have a verified disability status*;

Currently enrolled at an Institute of Higher Learning (IHL)# and

Applicants should have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:
a) Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

title: IHL Internship Programme
tagline: The SG Enable IHL Internship Programme matches students with disabilities to internships offered by inclusive organisations in various sectors based on their course of study, interests and preferences. text:
The SG Enable IHL Internship Programme matches students with disabilities to internships offered by inclusive organisations in various sectors based on their course of study, interests and preferences. Students will learn in a real work environment, build up their professional networks, and gain practical job-related knowledge and skills.

Matches students with disabilities to internships with inclusive employers
Job support provided during the internship
Opportunity to learn in real work environments and build professional networks

IHL students with disabilities will receive internship support services such as:

reviewing their resumes;

conducting internship search;

preparing them for interviews;

accompanying them for interviews (sitting in as observer);

advising potential employers on the student’s disabilities and support needs and;

providing job support (either virtual or in-person) during the internship.

Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents who have a verified disability status*;

Currently enrolled at an Institute of Higher Learning (IHL)# and

Applicants should have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:
a) Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment
b) Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye
c) Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear
d) Intellectual Disability
e) Autism

*You can check if your disability status is verified on SupportGoWhere by logging with your Singpass or contact us at IHL-to-work@sgenable.sg.

# IHLs refer to autonomous universities, polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education and other arts and private education institutions.

To register, applicants will need to:

Check if your disability status is verified via SupportGoWhere, or contact us at IHL-to-work@sgenable.sg.

Download and complete the Disability Verification Form (DVF). (To be completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional)
    - Note: Only applicants who do not have a verified disability status will be required to upload the DVF.

Upload the completed DVF as part of the online application on the IHL Internship Programme on SupportGoWhere.

SG Enable hotline: 1800-8585-885

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

title: Enabling Business Hub
tagline: Developed by SG Enable, Enabling Business Hubs (EBHs) bring training and employment opportunities closer to homes for persons with disabilities through customised job support and inclusive workplace practices.
text:
With an on-site Employment Support Team, persons with disabilities can look forward to a comprehensive suite of employment-related services such as vocational assessment, training, job-matching and post-placement job support. These services aim to better match persons with disabilities to jobs in the region based on their skills and interests and support them in employment. 

 

EBH@Jurong

Located beside Lakeside MRT, at 111A Yuan Ching Road, Singapore 619606, EBH@Jurong aims to bring training and employment opportunities closer to the homes of persons with disabilities living in the West, including those with higher work support needs.

Key Features of EBH@Jurong

Provide targeted training and support to prepare persons with disabilities for employment in the logistics sector.

Provide on-site employment support, including longer and more intensive employment support for persons with disabilities with higher work support needs and require a longer runway of support to work independently in the community.

title: Enabling Business Hub
tagline: Developed by SG Enable, Enabling Business Hubs (EBHs) bring training and employment opportunities closer to homes for persons with disabilities through customised job support and inclusive workplace practices.
text:
Applicants should be:

Aged 16 and above

Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents

Verified to have a permanent disability of any of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least one of the six Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye

Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear

Intellectual disability

Autism

Preferably living in the West or keen to work in the Logistics industry

title: Enabling Business Hub
tagline: Developed by SG Enable, Enabling Business Hubs (EBHs) bring training and employment opportunities closer to homes for persons with disabilities through customised job support and inclusive workplace practices.
text:
Prior to application, applicants are encouraged to check if your disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere. 

Preparation of documents 

The following supporting documents are required before making an application:

Disability Verification Form (DVF) (To be completed and endorsed by registered healthcare professional if disability status is unverified)

Copy of NRIC Front and Back

One Referral Form (For email/physical submissions only)

 

How to apply

Persons with disabilities seeking employment assistance may:

Apply online via SupportGoWhere, or

Download the One Referral Form (ORF) and email the supporting documents to EBH@Jurong at EBH_Jurong@bizlink.org.sg or;

Contact EBH_Jurong@bizlink.org.sg to schedule an appointment with the EBH@Jurong employment services team.

Persons with disabilities keen on other employment programmes, please email us at employment@sgenable.sg.

title: Matched MediSave Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
text: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).

title: Matched MediSave Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
text:
Dollar-for-dollar matching grant on cash top-ups to your MA of up to $1,000 per year
Build MA savings to help pay approved medical expenses and insurance premiums, e.g. for MediShield Life
Earn risk-free CPF interest rates of up to 6% per annum on cash top-ups and matching grant

title: Matched MediSave Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
text:
The Government will match every dollar of cash top-ups made to the MA of eligible beneficiaries, up to $1,000 per year, starting from January 2026, for a period of five years

The matching grant will automatically be credited to the MA of the eligible member at the beginning of the following year, i.e. from 2027

Eligible beneficiaries will enjoy interest rates of up to 6% per annum on the cash top-ups and matching grant

Cash top-ups that attract the Matched MediSave Scheme matching grant will not qualify for tax relief

title: Matched MediSave Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
text:
Singapore Citizen

Between 55 and 70 (inclusive) as of 31 December of the year

Does not own more than 1 property

Annual Value of residence not more than $21,000

Average monthly income not more than $4,000

MA balance less than half the current Basic Healthcare Sum

title: Matched MediSave Scheme
tagline: Helps eligible Singapore Citizens to strengthen MediSave adequacy by matching cash top-ups made to their MediSave Accounts (MA).
text:
No application is required

Eligibility is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year, starting from January 2026

If you are eligible, CPF Board will notify you via email or post

To receive the matching grant, you need to receive cash top-ups in your MA within the year that you are eligible for Matched MediSave Scheme

Eligibility is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year, starting from January 2026

If you are eligible, CPF Board will notify you via email or post

To receive the matching grant, you need to receive cash top-ups in your MA within the year that you are eligible for Matched MediSave Scheme

CPF hotline (within Singapore): 1800-227-1188 
CPF hotline (from overseas): +65-6227-1188 

Book an appointment to avoid waiting on the line.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

title: Live Well, Age Well Programme
tagline: The Live Well, Age Well programme is co-organised by HPB and PA to provide holistic activities to improve the physical, mental and social health of adults aged 50 and above.
text:
As we age, staying healthy and active is important so you can continue doing the activities you love.

The Live Well, Age Well programme features a wide range of FREE^ exciting activities in your neighbourhood to help you kickstart your healthy living journey.

Activities are specially designed based on the needs of adults aged 50 years old and above, so as to help you improve your physical, mental and social health to continue doing what you love.

These programmes are available at multiple locations near you, including Community Centres/Clubs, Residents’ Committee, Residents’ Network Centres, Senior Activity Centres. Some programmes (e.g. group exercise sessions and workshops) are also available online, so you can stay healthy in the comfort of your home.

^Except for subsidised health screening programmes under Live Assured.

title: Live Well, Age Well Programme

There may be additional criteria for selected programmes.

Visit Live Well, Age Well website for more information.

Step 2: Create profile if you are a new user. Log in to your existing profile with your NRIC and registered phone number if you are an existing user.

Step 3: Tap on "Explore" and select "Events" to view upcoming events.

Step 4: Search "Active Ageing" to filter for Live Well, Age Well programmes.

Step 5: Select the event you are interested in to view more details. Select "Book Now" to sign up.

You may also visit your nearest Community Clubs/Centres (CCs) or Residents’ Committees (RC)/Residents’ Network (RN) Centres for assistance.

title: Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession
tagline: Provides concessionary levy at $60 per month if there is an eligible person residing in the household.
text:
Concessionary rate of $60 per month, compared to standard levy of $300 for 1st MDW and $450 for subsequent MDWs
Each household is eligible for one MDW levy concession per eligible person, up to two MDW levy concessions per household

title: Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession
tagline: Provides concessionary levy at $60 per month if there is an eligible person residing in the household.
text:
Pay a lower levy of $60 per month if you live with an eligible person

Levy concession can only be granted for one MDW per eligible person and is capped at two levy concessions per household

title: Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession
tagline: Provides concessionary levy at $60 per month if there is an eligible person residing in the household.
text:
If you live with any of the following eligible person who is a Singapore Citizen:

Child below 16 years old

Elderly person who is at least 67 years old

Person with disabilities (PWD):

Must be certified by Singapore-registered doctor to require help with at least 1 activity of daily living (ADL), such as showering, feeding, dressing, toileting, transferring and walking or moving around

If you are a Singapore Citizen who lives with any of the following eligible person who is a Permanent Resident:

Parent, parent-in-law, grandparent or grandparent-in-law at least 67 years old

Parent, spouse, or child with disabilities

title: Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession
tagline: Provides concessionary levy at $60 per month if there is an eligible person residing in the household.
text:
No application is needed if:

You or your spouse is at least 67 years old

Your child is below 16 years old

It will automatically start once you meet the eligibility criteria and complete the required steps listed on MOM’s website.

If you were not automatically granted the levy concession, you can submit a form to check why.

title: MediFund
tagline: For Singaporeans who face difficulties with remaining medical bills after receiving government subsidies and other means of payment.
text:
For Singaporeans who face difficulties with remaining medical bills after receiving government subsidies and other means of payment.

Assistance with medical bills
Amount of assistance depends on the applicant's circumstances and bills incurred
Apply through Medical Social Workers at the institution where you received treatment

The actual amount of assistance you receive depends on you and your family members' financial, health and social circumstances, as well as the size of the medical bill incurred.

To apply for MediFund assistance, you must:

be a Singapore Citizen;

be a subsidised patient;

have received or require treatment from a MediFund-approved institution; and

be having difficulties paying for your healthcare bills after Government subsidies and other means including MediShield Life, MediSave, and cash.

Your application will be assessed holistically, taking into consideration you and your family’s financial, health and social circumstances.

You or your family can approach the Medical Social Workers at the MediFund-approved institution at which you had treatment

In addition to MediFund, they will also be able to explore other possible financing means with you

MediFund applications are administered directly by the MediFund-approved institution. You may wish to first contact the Medical Social Services Department or Business Office Department of the institution you are seeking care at for further assistance.

MOH Hotline: 6325 9220

title: MediSave
tagline: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
text: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.

title: MediSave
tagline: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
text:
Covers selected medical, treatment and hospital expenses, up to the approved limits
Covers for healthcare insurance premiums, rehabilitation and palliative care
Applicable at all MediSave accredited Public Healthcare Institutions (PHIs)

title: MediSave
tagline: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
text:
MediSave pays for a variety of medical expenses such as selected outpatient treatments, day surgery and hospitalisation expenses

It also covers the costs for your rehabilitation and palliative care

You can also tap on your MediSave to pay for health insurance premiums for yourself and your loved ones

title: MediSave
tagline: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
text:
You need to be a Singapore Citizen (SC) or Permanent Resident (PR)

Your spouse, children and parents can be of any nationality, except grandparents and siblings who must be SC or PR

title: MediSave
tagline: A personal healthcare savings account that helps you pay for you and loved ones' healthcare expenses over your lifetime, especially when you retire and no longer have regular income.
text:
To use your MediSave, inform the Public Healthcare Institutions (PHIs) or Medical Institutions (MIs) where you are receiving treatment at, and you would like to use your MediSave to pay for your medical expenses

If this is your first time applying, you will need to sign the Medical Claims Authorisation Form (MCAF)

If you had previously given a standing authorisation for the use of MediSave, the MI will submit a claim on your behalf

The PHI and/or MI will submit the medical claim on your behalf and payment from your healthcare plans will be made directly to them

Upon approval of the claim, the Board and/or insurer will notify you accordingly

title: MediShield Life
tagline: A basic health insurance plan managed by the CPF Board to help pay for large hospital bills and selected outpatient treatments.
text:
Provides lifetime coverage for hospital bills and selected outpatient treatments

MediShield Life coverage is sized for subsidised treatment in public hospitals and pegged at B2/C-type wards

If you choose to stay in an A/B1-type ward or in a private hospital, you are still covered by MediShield Life

However, your MediShield Life payout will cover only a proportion of your bill, and you would need to draw from MediSave and/or cash to pay the balance

title: Mobile Access for Seniors (MAS)
tagline: Provides subsidised smartphone and mobile plans to lower-income seniors who want to go digital but cannot afford them.
text:
For eligible seniors only:

Step 1: Attend our Seniors Go Digital programme at any SG Digital community hub^ and pick up at least 1 basic skill. Such skills include:

Digital communication

Navigating government digital services

Transacting with e-payments

Step 2: Collect the MAS redemption voucher from our Digital Ambassadors.

Step 3: Redeem the voucher at participating telcos.

Visit the Digital for Life website or call 6377 3800 to find your nearest hub.

Only 1 registration per senior is required.

^SG Digital community hubs are located at selected Community Clubs/Centres and public libraries. Visit the Digital for Life website or call 6377 3800 to find your nearest hub. As the hubs will be closed on public holidays, please check the Digital for Life website for updates on opening dates and hours.

title: Majulah Package – Earn and Save Bonus
tagline: Supplements eligible working seniors' retirement savings through an annual Earn and Save Bonus credited to their CPF account.
text:
Eligibility criteria

Born in 1973 or earlier

Singapore Citizen residing in Singapore

Employed, self-employed, and/or platform worker

Average monthly income in preceding Year of Assessment of $500 to $6,000

Annual Value of the place of residence (as indicated on NRIC) must not exceed $31,000, as at 30 November of the preceding year

Does not own more than 1 property

text: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.

title: MUIS Zakat Financial Assistance
tagline: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.
text:
Monthly financial assistance for eligible individuals
Support for emergency needs and burial expenses
Study grants and food vouchers available
Assessment based on Per Capita Income of $500 or lower for eligibility

title: MUIS Zakat Financial Assistance
tagline: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.
text:
Potential benefits include:  

Monthly financial support: Dependent on Per Capita Income (PCI) criteria 

Settlement of debt: One-time settlement of arrears for basic necessities (utility and conservancy charges)

Emergency assistance: Renders emergency assistance in times of crisis such as natural disasters, fire, or accidents

Burial expenses: Covers burial expenses incurred upon death of a Zakat beneficiary and their immediate family members

Study grants: Subsidies for national examinations mosque-based part-time Islamic education, and low-income students with special needs

Fidyah food vouchers: To purchase food supplies at approved Fidyah retailers islandwide

title: MUIS Zakat Financial Assistance
tagline: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.
text:
Available to Muslim Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents 

Per Capita Income (PCI) must be $500 or lower to qualify 

Applicants will undergo an assessment interview with a Social Development Officer (SDO) at the nearest Social Development (SD) mosque

Required documents: NRIC, birth certificate, income and bank documents, and other relevant supporting documents 

Outcome notified by mail within up to 6 weeks

title: MUIS Zakat Financial Assistance
tagline: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.
text:
Call a Social Development (SD) mosque closest to your home for an appointment. 

Schedule an assessment interview with an SDO. 

During the interview, please prepare to bring your personal identification documents, such as NRIC and birth certificate, income and bank documents (payslips, CPF statements, bank books etc.), and other relevant supporting documents (bills, medical memos, referral letters etc.) to help verify your application

title: MUIS Zakat Financial Assistance
tagline: Provides monthly financial assistance and support in other forms to eligible low-income Muslim households.
text:
MUIS Hotline: 6359 1199

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest SD mosque

title: MediSave Maternity Package

Claim up to $900 for pre-delivery medical expenses including pre-natal consultations, ultrasound scans, medications and tests

title: MediSave Maternity Package
tagline: Enables applicants to claim up to $3,670 for pre-delivery medical expenses and baby delivery expenses from MediSave.
text:
Submit pre-delivery medical bills to the hospital where the baby was delivered and inform the hospital that you would like to use your MediSave to pay for your expenses

The hospital will submit the MediSave claims to CPF Board on your behalf for both pre-delivery and delivery expenses

The claimable Medisave amount is capped by the total bill or withdrawal limit, whichever is lower

Provides the following benefits to support healthcare for Pioneers:

MediSave Top-Ups

	

Annual MediSave top-ups for life, ranging from $300 to $1,200 depending on birth cohort, to offset premiums for MediShield Life, ElderShield and CareShield Life. 

Additional MediSave top-ups of $50 to $200 will be disbursed from 2021 to 2025 for older Pioneers (i.e. those aged 82 and above in 2021) with serious pre-existing conditions who are paying additional premiums.

Additional Outpatient Care Subsidies 

	

Additional subsidies for outpatient care at polyclinics, public Specialist Outpatient Clinics and Community Health Assist Scheme clinics.

Special MediShield Life Premium Subsidies

	

Supports Pioneers in paying their MediShield Life premiums with special premium subsidies of up to 60%. 

Combined with the MediSave top-ups ($300 - $1,200), Pioneers born in 1934 or earlier will see their MediShield Life premiums fully covered, and Pioneers born from 1935 to 1949 will have about two-thirds of their MediShield Life premiums covered.

Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme 

	

Grants life-long cash assistance of $100 each month for those needing permanent assistance with at least 3 out of 6 of the following Activities of Daily Living:

Eating

Bathing

Dressing

Transferring

Toileting

Walking or moving around

[Incentives have ceased]
CareShield Life Additional Participation Incentives 

	

Provides participation incentives (totalling $3,000) to encourage Pioneers to join CareShield Life by 31 Dec 2024, in addition to any other premium subsidies seniors may receive.

If you would like more details on the above benefits as well as other useful information about this Package, please refer here.

title: Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR)

text:
Singapore tax residents who are married, divorced or widowed in the relevant year

Different qualifying conditions for your child will apply based on the following:

Children born or adopted on or after 1 Jan 2008

Children born or adopted before 1 Jan 2008

Please note that PTR is not available for your first child as well as from your fifth child onwards

Child is a Singapore Citizen at the time of birth/marriage/legal adoption or within 12 months thereafter

Additional conditions determining your eligibility are as follows:

Children given for adoption

If married is dissolved under a court order

If spouse passes away

For more information on the qualifying conditions, please visit IRAS’ PTR page

Applicants may use the PTR Eligibility Tool to check their eligibility

title: Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR)
tagline: Provides a one-off tax rebate of up to $20,000 per child to eligible parents to encourage them to have more children.
text:
For e-Filing:

If this is your first time claiming this relief:

Log in with Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Child/Parenthood Tax Rebate”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Yes” under “Claim one-time Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR) to indicate share of PTR as agreed by you and your Spouse"

Select “Update"

If you have claimed this relief before, any unutilised balance will be carried forward automatically to offset future tax payable

For paper filing:

Complete the particulars of your child in Appendix 2 of Form B1 & B if this is your first time claiming this relief

If you have claimed this relief before, any unutilised balance will be carried forward without further action

title: Change in HDB Flat Ownership After Flat Owner's Demise
tagline: Retain your HDB flat following the demise of joint owner or sole owner/tenant-in-common.

As the remaining family or single occupier, you can retain the existing flat provided you:

Are a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident who is at least 21 years old; and 

Satisfy the eligibility rules and conditions to own a flat.

title: Change in HDB Flat Ownership After Flat Owner's Demise
tagline: Retain your HDB flat following the demise of joint owner or sole owner/tenant-in-common.
text:
For demise of joint owner

Lodging a Notice of Death

	

For flats held under joint-tenancy, the remaining joint-owners can lodge the Notice of Death personally at SLA's FAQ ( How do I lodge a Notice of Death to inform SLA of the death of a joint owner), appoint your own solicitor to lodge the Notice of Death on your behalf or engage HDB’s services to lodge the Notice of Death.

If you wish to engage HDB’s services to handle the Notice of Death,  you must bring along these documents to lodge a Notice of Death:

Identity cards of remaining joint-owners 

Original death certificate or e-death certificate of deceased owner 

Title document (if any) 

Pay the registration and conveyancing fees at the point of application.

HDB’s Legal Group will then prepare the Notice of Death and inform the remaining joint-owners to sign the documents at the HDB Branch managing the flat. 

For demise of sole owner/tenant-in-common

Grant of Probate

	

If there is a will, the deceased's interest in the flat will be distributed according to the will. You will need the Grant of Probate. 

The deceased’s family should engage a private solicitor to apply for this court order, which gives legal authority to the Executor (the person named in the will) to manage the deceased's estate.

Grant of Letters of Administration

	

If there is no will, the deceased’s interest in the flat will be distributed according to the provisions of the Intestate Succession Act. You will need the Grant of Letters of Administration. 

The deceased’s family should engage a private solicitor to apply for this court order, which gives legal authority to the Administrator to manage the deceased’s estate.

Once the Grant of Letters of Administration or the Grant of Probate has been obtained, you can engage your own solicitors or appoint HDB's solicitors to act for you to register your legal right as the Administrator or Executor. You may also approach the HDB Branch managing the deceased’s flat to make the necessary application with the following documents: 

Original copy of the Grant of Letters of Administration and Statement for Grant of Letters of Administration or Grant of Probate and will 

Original copy of the Syariah Court Inheritance Certificate (for Muslim estates only) 

A copy of the deceased owner’s death certificate or e-death certificate 

Title document of the deceased’s flat  

Identity card(s) of all flat owner(s) and Administrator(s) or Executor(s)

If you are an Administrator/ Executor and engage HDB's services to act for you in the transaction, you will have to: 

Sign  the relevant documents 

Pay fees (stamp fees, conveyancing fees, etc.) 

Apply to HDB to effect an ownership change of the flat upon completion of the transaction either via a change of flat ownership to eligible beneficiaries or a resale of part-share, subject to the beneficiaries meeting the eligibility conditions.

title: Spouse Relief/Spouse Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $5,500 to both male and female taxpayers who have supported their spouses.
text:
For Spouse Relief:

You must fulfil all these conditions in the previous year:

Your spouse has been living with / supported by you

Your spouse did not have an annual income of more than $8,000

You have made maintenance payments under a Court Order or Deed of Separation (if you are legally separated from your spouse)

For Spouse Relief (Disability):

Dependant must be certified by a doctor to require the need for assistance with any of the 6 Activities of Daily Living:

Washing or Bathing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Transferring

Mobility

Dependant must be certified by a doctor to be impaired in any of these 3 areas:

Self-Care and Activities of Daily Living

Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment

Education or Work

No other claimants may claim the Parent Relief / Parent Relief (Disability) or Sibiling Relief (Disability) on the same dependant (your spouse)

title: National Service LifeSG Credits
tagline: $200 in LifeSG credits for all eligible past and present national servicemen to recognise their contributions to Singapore’s defence and security.
text:
$200 in LifeSG credits for all eligible past and present national servicemen, accessible via the ‘Your Benefits’ section of the LifeSG mobile application.

The credits are valid for 1 year from the date of disbursement. The validity period will not be extended.

Eligible national servicemen may check on the validity period of their credits through the LifeSG mobile application.

 

Where to use the NS LifeSG Credits:

The credits can be spent at online or physical merchants accepting payments via PayNow UEN QR or NETS QR.

As a show of support for National Service, several merchants are offering additional discounts and promotions when payments are made using the NS LifeSG Credits. For the list of participating merchants, please refer here.

Disbursements are subject to further eligibility checks.

Eligible national servicemen were notified by SMS and letter in 2024.

An SMS notification and notification letter were sent to eligible national servicemen after the credits were disbursed.

Credits have been automatically disbursed to eligible national servicemen in 2024
Valid for 1 year from the date of disbursement

$200 in LifeSG credits for all eligible past and present national servicemen, accessible via the ‘Your Benefits’ section of the LifeSG mobile application.

The credits are valid for 1 year from the date of disbursement. The validity period will not be extended.

Eligible national servicemen may check on the validity period of their credits through the LifeSG mobile application.

 

Where to use the NS LifeSG Credits:

The credits can be spent at online or physical merchants accepting payments via PayNow UEN QR or NETS QR.

As a show of support for National Service, several merchants are offering additional discounts and promotions when payments are made using the NS LifeSG Credits. For the list of participating merchants, please refer here.

Full-Time National Servicemen (NSFs) who have enlisted by 31 Dec 2024.

title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
text: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.

title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
text:
Up to 16 weeks of Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) for eligible working mothers
For the first and second child, the employer pays for the first 8 weeks, and the government reimburses the 9th to 16th week, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000
For the third and subsequent child, the government reimburses all 16 weeks, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $40,000

title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
text:
Up to 16 weeks of Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) for eligible working mothers.

For the first and second child, the employer pays for the first 8 weeks, and the government reimburses the 9th to 16th week, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000 per child order.

For the third and subsequent child, the government reimburses all 16 weeks, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $40,000 per child order.

Option to share up to 4 weeks of GPML with the spouse under the Government-Paid Shared Parental Leave scheme.

title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
text:
For live births:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Child is a Singapore Citizen at birth or within 12 months after birth

For employees: Served employer for at least 3 months before child's birth (GPML that is granted despite not meeting this criterion cannot be shared with your husband under the SPL scheme)

For self-employed: Engaged in work for at least 3 months before child's birth and lost income during maternity leave

For stillbirths: 
Similar conditions apply, with additional criteria:

The date of stillbirth is on or after 1 November 2021

You are a Singapore Citizen at the time of stillbirth or the child’s father is a Singapore Citizen and was lawfully married to the mother at some point between conception and stillbirth

title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
tagline: Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
text:
For employees:

Notify your employer at least one week before starting maternity leave.

Submit the declaration form (GPML1) to your employer with necessary documents at least one week before starting your Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML), or within one month from your child’s birth.

Follow your employer’s leave application process.

For self-employed:

Keep a record of your GPML dates

Submit your claim online via the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal after starting the government-paid portion of your GPML

Claims should be made within three months after the last day of your GPML

For employers:

Obtain and verify the declaration form (GPML1) from your employee

Keep the declaration form or records for five years from the last GPML date

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal after your employee has taken the government-paid portion of GPML

Claims should be made within three months after the last day of the employee’s GPML

text: CPF money of the deceased without a valid nomination is forwarded to the Public Trustee’s Office for administration

title: Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies
tagline: Distribution of the un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member in accordance with the relevant written law.
text:
The un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member can be distributed according to the applicable intestacy laws.  

If the beneficiaries include minors, the Public Trustee’s Office can hold the money in trust for them until they reach the maturity age of 21 years old.

title: Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies
tagline: Distribution of the un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member in accordance with the relevant written law.
text: Next-of-kin of deceased CPF member who wish to make a claim.

title: Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies
tagline: Distribution of the un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member in accordance with the relevant written law.
text:
Apply online via the “Deceased’s un-nominated CPF / Baby Bonus / Edusave / PSEA” option at Public Trustee's Office (PTO) E-Services using your Singpass.  

If you are ineligible to apply for a Singpass, you may apply for a login ID and password here. Select 'Login' and choose 'Register as Individual' from the dropdown menu.

Please refer to Annex A (for Muslims) and Annex B (for non-Muslims) for documents required. 

If the deceased was a Muslim, you will need to apply for the Certificate of inheritance via Syariah Court .
 

You may attach the required documents in your online submission, send via e-services or post to PTO at the following address: 
 
  Public Trustee’s Office 
  The URA Centre (East Wing) 
  45 Maxwell Road, #07-11 
  Singapore 069118 

Please indicate the case reference number and NRIC of the deceased on your supporting documents

You will be assigned a case officer after you have filed your online application. Please inform your case officer if you do not possess some of the required documents. Your case officer will advise you on what to do after you have submitted your online application.

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
tagline: Provides subsidies to Singapore Citizen seniors living in private properties, for the installation of senior-friendly fittings.
text:
EASE (Private) provides subsidies for the installation of senior-friendly fittings in private properties, to improve the mobility and safety of seniors within their homes. 

Eligible households will each receive $1,200 in vouchers that can be used to subsidise 75% of the cost of selected senior-friendly fittings purchased from appointed contractors. The household will only need to pay the remaining 25%. 

The seven senior-friendly fittings and their prices are as follows:  

 

	

Prices (after GST)

	

Prices after 75% subsidy

Single-Step Ramp 

	

$283.40 

	

$70.85 

Handrail 

	

$632.20 

	

$158.05 

Grab Bars  

First  set of 8 for combined toilet/bathroom or 10 pcs for separate toilet/bathroom 

	

$523.20 

	

$130.80 

Second set onwards 6 pcs 

	

$305.20 

	

$76.30 

Home Fire Alarm Device 

	

$119.90 

	

$29.98 

Bidet Spray 

	

$65.40 

	

$16.35 

Slip-Resistant Treatment to Existing Toilet Flooring* 

*The treatment is for size up to 3.5sqm. For non-standard bathroom sizes larger than 3.5 sqm, further assessment will be done during site survey by contractors.  

	

$239.80 

	

$59.95 

Shower Seat 

	

$305.20 

	

$76.30

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
tagline: Provides subsidies to Singapore Citizen seniors living in private properties, for the installation of senior-friendly fittings.
text:
EASE (Private) is available to private property households with at least one Singaporean household member aged:

65 years old and above; or  

Between 60 and 64 years old requiring assistance with at least one of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL)*

*ADL refers to daily self-care activities, including washing/bathing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility and transferring. 

Seniors aged 60 to 64 must submit a Functional Assessment Report endorsed by a qualified assessor, such as a doctor, registered nurse, or registered physiotherapist/occupational therapist.

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
tagline: Provides subsidies to Singapore Citizen seniors living in private properties, for the installation of senior-friendly fittings.
text:
Applications for EASE (Private) will be rolled out in phases (based on age of seniors in the household in 2026):  

When to Apply 

	

Who Can Apply 

From 1 Apr 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 80 and above 

Households with seniors aged 60 and above who require assistance with at least one of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL) 

From 1 Jul 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 70 and above 

From 1 Oct 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 65 and above 

Letters and SMSes will be progressively sent to eligible households to apply for EASE (Private). 

 

You can apply via BCA’s EASE (Private) Portal.

Seniors aged 60 to 64 who requires assistance for one or more of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL) must submit a Functional Assessment Report endorsed by a qualified assessor, such as a doctor, registered nurse or registered physiotherapist/occupational therapist.

title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
tagline: Provides subsidies to Singapore Citizen seniors living in private properties, for the installation of senior-friendly fittings.
text:
If you require assistance with the application, you may visit any Community Centre (CC) or ServiceSG Centre. You may also call BCA's hotline  1800-342-5222.

 

If you wish to obtain hard copy EASE (Private) vouchers, you may visit any Community Centre islandwide and they would assist you to print the vouchers.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

title: Staggered Downpayment Scheme
tagline: Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.
text: Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.

title: Staggered Downpayment Scheme
tagline: Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.
text:
Ease home purchase by splitting flat downpayment into two parts
Can pay in cash and / or CPF Ordinary Account (OA) savings

title: Staggered Downpayment Scheme
tagline: Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.
text:
Downpayment split into 2 instalments for easier financial management

First instalment is paid at the signing of the Agreement for Lease and is either 5% or 10% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan)

Second instalment is paid at key collection and is either 20% or 35% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan and the Loan-To-Value (LTV) limit)

Can be paid in cash and / or CPF OA savings (including CPF housing grant, if eligible), depending on loan type

For flat applications received from young couples eligible for deferred income assessment from the Jun 2024 sales exercise:

First instalment is capped at 2.5% of the purchase price

Second instalment is either 22.5% or 42.5% of the purchase price (depending on your choice of housing loan and the LTV limit)

Applicants who acquire a housing loan with a 75% or 55% LTV limit from a Financial Institution will have to pay at least 5% or 10% of the purchase price or value (whichever is lower) in cash before disbursement

title: Staggered Downpayment Scheme
tagline: Eases home purchase by allowing eligible first-timer couples and right-sizing flat owners to pay downpayment in 2 parts.
text:
First-timer couples, including those with 1 second-timer applicant

Valid HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter obtained on or before the younger applicant's 30th birthday

Booked an uncompleted 5-room or smaller flat in any HDB sales exercise

Flat owners right-sizing to a 3-room or smaller flat in non-mature estates who have not completed the sale of their existing flat at the time of HFE letter application and booked an uncompleted 3-room or smaller flat in a non-mature estate in any HDB sales exercise

title: Earned Income Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.
text: Provides tax relief of up to $12,000

title: Earned Income Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.
text:
Receive tax relief of up to $8,000, depending on your age

Persons with Disabilities (PWDs) are granted increased relief of up to $12,000

title: Earned Income Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.
text:
You are employed / self-employed and have received taxable earned income from your employment, pension or trade / business / profession / vocation in the previous year

To be considered eligible for higher relief, PWDs must have a permanent disability or mental impairment affecting work ability defined as follows:

Physical disability - requires assistance in any of the following 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Washing or Bathing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Transferring

Mobility

Mental disability - impaired in any of the following 3 activities:

Self Care and ADLs

Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment

Education or Work

title: Earned Income Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.
text:
The relief is granted automatically if you have taxable earned income

If you are claiming Earned Income Relief (Disability) for the first time, please email the following via myTax Mail:

Completed Tax Relief Form - Application for Claim of Disability - Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association, indicating details of the disabilities

Next, you must complete your Income Tax Return as follows:

If e-Filing, enter disability or mental impairment details under Section "4. Deductions, Reliefs and Parenthood Tax Rebate" > "Earned Income Relief"

If using paper filing, include handicap details in Appendix 2 of the Income Tax Form

Once claimed, the relief will be granted automatically in subsequent years if you are eligible

title: Earned Income Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.
text:
Provides tax relief for individuals who are gainfully employed or carrying on a trade, business, profession or vocation.

Provides tax relief of up to $12,000

Receive tax relief of up to $8,000, depending on your age

Persons with Disabilities (PWDs) are granted increased relief of up to $12,000

You are employed / self-employed and have received taxable earned income from your employment, pension or trade / business / profession / vocation in the previous year

To be considered eligible for higher relief, PWDs must have a permanent disability or mental impairment affecting work ability defined as follows:

Physical disability - requires assistance in any of the following 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Washing or Bathing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Transferring

Mobility

Mental disability - impaired in any of the following 3 activities:

Self Care and ADLs

Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment

Education or Work

The relief is granted automatically if you have taxable earned income

If you are claiming Earned Income Relief (Disability) for the first time, please email the following via myTax Mail:

Completed Tax Relief Form - Application for Claim of Disability - Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association, indicating details of the disabilities

Next, you must complete your Income Tax Return as follows:

If e-Filing, enter disability or mental impairment details under Section "4. Deductions, Reliefs and Parenthood Tax Rebate" > "Earned Income Relief"

If using paper filing, include handicap details in Appendix 2 of the Income Tax Form

Once claimed, the relief will be granted automatically in subsequent years if you are eligible

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch
