title: Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person
tagline: Enables executors or administrators to settle the deceased's taxes and outstanding debts using the estate's funds before distribution to beneficiaries.
text:
Executors or administrators of a deceased can act on the deceased’s following matters:

Filing personal income tax for the deceased on the income earned up to the date of death.

Filing estate or trust income tax for the deceased on the income. Estate income refers to the income generated by the deceased’s assets after date of death, during the administration of the estate. Trust income refers to the income from assets held in trust.

Settling the taxes and other bills such as loans, funeral expenses and medical bills from the estate funds of the deceased before distribution to deceased’s beneficiaries.

title: Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person
tagline: Enables executors or administrators to settle the deceased's taxes and outstanding debts using the estate's funds before distribution to beneficiaries.
text:
Executors or administrators of a deceased’s estate who are responsible for settling outstanding taxes, debts and liabilities. 

Remember, the executor or administrator must ensure all outstanding taxes, loans, funeral expenses, and medical bills are paid from the deceased's estate before distributing the remaining assets to the beneficiaries.

title: Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person
tagline: Enables executors or administrators to settle the deceased's taxes and outstanding debts using the estate's funds before distribution to beneficiaries.
text:
File personal income tax for the deceased

	

Provide IRAS with the following details via myTaxMail:

Name, address and identification number of the legal personal representative who is handling the affairs of the deceased;

Copy of the death certificate;

Copy of the Grant of Probate/Letter of Administration, if available;

Income details from all sources, up to the date of death (please specify the breakdown for each income source); and

Type and amount of tax relief available/applicable to reduce the tax burden.

File an Income Tax Return for Estate or Trust Income

	

Download and complete the Request Form for Form T from IRAS's website.

Email the completed form to trust@iras.gov.sg and IRAS will provide you with:

a Trust Reference Number

a copy of Form T to complete

Mail the completed Form T to this address: The Comptroller of Income Tax, 55 Newton Road, Singapore 307987.

title: Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS)
tagline: From 2025 to 2028, the Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS) scheme provides monthly cash payments to support eligible lower-income platform workers who make increased CPF Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions.
text:
From 2025 to 2028, the Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS) scheme provides monthly cash payments to support eligible lower-income platform workers who make increased CPF Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions.

Monthly cash payments from 2025 to 2028 to support lower-income platform workers who see an increase in their share of Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions
Lower-income platform workers who qualify for PCTS will automatically receive the payments; there is no need to apply
Payments credited directly to PayNow NRIC-linked bank accounts, registered bank accounts or via GovCash

Monthly cash payments from 2025 to 2028 to offset part of the year-on-year increase in the platform worker’s share of CPF Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account contributions for eligible lower-income platform workers.

An online PCTS calculator is available to estimate how much you can receive.

Singapore Citizen only

Born in 1995 or later, or born before 1995 and opted in to increase CPF contributions;

Net income from platform work (i.e. gross earnings minus the Fixed Expense Deduction Amount) and other employment sources does not exceed $3,000 per month;

Must have an increase in platform worker’s own share of CPF contributions to the Ordinary and Special/Retirement Account compared to the previous year

Assessment for PCTS is automatic, no application is required.

CPFB Hotline: 1800 227 1188

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: By appointment only

title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
tagline: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
text: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.

title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
tagline: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
text:
Professional deputies have to pass a certification course and a list of stringent criteria (e.g. no history of bankruptcy or criminal proceedings related to financial dishonesty) before they can be registered
The professional deputies have to re-register every 2 years with the Office of the Public Guardian to ensure they still meet the stringent criteria
The charges of the Professional Deputies vary depending on their professions and individual case requirements. Individuals may engage them to mutually agree on the fee charge

title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
tagline: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
text:
Professionals registered under this scheme include lawyers, doctors, accountants, allied health professionals, nurses, and social workers

Professional deputies apply to Court to act for individuals who lack mental capacity (known as 'P') on property &, affairs, and personal welfare matters

Professional deputies provide a (i) P-focused plan and a (ii) Schedule of Assets as part of the application

title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
tagline: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
text:
Persons who lack mental capacity (known as ‘P’) who do not have suitable family members or friends to be their proxy decision makers

Individuals with mental capacity can also choose to make a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA), and appoint a Professional Deputy to act as a Donee for renumeration

title: Professional Deputies and Donees Scheme
tagline: The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.
text:
The scheme serves individuals who may not have suitable family members or friends to appoint as their proxy decision makers. Professionals may be appointed as proxy decision makers for these individuals for remuneration.

Professional deputies have to pass a certification course and a list of stringent criteria (e.g. no history of bankruptcy or criminal proceedings related to financial dishonesty) before they can be registered
The professional deputies have to re-register every 2 years with the Office of the Public Guardian to ensure they still meet the stringent criteria
The charges of the Professional Deputies vary depending on their professions and individual case requirements. Individuals may engage them to mutually agree on the fee charge

Professionals registered under this scheme include lawyers, doctors, accountants, allied health professionals, nurses, and social workers

Professional deputies apply to Court to act for individuals who lack mental capacity (known as 'P') on property &, affairs, and personal welfare matters

Professional deputies provide a (i) P-focused plan and a (ii) Schedule of Assets as part of the application

Persons who lack mental capacity (known as ‘P’) who do not have suitable family members or friends to be their proxy decision makers

Individuals with mental capacity can also choose to make a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA), and appoint a Professional Deputy to act as a Donee for renumeration

Members of the public who are keen to appoint a professional deputy/donee can approach any of the individuals on this list of Registered Professional Deputies/Donees to enquire on their professional fees and to make an appointment.

MSF Consolidated Hotline: 1800-111-2222 
(To reach the Office of the Public Guardian, say "OPG" when prompted by the virtual assistant)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Family@Enabling Village 20 Lengkok Bahru, #04-02 Singapore 159053

title: NSman Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief to eligible operationally ready National Servicemen (NSmen) to recognise their contributions to National Service, as well as to the wives and parents of NSmen to recognise the support they give to their husbands or sons.
text:
NSman Self Relief of $1,500 to $5,000 for eligible NSmen

NSman Wife Relief of $750 for the wives of eligible NSmen

NSman Parent Relief of $750 for each parent of the eligible NSman

title: NSman Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief to eligible operationally ready National Servicemen (NSmen) to recognise their contributions to National Service, as well as to the wives and parents of NSmen to recognise the support they give to their husbands or sons.
text:
NSman Self Relief:

Completed full-time National Service under the Enlistment Act or deemed to have completed such service by the proper authority

Not a regular in the following organisations:

MINDEF

Singapore Police Force

Singapore Civil Defence Force

Central Narcotics Bureau

Singapore Prison Service

Have not committed disciplinary or criminal offences in the preceding work year

If you and your son are both NSmen, you will be eligible for either the NSman Self Relief or NSman Parent Relief, whichever is higher

NSman Wife Relief:

You are a Singapore Citizen in the previous year

Your husband is eligible for Nsman Self Relief

Widows of deceased NSmen may also be eligible unless they have re-married

NSman Parent Relief:

You are a Singapore Citizen in the previous year

Your child is eligible for NSman Self Relief

Your child was born to you and your spouse or ex-spouse / is your step-child / was legally adopted

Parents of deceased NSmen are also eligible

If you and your son are both NSmen, you will be eligible for either the NSman Self Relief or NSman Parent Relief, whichever is higher

The relief amount per parent is capped at $750, regardless of how many children are NSmen

title: NSman Relief
tagline: Provides tax relief to eligible operationally ready National Servicemen (NSmen) to recognise their contributions to National Service, as well as to the wives and parents of NSmen to recognise the support they give to their husbands or sons.

text:

title: Proximity Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $30,000 for families buying a resale flat to live with or near parents or children.

title: Proximity Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides up to $30,000 for families buying a resale flat to live with or near parents or children.
text:
Singapore Citizen applicants with at least 1 additional Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident included in the application

Aged 21 years or above

Have not received the Proximity Housing Grant (PHG) before

Applicants buying a resale flat to live with, or within 4km of, their parents or child

If parents/child are living with you, they must be included in the resale flat as co-applicants or essential occupiers

If your parents/child are living in a private residential property they do not own, it must be owner-occupied by immediate family members i.e. child, adopted child, parent(s) or parent(s)-in-law, or siblings

Parents buying over their child's flat will not be eligible for the PHG

The resale flat must have a remaining lease of more than 20 years

title: Proximity Housing Grant (Singles)
tagline: Provides up to $15,000 for eligible singles buying a resale flat to live with, or near, their parents or children.
text:
Provides up to $15,000 for eligible singles buying a resale flat to live with, or near, their parents or children.

Proximity Housing Grant (PHG) of $15,000 for singles living with parents or child
PHG of $10,000 for singles living near parents or child (within 4km)
If applicant is living near them, parents / child may be residing in either a HDB flat or private residential property to meet the PHG criteria

Receive a PHG of $15,000 if living with parents or child

Receive a PHG of $10,000 if living within 4km of parents or child

Singapore Citizen aged 35 or above, buying alone, with other single citizens or with their parents

Singapore Citizen aged 21 or above, buying with a non-resident spouse

The parents/child helping to qualify for the PHG must be Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident

Applicants buying a resale flat to live with, or within 4km of, their parents or child

If parents/child are living with you, they must be included in the resale flat as co-applicants or essential occupiers

If your parents/child are living in a private residential property they do not own, it must be owner-occupied by immediate family members i.e. child, adopted child, parent(s) or parent(s)-in-law, or siblings

The resale flat has a remaining lease of more than 20 years

Applicants must not have received the PHG before

Obtain a HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal

If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may apply for the grant when submitting your resale application

HDB Hotline: 6866-3066

Submit an enquiry

title: CareShield Life Premium Subsidies
tagline: To ensure that CareShield Life premiums are affordable, the Government has committed to providing several premium support measures, such as Premium Subsidies, Participation Incentives and Additional Participation Incentives for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation.
text:
Means-tested Premium Subsidies of up to 30% for eligible lower- to middle-income households

Transitional support will also be provided to all policyholders to help manage the premium increases from 2026 to 2029, following the CareShield Life 2025 Review

Participation Incentives for Singapore Citizens born in 1979 or earlier who joined and were covered under CareShield Life between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024: Up to $1,875 ($187.50/year)

Additional Participation Incentives for Pioneer and Merdeka Generation citizens who joined and were covered under CareShield Life between 1 January 2024 and 31 December 2024: $1,125 ($112.50/year)

Participation incentives and Additional Participation Incentives will be spread equally over 10 years and used to offset premiums payable for each year

title: CareShield Life Premium Subsidies
tagline: To ensure that CareShield Life premiums are affordable, the Government has committed to providing several premium support measures, such as Premium Subsidies, Participation Incentives and Additional Participation Incentives for Merdeka and Pioneer Generation.
text:
Eligibility for means-tested Premium Subsidies:

Subsidies of up to 30% for eligible Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents

Permanent Residents will receive half the subsidy rate applicable for Singapore Citizens

Household monthly income per person of $3,600 or below

Living in residences with an Annual Value (AV) of $31,000 and below

Not owning more than 1 property

As of 1 Jan 2025,

Household Income Tier

	

Household Monthly Income Per Person

	

Subsidy

Lower-Income

	

$0 - $1,500

	

30%

Lower-Middle-Income

	

> $1,501 - $2,600

	

25%

Upper-Middle-Income

	

> $2,601 - $3,600

	

20% 

Note: The above subsidy rates apply to those living in residences with an AV of $21,000 or less. Residences with an AV between $21,001 and $31,000 receive 10% less subsidies. Those who live in residences with an AV above $31,000 or own multiple properties will not receive these subsidies.

Eligibility for Participation Incentives:

For Singapore Citizens born in 1979 or earlier who joined and were covered under CareShield Life by 31 December 2024

Depending on birth year

Total Participation Incentives will be spread equally over 10 years and used to offset premiums payable for each year

Eligibility for Additional Participation Incentives:

For Merdeka and Pioneer Generation seniors who joined and were covered under CareShield Life by 31 December 2024

Provided on top of Participation Incentives

Total Participation Incentives provided will be spread equally over 10 years and used to offset premiums payable for each year

Eligibility for Transitional Support (CareShield Life 2025 Review)

All policyholders who see an increase in their gross premiums. Transitional support is directly provided to policyholders. Policyholders do not need to apply for this support.

Please click here for more information on Premium Subsidies, Participation Incentives and Additional Participation Incentives.

text:
Free programme
Access to bite-sized, self paced e-learning modules
Regular ‘Ask-Me-Anything’ online support group sessions to learn practical strategies and tips from experienced caregivers on top-of-mind topics e.g. handling tantrums and meltdowns, supporting siblings, money matters

title: Step One
tagline: Programme that equips caregivers of young children up to 7 years old diagnosed with developmental delay / disability with knowledge and skills to support their loved ones and themselves.
text: Step One programme helps you understand your child, find resources and learn strategies to help your child develop and grow and connects you with other caregivers for support. You can learn from experienced caregivers’ perspectives and be equipped with practical knowledge and skills, to ensure you are well-prepared and confident to start your caregiving journey.

title: Step One

text:
Programme that equips caregivers of young children up to 7 years old diagnosed with developmental delay / disability with knowledge and skills to support their loved ones and themselves.

Free programme
Access to bite-sized, self paced e-learning modules
Regular ‘Ask-Me-Anything’ online support group sessions to learn practical strategies and tips from experienced caregivers on top-of-mind topics e.g. handling tantrums and meltdowns, supporting siblings, money matters

Step One programme helps you understand your child, find resources and learn strategies to help your child develop and grow and connects you with other caregivers for support. You can learn from experienced caregivers’ perspectives and be equipped with practical knowledge and skills, to ensure you are well-prepared and confident to start your caregiving journey.

Caregivers of young children up to 7 years old with developmental needs or disability (defined as autism, intellectual disability, physical disability, hearing loss, visual impairment and excludes learning disorders).

Caregivers or child(ren) should be Singapore Citizens or Singapore Permanent Residents.

Sign up with CaringSG at https://caring.sg/stepone

title: 2025 Public Transport Voucher (PTV) Exercise
tagline: Provides lower-income households with PTVs to cope with the fare adjustment from 27 December 2025.
text:
Households that received a PTV under the 2024 PTV Exercise and continue to meet the income eligibility criterion would have received a digital or physical PTV notification letter (depending on indicated contact preference) by mid-January 2026.

Households that meet the income eligibility criterion but did not receive a PTV may apply online or in person at their local Community Centres/Clubs (CCs) from 20 January 2026. Households that missed the income eligibility criterion slightly can request a review of their eligibility through the same link or approach their local CCs for assistance.

For more information, you may visit this page.

21 years old and above

Singapore Citizen

Form a family nucleus with at least another SC or SPR. A family nucleus refers to one of the following:

You and your spouse

If single, you and your parents

If widowed/divorced, you and your children under your legal care and control

If orphaned, you and your siblings

No family support and no other housing options. HDB will assess this based on your individual circumstances such as your household income, household size and housing budget

Not existing HDB flat owners, essential occupiers, tenants under HDB’s rental schemes or private property owners

For families with children below 21 years old, your application comes under the ComLink+ Rental Scheme, which offers customised support from government agencies and community partners

title: New/Resale Flats (Singles)
tagline: Helps eligible singles decide if new or resale flats would be suitable for their consideration and provides them a holistic understanding of their housing and financing options for these flats.
text:
Details the criteria for eligible singles to purchase new and resale flats:

New flats are sold through public sales exercises e.g. Build-To-Order (BTO), or through open booking of flats

Resale flats refer to previously owned flats sold in the open market

Option to buy a 2-room Flexi flat or a resale flat of any flat type (excluding 3Gen flats) on the open market

Access to various housing grants for eligible singles

title: New/Resale Flats (Singles)
tagline: Helps eligible singles decide if new or resale flats would be suitable for their consideration and provides them a holistic understanding of their housing and financing options for these flats.
text:
The following criteria apply to both single applicants and singles collectively purchasing a flat:

All applicants must be Singapore Citizens

If applying jointly, there must be at least 1 other Singapore Citizen co-applicant

If anyone listed in the application owns or has an interest in a HDB flat, they must dispose of it within 6 months of the new flat purchase

If any core applicants or occupiers have previously received a housing subsidy, they are not eligible

No ownership or interest in any local or overseas private residential property, and no disposal of such property within the following time periods:

If buying a flat from HDB / buying a resale flat with CPF housing grant(s) / applying for a housing loan from HDB

	

Within the last 30 months

Buying a non-subsidised resale flat (including Proximity Housing Grant)

	

At least 15 months before 
HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application

In addition, the following criteria will apply based on flat type and whether you are applying individually or jointly:

Purchasing flats as a single applicant

Criteria Type

	

If buying a new flat

	

If buying a resale flat

Flat type

	

2-room Flexi flat in the non-mature estates

	

All flat types (excluding 3Gen flats) in any location.

Applicant age

	

At least 35 years old

	

If unmarried or divorced, 35 years old or above.
If widowed or an orphan, 21 years old or above.

Monthly household income ceiling

	

$7,000 for 2-room Flexi flat (99-year lease). The incomes of all persons listed in HFE application are considered.

	

Income ceiling does not affect flat application eligibility. It only affects eligibility for various housing grants and the HDB housing loan.

Housing subsidies received

	

If core applicants have previously received a housing subsidy, they are not eligible.

	

Previously received housing subsidies will not affect your eligibility to buy a resale flat. However, you will not be eligible for CPF housing grants (excluding the Proximity Housing Grant).

Additional amount payable

	

Additional amount of $15,000 incorporated into the price of the booked flat when buying from HDB.

	

None

Jointly purchasing flats with other single applicants

Criteria Type

	

If buying new flat

	

If buying a resale flat

Flat type

	

2-room Flexi flat in the non-mature estates

	

All flat types (excluding 3Gen flats) in any location.

Applicant age

	

At least 35 years old

	

If unmarried or divorced, 35 years old or above.
If widowed or an orphan, 21 years old or above.

Monthly household income ceiling

	

$7,000 for 2-room Flexi flat (99-year lease). The incomes of all persons listed in HFE application are considered.

	

Income ceiling does not affect flat application eligibility. It only affects eligibility for various housing grants and the HDB housing loan.

Housing subsidies received

	

If core applicants have previously received a housing subsidy, they are not eligible.

	

Previously received housing subsidies will not affect your eligibility to buy a resale flat. However, you will not be eligible for CPF housing grants (excluding the Proximity Housing Grant).

If buying a flat from HDB / buying a resale flat with CPF housing grant(s) / applying for a housing loan from HDB

	

Within the last 30 months

Buying a non-subsidised resale flat (including Proximity Housing Grant)

	

At least 15 months before 
HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application

In addition, the following criteria will apply based on flat type and whether you are applying individually or jointly:

Purchasing flats as a single applicant

Criteria Type

	

If buying a new flat

	

If buying a resale flat

Flat type

	

2-room Flexi flat in the non-mature estates

Income ceiling does not affect flat application eligibility. It only affects eligibility for various housing grants and the HDB housing loan.

title: Executive Condominiums (ECs)
tagline: Provides eligible households the opportunity to purchase an Executive Condominium (EC) from property developers.
text:
For fiancé or fiancée, married couples and/ or parent(s) with child(ren), orphaned siblings

Applicant must:

be a Singapore citizen

include at least 1 other Singapore citizen or Permanent Resident

be at least 21 years old

For 2 or more singles applying jointly

Both applicant must be:

Singapore citizen

at least 35 years old

Household core nucleus

Your household must comprise 1 of the following groups:

You and your fiancé or fiancée

You and your spouse, and child(ren)

You, your parents and siblings

If you are unmarried / widowed / divorced and buying an EC with parents, at least 1 of your parents must be a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

You and your child(ren) under your legal custody, care and control (for widowed / divorced persons)

If this responsibility is shared with your ex-spouse, you must obtain his / her written agreement before listing your child in an EC application

You and your siblings who are orphans and single i.e. unmarried, divorced or widowed, and you must fulfil the following criteria:

All your siblings are listed in the same application and are not applying for / owning / renting a flat separately

At least 1 of your deceased parents was a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

You and up to 3 other singles as core applicants

Monthly household income

Your monthly household income (considering total income of all persons listed in the EC application) must not exceed $16,000

Property ownership

You and the occupiers listed in the EC application must not own or have an interest in any local or overseas private property or disposed of any within the last 30 months

For EC land sales launched on or after 9 May 2023 (including open tenders), households may own 1 non-residential proper

Housing subsidies

If any core applicants / occupiers have previously received a housing subsidy, a resale levy may be payable

If you have received 2 housing subsidies, you will no longer be eligible to apply for, or be listed as, a core occupier in the EC application

If core applicants / occupiers own or have an interest in a HDB flat, they must dispose of it within 6 months of EC purchase completion

Undischarged bankrupt

Must obtain prior consent from the Official Assignee or private trustee to purchase EC unit

Consent is not required if you are an occupier of the unit

Wait-out period

When applying to buy an EC, the following specific wait-out periods apply:

Cancellation of HDB flat application after booking (1 year from date of cancellation)

Have previously bought an EC unit and subsequently terminated the Sale and Purchase Agreement (5 years from date of termination)

Currently own (or have disposed of) an EC unit (30 months from date of disposal)

Contact the property developer directly for details on the application process and required documents

title: Persons with Disabilities Concession Card (PWDCC)
tagline: Provides persons with disabilities with concessionary rates for travel on public transport.
text:
Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident

Aged 7 to below 60 years old

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

A hearing impairment: Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

title: Large Families Scheme
tagline: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.
text: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.

title: Large Families Scheme
tagline: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.
text:
Increased Child Development Account (CDA) First Step Grant to $10,000
Large Family MediSave Grant of $5,000
Large Family LifeSG Credits of $1,000 annually, during the years that the third and subsequent child turns 1 to 6 years old

title: Large Families Scheme
tagline: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.
text:
Families will receive additional support of up to $16,000 for each third and subsequent Singapore Citizen child, on top of existing Baby Bonus Scheme benefits.

 

 Child Development Account (CDA) First Step Grant

$10,000 CDA First Step Grant (increase from $5,000)

For each third and subsequent Singapore Citizen child born on or after 18 February 2025

Deposited into the eligible child's CDA within two weeks of CDA opening

Can be used to defray approved preschool and healthcare expenses of the child and/or his/her siblings

 

 Large Family MediSave Grant

$5,000 Large Family MediSave Grant

For each third and subsequent Singapore Citizen child born on or after 18 February 2025

Deposited into the mother's MediSave account, following child's Baby Bonus enrolment

Can be used to offset the mother’s pregnancy and delivery costs, or approved dependant's healthcare expenses. 

 

 Large Family LifeSG Credits

$1,000 annually in Large Family LifeSG Credits 

For each third and subsequent Singapore Citizen child, including those turning 1 to 6 years old in 2025 

Credits are disbursed to the CDA trustee of the child and accessible via LifeSG Mobile App 

Can be used at any merchant that accepts payments using PayNow UEN QR or NETS QR, to defray household expenses

title: Large Families Scheme
tagline: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.
text:
Families with a third or subsequent Singapore Citizen child born on or after 18 February 2025 will receive:

the increased CDA First Step Grant of $10,000, 

a $5,000 Large Family MediSave Grant, and

$1,000 annually in Large Family LifeSG Credits during the years that the child turns 1 to 6 years old*.

*The Large Family LifeSG Credits also applies to existing large families with at least one child aged six or below.

tagline: Provides more support for Singaporean families who have three or more children.
text: No application is required.

title: MediSave for Assisted Conception Procedures
tagline: Allows eligible couples to withdraw up to $15,000 per patient from MediSave to pay for Assisted Conception Procedures (ACP) that aid them in conceiving.
text:
Withdraw a total of $15,000 per patient to support conception
Available at both private and public Assisted Reproduction centres

title: MediSave for Assisted Conception Procedures
tagline: Allows eligible couples to withdraw up to $15,000 per patient from MediSave to pay for Assisted Conception Procedures (ACP) that aid them in conceiving.
text:
Covers procedures such as in-vitro fertilisation and intra-uterine insemination and is available for both private and public Assisted Reproduction centres

MediSave withdrawal limits vary accordingly for each ACP treatment cycle:

Up to $6,000 for the first ACP cycle

Up to $5,000 for the second ACP cycle

Up to $4,000 for the third and subsequent ACP cycles

The total lifetime MediSave withdrawal limit is $15,000 per patient

title: MediSave for Assisted Conception Procedures
tagline: Allows eligible couples to withdraw up to $15,000 per patient from MediSave to pay for Assisted Conception Procedures (ACP) that aid them in conceiving.
text:
Only the MediSave of the patient and her spouse can be used

If an ACP is not completed due to non-medical reasons, MediSave cannot be used to cover the costs of the incomplete cycle

title: MediSave for Assisted Conception Procedures

The centre will submit the MediSave claims to CPF Board on your behalf for the treatment expenses

Up to $6,000 for the first ACP cycle

Up to $5,000 for the second ACP cycle

Up to $4,000 for the third and subsequent ACP cycles

The total lifetime MediSave withdrawal limit is $15,000 per patient

Only the MediSave of the patient and her spouse can be used

If an ACP is not completed due to non-medical reasons, MediSave cannot be used to cover the costs of the incomplete cycle

Inform the Assisted Reproduction centre which you are receiving ACP treatment(s) at that you would like to use your MediSave to pay for your expenses

The centre will submit the MediSave claims to CPF Board on your behalf for the treatment expenses

CPF hotline: 1800-222-3399

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

title: Equipment Rental Scheme (ERS)
tagline: Provides subsidised rental of essential care equipment for patients with serious illnesses.
text:
The Equipment Rental Scheme (ERS) provides patients with serious illnesses who have a prognosis of less than a year with the following subsidies:

Weekly rental fees for approved equipment such as wheelchair/pushchair, hospital bed, oxygen concentrator, and more.

Up to two-way transport cost for delivery and collection of rented equipment

title: Equipment Rental Scheme (ERS)
tagline: Provides subsidised rental of essential care equipment for patients with serious illnesses.
text:
You must be a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident.

You are currently receiving care from a Palliative Care Provider (PCP)^; whether home-based or in a public healthcare institution (PHI).

You are assessed to have a serious illness with a prognosis of less than a year.

You have been assessed by a qualified assessor on the type(s) of equipment required.

You must not be receiving subsidies for the same or similar equipment through other Government assistance schemes.^^

^Palliative care providers support patients who are coping with serious illnesses and helps their families cope by reducing caregiving burden and improving their quality of life.

^^ If you have previously received Government assistance or subsidies for same or similar type of equipment prior to your ERS application, eligibility to rent equipment(s) under ERS will be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

title: Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL), for eligible working fathers.
text:
Child born/FIA/estimated delivery date before 1 Jan 2024:

Up to 2 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL) for fathers, to be taken within 12 months from the child's date of birth.

Government reimbursement for all 2 weeks of GPPL, capped at $2,500 per week, up to a total of $5,000.

Child born/FIA/estimated delivery date between 1 Jan 2024 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive):

Up to 4 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL) for fathers, to be taken within 12 months from the child's date of birth.

Government reimbursement for all 4 weeks of GPPL, capped at $2,500 per week, up to a total of $10,000.

Weeks 3-4 are additional, and employers can grant on a voluntary basis

Child born/FIA/estimated delivery date on or after 1 Apr 2025:

Up to 4 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL) for fathers, to be taken within 12 months from the child’s date of birth.

Government reimbursement for all 4 weeks of GPPL, capped at $2,500 per week, up to a total of $10,000.

title: Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL), for eligible working fathers.
text:
For natural fathers with live births:

Child born or estimated delivery date on or after January 1, 2017

Child is a Singapore Citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth

Lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and birth or within 12 months from birth

Served employer for at least 3 months before child's birth

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before child's birth

For natural fathers with stillborn children:

Stillbirth occurred on or after November 1, 2021

Lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and stillbirth or within 12 months from stillbirth, and either you or the child’s mother are Singapore citizens

Served employer for at least 3 months before the date of stillbirth

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before the date of stillbirth

For adoptive fathers:

Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date on or after January 1, 2017

Not the natural father of the child

If child is not a Singapore Citizen, either you or your wife must be a Singapore Citizen since the child's Dependant's Pass issuance

Served employer for at least 3 months before FIA date

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before FIA date

title: Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL), for eligible working fathers.
text:
For employees:

Notify your employer about your leave arrangement early.

Submit the declaration form (GPPL1) to your employer with all necessary supporting documents.

Apply for leave according to your employer's procedures.

For self-employed:

Record your leave dates.

Submit your claim online via the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal within 3 months after your leave.

Check your application status on the GPL Portal.

For employers:

Obtain and verify the declaration form (GPPL1) from your employee.

Keep the declaration form or records for 5 years from the last leave date.

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal within 3 months after your employee's leave.

Check your application status on the GPL Portal.

title: Family Care Scheme (FCS) (Joint Balloting)
tagline: Helps parents and their child(ren), married or single, to jointly apply for 2 units in the same Build-To-Order (BTO) project.
text:
Seeks to support families to live closer together in the same BTO project.

Parents and their child(ren), married or single, can make a joint application for 2 units in a BTO project that offers 2-room Flexi or 3-room flats.

Successful parent-child pairs will each receive a distinct queue position within the respective flat supplies.

HDB will schedule flat booking appointments based on the respective queue positions, which may take place separately.

*Relevant period is the calendar year or any 12-month relevant period with mutual agreement between employer and employee.

If you are self-employed or if there is no mutual agreement with your employer, the 12-month relevant period will be the calendar year by default (1 Jan to 31 Dec of the year).

tagline: Members with reduced life expectancy due to a medical condition, as certified by an accredited doctor (from public hospitals, polyclinics, or a specialist), may apply to withdraw some of their CPF savings. Severe medical conditions causing them to be permanently unfit for work or to lack mental capacity permanently can also be considered.

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings on Reduced Life Expectancy
tagline: Members with reduced life expectancy due to a medical condition, as certified by an accredited doctor (from public hospitals, polyclinics, or a specialist), may apply to withdraw some of their CPF savings. Severe medical conditions causing them to be permanently unfit for work or to lack mental capacity permanently can also be considered.
text:
Depending on the extent of your reduced life expectancy and your CPF balances, you may withdraw a lump sum of $5,000^ or more 

You may also need to set aside a reduced retirement sum in your Retirement Account (RA), from which you will receive monthly payouts

^ Excludes monies received under the Retirement Sum Topping-Up Scheme and Matched Retirement Savings Scheme, government grants and your MediSave Account savings.

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings on Reduced Life Expectancy
tagline: Members with reduced life expectancy due to a medical condition, as certified by an accredited doctor (from public hospitals, polyclinics, or a specialist), may apply to withdraw some of their CPF savings. Severe medical conditions causing them to be permanently unfit for work or to lack mental capacity permanently can also be considered.
text:
Singapore Citizen (SC) and Permanent Resident (PR) who have any of the following conditions as certified by an accredited doctor:

Severely reduced life expectancy due to medical condition

Permanently unfit for work

Permanently lack mental capacity to make decisions, as defined in the Mental Capacity Act

title: Withdrawal of CPF Savings on Reduced Life Expectancy
tagline: Members with reduced life expectancy due to a medical condition, as certified by an accredited doctor (from public hospitals, polyclinics, or a specialist), may apply to withdraw some of their CPF savings. Severe medical conditions causing them to be permanently unfit for work or to lack mental capacity permanently can also be considered.
text:
Applying for self

Submit an online application after logging in with your Singpass

Download the form “Medical Assessment Report” that is provided upon submission of the online application and have it completed by an accredited doctor. Thereafter, please request the medical institution to submit the completed medical assessment report to CPF Board directly (instructions are provided within the form). 

If you are mailing your documents from overseas, all documents must be witnessed/certified true by an official of a Singapore Overseas Mission with his official seal/stamp duly affixed.

Applying as a donee or deputy for your next-of-kin

Submit this form with a copy of your NRIC attached and provide the following original documents to CPF Board: 

Medical report from an accredited doctor stating that the member lacks mental capacity permanently; 

Lasting Power of Attorney or Court Order; and 

Statement for the bank account held on trust on behalf of the member. 

Make an appointment to visit any of our CPF Service Centres to submit the above original documents or mail them to: 

CPF Board 
Retirement Withdrawals Department (RWD) 
Privy Box No. 920651 

Singapore 929292

text: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible

title: Enabled Living Programme (ELP)
tagline: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible
text: Basic support (such as basic home maintenance, community engagement, and referral to other agencies for additional support as needed) to enable independent living and social engagement in the community

title: Enabled Living Programme (ELP)
tagline: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible
text:
The Enabled Living Programme (ELP) is a community living initiative that empowers adults with disabilities with low to moderate support needs to live independently for as long as possible.

 

Sited in public rental flats, ELP helps adults with disabilities age in place by providing an ecosystem of care and social support that includes on-site services – such as daily living and social skills coaching and community engagement.

 

By fostering community integration, ELP supports adults with disabilities to build meaningful connections, enhancing their overall well-being, autonomy, and dignity. 

 

The ELP sites and appointed service providers are listed on the Enabling Guide.

title: Enabled Living Programme (ELP)
tagline: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible
text:
Applicants should:

Be a Singapore Citizen​

Be aged 35 to 64

Have a permanent disability based on one or more the following (refer to the disability profile(s) that each ELP site serves):

Physical disability – Requires assistance with at least one of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)* due to physical impairment

Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye

Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear

Intellectual disability

Autism 

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form**  completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional. 

Not be enrolled in any government-funded adult disability hostel, adult disability residential home or day activity centres

Not have any caregivers who are available or capable of caring for them

Not have significant physical health, mental health or behavioural needs that are beyond what ELP can reasonably support

Fulfils Housing and Development Board (HDB)’s public rental flat criteria under the Joint Singles Scheme 

Applicants who do not meet the eligibility criteria may still be considered, subject to a case-by-case assessment.

Notes:

* The six ADLs are washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility and transferring.

** From 8 September 2025, the Disability Verification Form (DVF) is the standardised proof of disability required to verify an individual’s disability status when applying for selected MSF disability schemes and services and other selected Government disability schemes. Applicants can check if their disability status is already verified via their Touchpoints, SupportGoWhere, or call SG Enable at 1800-8585-885.

title: Enabled Living Programme (ELP)
tagline: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible
text:
Interested and eligible individuals can indicate their interest by submitting the online Interest Form to SG Enable. 

The relevant Service Provider will reach out to the applicants for assessment within 10 working days. Please note that submitting this form does not guarantee enrolment into the Enabled Living Programme.

title: Enabled Living Programme (ELP)
tagline: A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible
text:
A community living programme that empowers adults with disabilities to live independently in the community for as long as possible

Basic support (such as basic home maintenance, community engagement, and referral to other agencies for additional support as needed) to enable independent living and social engagement in the community

The Enabled Living Programme (ELP) is a community living initiative that empowers adults with disabilities with low to moderate support needs to live independently for as long as possible.

 

Sited in public rental flats, ELP helps adults with disabilities age in place by providing an ecosystem of care and social support that includes on-site services – such as daily living and social skills coaching and community engagement.

 

By fostering community integration, ELP supports adults with disabilities to build meaningful connections, enhancing their overall well-being, autonomy, and dignity. 

 

The ELP sites and appointed service providers are listed on the Enabling Guide.

Applicants should:

Be a Singapore Citizen​

Be aged 35 to 64

Have a permanent disability based on one or more the following (refer to the disability profile(s) that each ELP site serves):

Physical disability – Requires assistance with at least one of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)* due to physical impairment

Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye

Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear

Intellectual disability

Autism 

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form**  completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional. 

Not be enrolled in any government-funded adult disability hostel, adult disability residential home or day activity centres

Not have any caregivers who are available or capable of caring for them

Not have significant physical health, mental health or behavioural needs that are beyond what ELP can reasonably support

Fulfils Housing and Development Board (HDB)’s public rental flat criteria under the Joint Singles Scheme 

Applicants who do not meet the eligibility criteria may still be considered, subject to a case-by-case assessment.

Notes:

* The six ADLs are washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility and transferring.

** From 8 September 2025, the Disability Verification Form (DVF) is the standardised proof of disability required to verify an individual’s disability status when applying for selected MSF disability schemes and services and other selected Government disability schemes. Applicants can check if their disability status is already verified via their Touchpoints, SupportGoWhere, or call SG Enable at 1800-8585-885.

Interested and eligible individuals can indicate their interest by submitting the online Interest Form to SG Enable. 

The relevant Service Provider will reach out to the applicants for assessment within 10 working days. Please note that submitting this form does not guarantee enrolment into the Enabled Living Programme.

https://www.enablingguide.sg/elp-and-hsp

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Please also refer to the Enabling Guide for the contact details of the respective service providers for more information on the programme.

title: Student Care Fee Assistance (SCFA)
tagline: Provides fee assistance for children from lower-income working families enrolled in Student Care Centres (SCCs) registered with MSF.
text:
Child is a Singapore Citizen or a Permanent Resident (at least one immediate family member in the same household must be a Singapore Citizen)

Both you and your spouse are each working at least 56 hours a month

Child must attend a Student Care Centre registered with MSF

Monthly household income of $4,500 or less, or monthly household income per capita of $1,125 or less

 

How Income is Calculated

For salaried employees:
Based on average gross monthly income over 12 months from CPF records or the latest IRAS assessment (within the last 2 years).
Includes: basic salary, bonuses, commissions, overtime pay, allowances, cash rewards, and employee CPF contributions.

For self-employed individuals:
Based on average trade income over 12 months from the latest IRAS assessment (within the last 2 years), or income declared to CPF Board.

 

Note: Family members refer to the child’s grandparents or siblings below 21 years old who live at the same address, are not working or have no income, and are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents.

Your gross monthly income will be determined via HOMES as part of the means-test. 

For further information, please refer to MSF | Student Care Fee Assistance.

title: Student Care Fee Assistance (SCFA)
tagline: Provides fee assistance for children from lower-income working families enrolled in Student Care Centres (SCCs) registered with MSF.
text:
Apply online via SupportGoWhere. Please refer to this step-by-step guide for assistance with filling up the SupportGoWhere application form.

If you're unemployed or looking for work, you will need to provide supporting documents (for example, proof of job search, medical letter, or medical certificate). 

To make an appeal for your SCFA support, approach your MSF-registered SCC. Please note that appeals are subject to approval.

Singapore Citizens aged 40 and above as of 1 Jan 2024 received the top-up in May 2024
Singapore Citizens turning 40 years old after 2024 will receive their SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) top-up of S$4,000 in end-January of the same year

Singapore Citizens aged 40 and above as of 1 Jan 2024 received the SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) top-up of S$4,000 in May 2024. The credit will not expire. In addition, the outstanding balance from Additional SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career Support) top-up in 2020 is now merged with SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career).

Singapore Citizens turning 40 years old after 2024 will receive their SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) top-up of S$4,000 in end-January of the same year.

Can be used on top of existing SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) course subsidies to pay for selected courses which are oriented towards meeting industry and employment needs

Singapore Citizen

Aged 40 and above

Starting from 1 May 2024, the SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) top-up of $4,000, which does not expire, can further offset nett course fees for selected courses that have better employability outcomes.

Please refer to the following websites for more details on SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme and SkillsFuture Credit.

title: Mid-Career Pathways Programme (MCPP)
tagline: A full-time attachment programme for mature, mid-career individuals seeking a career switch. Applicants are attached to a host organisation for 4-6 months to gain industry-relevant skills and experience.
text:
Offers full-time attachment opportunities of between 4 to 6 months to support mature mid-career jobseekers in widening their professional networks and gaining meaningful industry-relevant skills and experience

Training Allowance

Mature mid-career trainees will receive a training allowance of up to $3,800 per month for the duration of the attachment or programme. The approved monthly training allowance may vary depending on the scope of the attachment proposed by the host organisation. The government co-funds the training allowance with the host organisation.

Monthly Training Allowance

	

$1,800 to $3,800

Government Funding

	

70% of the training allowance

title: Silver Housing Bonus (SHB)
tagline: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.
text: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.

title: Silver Housing Bonus (SHB)
tagline: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.
text:
Receive CPF monthly payouts for as long as they live, under CPF LIFE
Application must be submitted within 1 year from date of completion of the second housing transaction

title: Silver Housing Bonus (SHB)
tagline: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.
text:
Supplement your retirement income if you sell your current flat or private housing with an Annual Value not exceeding $21,000 and buy a 3-room or smaller HDB flat

Receive up to $30,000 cash bonus per household, when you top-up up to $60,000 of your proceeds^ into your CPF Retirement Account (RA)~ and join CPF LIFE

If the top-up is less than $60,000, the cash bonus will be pro-rated based on a 1:2 ratio (i.e. $1 bonus for every $2 top-up)

From 1 December 2025

The SHB will be extended to seniors who right-size from a private residential property with an Annual Value (AV) between $21,000 and $31,000.

Seniors who commit to a net increase of up to $60,000 in their CPF RA after right-sizing, with the sum going towards their retirement payouts, can qualify for SHB. They can do so using their CPF housing refunds, if sufficient, and may no longer need to make the top-up in cash.

The SHB will be enhanced to provide a cash bonus of up to $40,000. You can qualify for an additional $10,000 cash bonus if you right-size to a 2-room or smaller flat, including Community Care Apartments. The additional cash bonus of $10,000 will apply regardless of the amount that you commit to your CPF retirement payouts, i.e. there will be no pro-ration based on the amount committed. Including this additional $10,000 cash bonus, the maximum SHB amount that eligible seniors may receive will be $40,000.

^ Proceeds refer to selling price of the current property minus the sum of any outstanding loan on the current property, the purchase price of the next flat, resale levy payable (if applicable), and  subsidy recovery (if applicable) for Plus and Prime flats.

~ If your CPF RA balance is already at the prevailing Full Retirement Sum (FRS), you will not be able to join SHB.

title: Silver Housing Bonus (SHB)
tagline: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.
text:
At least 1 owner must be a Singapore Citizen

Singapore Citizen owner is aged 55 or above

Monthly household income not more than $14,000

Existing property is an HDB flat that fulfils minimum occupation period for resale, or private housing with an Annual Value of $21,000 or less^

No concurrent ownership of a second property

Other conditions:

Next property you are buying is 3-room (excluding 3-room terrace) or smaller flat from HDB or from the resale market

Purchase price does not exceed selling price of the current/ last sold property

Booking of new HDB flat or application to buy resale flat must either be before sale of existing property or within 12 months of completing sale of existing property

SHB application must be submitted within 1 year from date of completion of the second housing transaction (either the purchase or sale)

^With effect from 1 December 2025, the SHB will be extended to seniors who right-size from a private residential property with an Annual Value (AV) between $21,000 and $31,000.

title: Silver Housing Bonus (SHB)
tagline: Seniors aged 55 or above may receive cash bonus when they right-size to a 3-room or smaller HDB flat, and top-up the proceeds from the sale of their existing property into their CPF Retirement Account (RA) and join CPF LIFE.
text:
Learn about your eligibility for this scheme (and other housing monetisation options) and the estimated monthly payouts you can receive via HDB’s 'Check Housing Monetisation Option' e-Service

To apply:

Enquire with our Customer Relations Executive during the appointment for second housing transaction (either resale completion or new flat keys collection appointment)

HDB will then schedule a financial counselling session where all the details and information such as the CPF top-up amount, bonus and the monthly payout amount would be shared during the session

65 years old and above

title: SkillsFuture Credit
tagline: For Singapore Citizens aged 25 and above to pay for or offset the out-of-pocket course fees of approved courses.
text:
Singapore Citizens aged 25 and above will receive an opening credit of S$500. Opening credits will not expire.

A one-off SkillsFuture Credit top-up of $500 was provided to every Singapore Citizen aged 25 and above as at 31 December 2020. These credits will expire on 31 December 2025.

Can be used on top of existing government course subsidies to pay for a wide range of approved skills-related courses, such as:

Courses subsidised or approved by SkillsFuture Singapore including online courses

Selected courses offered by Ministry of Education (MOE)-funded institutions

Courses supported by lead agencies

SkillsFuture@PA courses offered by the People’s Association and selected courses under National Silver Academy

How to use your SkillsFuture Credit:

Step 1: Inform your Training Provider that you intend to use your SkillsFuture Credit in part or in full for the qualifying course fees at the point of course registration

Step 2: Submit your claim via MySkillsFuture within 60 days before the course start date (date inclusive)

title: Short-lease 2-room Flexi Flats
tagline: Provides seniors aged 55 and above with the option to purchase a 2-room Flexi flat.
text:
Citizenship

	

You must be a Singapore Citizen and your co-applicants' / occupiers’ citizenships are as follows:

Your fiancé / fiancée or spouse must be a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

At least one of your parent(s) or child(ren) must be a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Your siblings, if they are orphans and unmarried /widowed/divorced are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents

Your non-resident spouse must be included as a core occupier

Up to 4 unrelated singles who are unmarried/widowed/divorced must all be Singapore Citizens

Age

	

All buyers and their spouses must be at least 55 years old at the time of HDB Flat Eligibility Letter (HFE) application.

Remaining lease of flat

	

The chosen lease of your Flexi Flat must be sufficient to cover all buyers (and their spouses) until age 95 at least (computed at the time of HFE letter application).

Monthly Household income ceiling

	

Monthly household income ceiling of $14,000

Property ownership / Ownership interest in HDB flat

	

All persons listed in the HFE letter application who own or have an interest in any local or overseas private residential property/HDB flat must terminate ownership within 6 months of completing the flat purchase. Disposal is not required if only one non-residential property is owned.

Previous housing subsidies

	

You must not have previously benefited from two or more subsidized housing options, including a Studio Apartment, short-lease 2-room Flexi flat, or Community Care Apartment.

Note: Additional fees may be payable depending on your income, household type and whether you have previously received any housing subsidies. Please visit HDB Website for more information.

title: Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund
tagline: Provides subsidies to seniors for mobility and assistive devices or home healthcare items.
text:
Up to 90% subsidy for mobility and assistive devices (e.g. wheelchairs, hearing aids)
Up to 80% subsidies for home healthcare items (e.g. milk feeds, adult diapers)
Approach your therapist or AIC directly to apply

title: Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund
tagline: Provides subsidies to seniors for mobility and assistive devices or home healthcare items.
text:
To support ageing in place, you may apply for the following subsidies for seniors who live at home:

Subsidies of up to 90% for approved mobility and assistive devices (e.g. mobility aids such as walking/quad stick, wheelchair, geriatric chair, commodes, pressure relief mattress/cushions, hospital beds, spectacles, hearing aids, etc.)

Subsidies of up to 80% for approved home healthcare items (e.g. milk feeds, diapers, etc.)

title: Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund
tagline: Provides subsidies to seniors for mobility and assistive devices or home healthcare items.
text:
60 years old and above

Singapore Citizen or PR

Monthly household income per capita of less than or equal to $4,800 (or property annual value of $21,000 or below for households with no income)

Pass a means-test for Intermediate and Long-Term Care (ILTC) services

Seniors and persons with disabilities will be served under one scheme and agency throughout their lifetime

If the individual has tapped on Assistive Technology Fund (ATF)^ before the age of 60, they will continue to be served by ATF throughout their lifetime and not SMF

If the individual has not tapped on ATF subsidies before the age of 60, they would be served by SMF throughout their lifetime

Individuals can approach their care provider, e.g. hospitals, community care organisations, social service agencies, to enquire about their eligibility for the schemes

They may also approach the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) if they are not currently receiving support from any care provider

^ATF provides subsidies for persons with disabilities (PWDs) to purchase the assistive technology devices to enable independent living

Additional criteria for assistive devices:

Assessed by a qualified assessor to require the device

Must not reside in a nursing home or sheltered home

Additional criteria for motorised devices:

Must not have a full-time caregiver or domestic worker

Unable to use a manual wheelchair independently

Assessed to be mentally and physically able to safely use the motorised device

Additional criteria for home healthcare items:

Must not be a current recipient of subsidies for the same items

Must be receiving Home Nursing, Home Medical, Home Palliative Care, or Day Hospice services

title: Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund
tagline: Provides subsidies to seniors for mobility and assistive devices or home healthcare items.
text:
You can apply:

via the therapist or social worker at the hospital, day rehabilitation centre, or senior care centre from which your loved one is receiving care

online using Singpass or without Singpass

By mailing the completed application form for assistive device or mobility device and supporting documents to Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund (SMF), No. 7 Maxwell Road #04-01, MND Complex Annex B, Singapore 069111

You'll need to provide:

Copy of Applicant's NRIC (front and back) or a copy of the digital NRIC available on SingPass

For first time application for motorised device, doctor memo or Need for Assessment form indicating need for motorised device must be submitted.

Copies of medical reports, if available

Copies of financial assistance documents, if available

title: Special Needs Savings Scheme (SNSS)
tagline: Enables parents to set aside CPF savings for the long-term care of children with special needs.
text:
Parents may nominate child to receive payouts from their CPF savings after they pass on
Minimum monthly payout is $250, parents may decide on a higher amount
Submit the application and supporting documents to SNTC via email or mail

title: Special Needs Savings Scheme (SNSS)
tagline: Enables parents to set aside CPF savings for the long-term care of children with special needs.
text:
Parents may nominate child with special needs to receive payouts from their CPF savings after death with minimum monthly payout of $250.

If the nominating parent’s CPF savings upon his/her demise is insufficient to support a year’s worth of payout (e.g. a minimum monthly payout is S$250 x 12 months = S$3,000), the CPF savings will be disbursed as a lump sum to the nominee with special needs.

title: Special Needs Savings Scheme (SNSS)
tagline: Enables parents to set aside CPF savings for the long-term care of children with special needs.
text:
The nominating applicant should be:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

The parent or an appointed legal guardian of the child with special needs

The child with special needs should:

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

A hearing impairment: Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Or

is attending or had attended a Special Education School

The 6 ADLs are:

Eating

Bathing

Dressing

Transferring

Toileting

Walking or moving around

title: Special Needs Savings Scheme (SNSS)
tagline: Enables parents to set aside CPF savings for the long-term care of children with special needs.
text:
Before proceeding, all applicants are encouraged to check if your child’s disability status has already been verified.

Find out more here: https://www.enablingguide.sg/disability-verification

You can apply by submitting the completed application form and supporting documents:

via mail to Special Needs Savings Scheme (SNSS), Future Care Planning Resource Centre by SG Enable, 22 Lengkok Bahru, Vista, Enabling Village #03-03  Singapore 159824

via email to futurecareplanning@sgenable.sg.

You'll need to provide copies of:

Nominating applicant's and child's NRIC

Child's Birth Certificate

If your child's disability status has not been verified:

Supporting document from the SPED school issued on the school’s official letterhead stating that the Person with Special Needs (PSN) is currently attending or has previously attended the SPED school or

Disability Verification Form (DVF) completed by a Registered Healthcare Professional*

After mailing the application and upon approval by MSF, parents will receive an eligibility letter, which they should bring to the CPF Board to make the SNSS nomination.

*Please note that a submitted Disability Verification Form (DVF) does not mean that your child’s disability status has been verified.

title: Step-Up CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides a $15,000 Step-Up CPF Housing Grant for eligible second-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat.
text:
Grant of $15,000 for eligible second-timer applicants purchasing a new or resale flat

The Grant can be used to offset the flat’s purchase price or reduce the housing loan needed

title: Step-Up CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides a $15,000 Step-Up CPF Housing Grant for eligible second-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat.
text:
Applicants forming a core family nucleus, consisting of the core applicant(s) and occupier(s)

Second-timer applicants (married couples or families) with 1 housing subsidy who are either:

Currently living in a 2-room flat in a non-mature estate (Bought from HDB after October 1995 / on the open market with a CPF housing grant) and applying for a 3-room flat from HDB or on the open market (non-mature estate)

Currently living in a public rental flat and applying for 2- to 3-room flats from HDB or on the open market (non-mature estates)

Have worked continuously for at least 12 months 2 months before HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application and were employed at the time of the HFE letter application

Average gross monthly household income not exceeding $7,000

No ownership or interest in any private residential property, local or overseas, in the last 30 months

All persons listed in the HFE letter application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property at HFE application and up to 30 months before

For resale flats, the remaining lease must be more than 20 years and be sufficient to cover the youngest core applicant and / or core occupier to the age of 95

title: Step-Up CPF Housing Grant (Families)
tagline: Provides a $15,000 Step-Up CPF Housing Grant for eligible second-timer applicants buying a new or resale flat.
text:
Apply for the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal to check your eligibility for this Grant and others

If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may apply for the grant when submitting your resale application or after you have booked a flat from HDB

The Step-Up CPF Housing Grant will be credited to the CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible household members

Only core applicants can use their share of the housing grants to pay for the flat purchase

title: Tax Reliefs
tagline: Provides tax reliefs to taxpayers who support family members in need, inclusive of dependants with disabilities.
text:
Grandparent Caregiver Relief (GCR) 

Provides tax relief of up to $3,000 to working mothers who engage their parents, grandparents or in-laws as caregivers for their children. 

Parent Relief/ Parent Relief (Disability) 

Provides tax relief of up to $14,000 per dependant for individuals supporting their parents, grandparents or in-laws, with additional relief for dependants with disabilities.  

Sibling Relief (Disability) 

Provides tax relief of up to $5,500 per dependant to individuals supporting their siblings or siblings-in-law with disabilities.

 Spouse/Spouse Relief (Disability) 

Provides tax relief of up to $5,500 to individuals who support their spouse, including those with disabilities.

title: Tax Reliefs
tagline: Provides tax reliefs to taxpayers who support family members in need, inclusive of dependants with disabilities.
text:
Working mothers (married, divorced, or widowed) with a Singaporean child under 12/unmarried child with disabilities and caregiver/s that resides in Singapore 

Taxpayers supporting dependant/s aged 55 and above, including those with disabilities, living in their household.

Taxpayers supporting sibling/s with a disability/mental impairment in the same household. 

Taxpayers supporting a spouse with an annual income under $4,000, including those with disabilities.

title: Parent Relief/Parent Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief of up to $14,000 per dependant, to recognise individuals supporting their parents, grandparents, parents-in-law or grandparents-in-law in Singapore.
text:
For e-Filing

Log in with your Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Go to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Parent”

Click “Update” and enter your claim

For paper filing

Complete the item on "Parent/Parent Relief (Disability)" on Form B1 & B

Complete the particulars of your dependant(s) in Appendix 2

title: Third Child Priority Scheme (TCPS)
tagline: Provides priority in flat allocation to eligible families with 3 or more children.
text:
Up to 10% of Build-To-Order (BTO) flats, and up to 10% of Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) flats allocated for eligible families with 3 or more children
Provides an additional ballot chance to secure a flat

title: Third Child Priority Scheme (TCPS)
tagline: Provides priority in flat allocation to eligible families with 3 or more children.
text:
Up to 10% of BTO flats, and up to 10% of SBF flats allocated for eligible families

Parent(s) expecting their third child can apply for a flat under the TCPS subject to meeting all other eligibility conditions

title: Third Child Priority Scheme (TCPS)
tagline: Provides priority in flat allocation to eligible families with 3 or more children.
text:
You and/or your spouse are Singapore Citizens

If you are divorced or widowed, you must be a Singapore Citizen to qualify

Your family must consist of at least 3 children or be expecting a third child who are from a legal marriage or legally adopted

If the family is expecting*, you must have a doctor’s certificate confirming the pregnancy

Divorced parents must have legal custody, care, and control of their children

Adoptive parents must provide the Adoption Order when applying for a flat

Your third child must be born on or after 1 January 1987

Your youngest child or the child you are expecting must be a Singapore Citizen

Your other children must be either Singapore Citizens or Singapore Permanent Residents

You have not previously bought a flat under the TCPS

*This includes cases where the parent(s) have 1 child and are expecting 2 or more children, or where the parent(s) do not have a child yet and are expecting 3 or more children.

title: Third Child Priority Scheme (TCPS)
tagline: Provides priority in flat allocation to eligible families with 3 or more children.
text:
Obtain a HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal

If you meet the eligibility criteria during your HFE letter application, you may apply for this scheme in your flat application

title: MyCareersFuture
tagline: The one-stop portal for job search and application needs. text:
Advanced Matching Algorithm: Our latest Job-to-Skills job matching algorithm connects you with opportunities tailored to your skills and experience.

CareersFinder by MyCareersFuture: CareersFinder harnesses Government data and artificial intelligence to help individuals discover personalised upskilling and career recommendations based on their unique profile to achieve their career goals.

Government Support Eligibility: Filter jobs eligible for Government support such as: 

Career Conversion Programmes (CCP) if you are open to reskill and change career/occupations

GRaduate Industry and Traineeship Programme (GRIT) if you are a fresh graduate looking for a traineeship opportunity, and

Mid-Career Pathways Programmes (MCPP) if you are a mature individual looking for a mid-career attachments. 

CareersCompass by MyCareersFuture: Access curated articles, career guides and expert insights to help you navigate career planning, skills development and job search strategies.

CareersHorizon by MyCareersFuture: A one-stop digital platform for career-related event registrations and management. It enables Singaporeans to access a comprehensive suite of career resources, including online articles, virtual career events, seminars, and workshops. The platform also offers micro-learning content, such as podcasts, covering topics relevant to all career stages. Additionally, it supports Singaporeans in searching and registering for career events, and it also facilitates networking opportunities between employers and individuals.
