CareersHorizon by MyCareersFuture: A one-stop digital platform for career-related event registrations and management. It enables Singaporeans to access a comprehensive suite of career resources, including online articles, virtual career events, seminars, and workshops. The platform also offers micro-learning content, such as podcasts, covering topics relevant to all career stages. Additionally, it supports Singaporeans in searching and registering for career events, and it also facilitates networking opportunities between employers and individuals.

Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents who are looking to plan their careers, make career transitions, or search for jobs.

Embark on your journey to professional success with MyCareersFuture.

WSG Hotline: 6883 5885

text: For persons with permanent disabilities who are medically certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel to school, work or SG Enable-supported training.

title: Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS)
tagline: For persons with permanent disabilities who are medically certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel to school, work or SG Enable-supported training.
text:
Up to 80% means-tested subsidy for taxi travel for the purpose of school, work or employment-related training supported by SG Enable
From July 2026, additional 15% subsidies for Singapore Citizens born in and before 1969
Application will be processed within 15 working days upon complete document submission

title: Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS)
tagline: For persons with permanent disabilities who are medically certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel to school, work or SG Enable-supported training.
text:
Persons with disabilities can apply for the means-tested subsidy for taxi travel for the purpose of school, work or employment-related training supported by SG Enable. The approved subsidy percentage* applies to the total fare for each trip (which covers additional charges such as booking fees, peak period surcharges and ERP charges).

The subsidy amounts will depend on:

Whether applicants are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents

Per capita monthly household income (PCHI)

* subjected to means testing and meeting eligibility criteria

title: Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS)
tagline: For persons with permanent disabilities who are medically certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel to school, work or SG Enable-supported training.
text:
Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel

Currently schooling, working, or attending employment-related training supported by SG Enable

Monthly Per Capita Household Income (PCHI) of $3,600 and below

Annual Value (AV) of residence reflected on NRIC of $21,000 and below for households with no income

Currently not owning any motor vehicles

From July 2026, individuals with monthly per capita household income of $3,601 to $4,800 will also qualify for TSS.

text: Claim up to $5,500 per dependant

title: Sibling Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to individuals supporting their siblings and siblings-in-law with disability.
text: Relief of $5,500 granted to individual for each sibling or sibling-in-law supported.

title: Sibling Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to individuals supporting their siblings and siblings-in-law with disability.
text:
To claim Sibling Relief (Disability), all of the following conditions must be met:

Dependant must have disability^ or mental impairment^^

Dependant lived / has been living with you (on a permanent basis) in the same household in Singapore

If they live in a separate household, you must have incurred $2,000 or more in supporting them

No other type of relief has been claimed on the same dependant by someone else e.g. another family member

If multiple claimants are supporting the same sibling or sibling-in-law and claim the Handicapped Brother / Sister Relief for them, it will be shared among the claimants

^ Certified by a doctor to require assistance in any of the 6 Activities of Daily Living (Washing or Bathing, Dressing, Feeding, Toileting, Transferring, Mobility).

^^ Certified by a doctor to be impaired in any of these 3 areas: Self Care and Activities of Daily Living, Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment, Education or Work.

title: Sibling Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to individuals supporting their siblings and siblings-in-law with disability.
text:
If you were previously approved for relief last year, the amount will be pre-filled and granted to you automatically. If you need to make changes to your relief, please refer to the below instructions for first-time applicants.

For e-Filing

If this is your first time claiming this relief:

Log in with your Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select“File" to File Income Tax Return

Go to ““4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select "Add New" > “Sibling”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Update”

Email IRAS through myTax Mail under myTaxPortal via Singpass login:

Completed Application for Claim of Disability Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association / Special Education school in respect of your sibling, indicating details of their disabilities

For paper filing

Complete the item on “Sibling Relief (Disability)” in Form B1 & B

If this is your first time claiming the relief:

Fill in the details of your sibling/sibling-in-law in Appendix 2

Email IRAS through myTax Mail under myTaxPortal via Singpass login:

Completed Application for Claim of Disability Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association / Special Education school in respect of your sibling, indicating details of their disabilities

If you were eligible for the relief but no longer qualify, please withdraw the claim from your Income Tax Return if the relief has been auto-included.

tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
3 to 6-month traineeships in Public Service for fresh graduates
Enables fresh graduates to gain useful skills and experience through short-term traineeships
Trainees receive a monthly training allowance ranging from S$1,800 to $2,400

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
The GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov) Programme supports fresh graduates in gaining experience and skills, to facilitate their transition into full-time employment. GRIT@Gov offers traineeships for fresh graduates within public sector agencies.

 

Trainees will receive a monthly training allowance ranging from S$1,800 to $2,400 for the duration of the traineeship. This training allowance may vary depending on the scope of each traineeship.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
All trainees must fulfil the following criteria:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident; and

Graduated or graduating in 2024, 2025 or 2026 from Universities, Polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education (ITE) and other Educational Institutions (e.g. private universities and overseas institutions); or

Graduated earlier from above institutions and completed National Service in 2024, 2025 or 2026. 

Applicants must have no prior working experience and must be prepared to commit to a full-time traineeship.

 

Trainees who complete their studies from Universities, Polytechnics, the Institute of Technical Education (ITE) or other Educational Institutions within 2026 but who will only receive their qualification/certification award in 2027 are eligible for the programme as well.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
Browse and apply for GRIT@Gov traineeships via Careers@Gov.

 

If you are a fresh graduate who is considering a role in the Public Service, check out our Early Careers page.

 

To view all vacancies across the Public Service (including for mid-careerists), please visit our Careers@Gov job portal.

title: Fostering Scheme
tagline: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
text: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.

title: Fostering Scheme
tagline: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
text:
Allows children in need to benefit from a nurturing home environment that facilitates their growth and enables them to fulfil their potential
Support services are available for foster families, including fostering allowance to defray the cost of caring for the child, training for foster parents, and support and guidance from Foster Care Worker and other professionals
Aims to continue building up a diverse pool of foster parents to meet the different needs of foster children

title: Fostering Scheme
tagline: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
text:
Monthly fostering allowance of $1,100 for each foster child, or $1,500 for a child with special needs

Childcare subsidies with a monthly co-payment of $200 for full-day care and $150 for half-day care to be paid by foster parents

Student care subsidies with a monthly co-payment of $80 to be paid by foster parents for MSF-registered centres

Medical subsidies cover medical treatment at public healthcare institutions (polyclinics, public hospitals, and public specialist centres)

Foster children also receive subsidies at participating CHAS General Practitioners and dental clinics

Support and guidance from Foster Care Worker attached to every foster parent

Training courses conducted by Social Service Institute for new foster parents

Immediate support via an emergency hotline available 24/7

Support groups and respite care available

Foster parents enjoy childcare leave benefits, just as birth parents do under the Child Development Co-Savings Act and Employment Act

title: Fostering Scheme
tagline: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
text:
At least 25 years old

Married and applying as a couple

Medically fit to care for children

Minimum secondary school education

Minimum monthly household income of $2,000 and per capita income of at least $700 (Retirees can apply to be assessed based on non-work sources of income and saving)

Provide a child-safe home environment, which includes commitment not to use physical punishment, and installation of window grilles for foster children below 13 years old

title: Fostering Scheme
tagline: Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
text:
Apply online through the MSF Fostering Scheme portal:

Click "Apply"

Register or sign in

Fill in the application form

Upload supporting documents

MSF or an appointed agency will contact you within three working days

The assessment include:

Interview session(s)

Home Visit

Medical screenings

After approval:

Attend mandatory training sessions

Await foster child placement

title: Retirement Sum Topping-Up Scheme
tagline: Boost your own or loved ones’ retirement savings and payouts through voluntary cash top-ups or CPF transfer to your own or loved ones' Special Account (SA) or Retirement Account (RA).
text:
Receive higher monthly payouts in retirement

Earn CPF interest rates of up to 6% per annum on retirement savings

Grow your retirement savings through the power of compound interest for higher monthly payouts

Enjoy tax relief of up to $16,000 for cash top-ups made to your own and loved ones' retirement savings each calendar year

Find out more on the conditions to qualify for tax relief

Note: 

In keeping with the objective of the scheme, cash top-ups and CPF transfers are irreversible, and cannot be withdrawn for other purposes (e.g. for housing, investment or withdrawn for immediate needs after age 55).

Cash top-ups that attract the MRSS matching grant do not qualify for tax relief.

title: Retirement Sum Topping-Up Scheme
tagline: Boost your own or loved ones’ retirement savings and payouts through voluntary cash top-ups or CPF transfer to your own or loved ones' Special Account (SA) or Retirement Account (RA).
text:
You must be a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident to receive cash top-ups and CPF transfers

You can check your top-up limits by logging in to your Retirement Dashboard

Members aged 55 and above can top up to the Enhanced Retirement Sum (ERS)

The ERS increases each year so you can top up more each year

Members below 55 can top up to the Full Retirement Sum (FRS)

CPF transfers can be made to yourself or your loved ones:

CPF transfer to

	

Remarks

Yourself

	

You can transfer your Ordinary Account (OA) savings to your SA if you are below 55. 

If you are aged 55 or above, you can transfer your OA savings to your RA. 

How much you can transfer depends on your age and CPF balances. 

Loved ones 
(parents, parents-in-law, grandparents, grandparents-in-law, spouse and siblings)

	

Before transferring your CPF savings to your loved ones, you must first set aside enough for your own retirement. 

The amount of CPF transfers you can make to them depends on how much your loved one can receive, and the amount you can transfer. 

For more information on your eligibility for cash transfers, CPF top-ups etc., please refer here

To complete your top-up form, you will need:

your recipient’s NRIC, if making a cash top-up or CPF transfer to a loved one

additional supporting documents if making a CPF transfer to a recipient for the first time (not required for cash top-ups)

For more information on the application process, please refer here.

title: Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
tagline: Support eligible working parents in sharing parental responsibility and caring for their infants.
text:
SPL (2013)

	

SPL (from 1 Apr 2025)

Amount of leave entitled 

	

Eligible working fathers can take up to 4 weeks of leave from their wife’s Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) or Adoption Leave (AL). 

	

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date or Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, up to 6 weeks of leave to be shared between parents 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, up to 10 weeks of leave to be shared between parents 

Amount of government reimbursement/payment

	

Government reimbursement/payment capped at $2,500 per week, up to a total of $10,000 for 4 weeks. 

	

Government reimbursement/payment capped at $2,500 per week. 

Flexibility of leave 

	

Flexibility for leave to be taken in one continuous block or non-continuously, with mutual agreement between employer and employee. 

When can SPL be taken? 

	

Within 12 months from the child's date of birth. 

	

After Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)/ Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL)/ Adoption Leave (AL) is fully consumed; and  

Within 12 months from the child’s date of birth.

title: Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
tagline: Support eligible working parents in sharing parental responsibility and caring for their infants.
text:
Parents whose child is born, or has an estimated delivery date (EDD), or formal intent to adopt (FIA) date on or after 1 Apr 2025, are entitled to the new SPL scheme and should not be making an election for 2013-SPL. 

	

SPL (2013) (For father) 

	

SPL (from 1 Apr 2025) (For both father and mother) 

For live births 

	

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth  
 

Child's birth or estimated delivery date is between 1 Jul 2017 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Wife is eligible for Government-Paid Maternity Leave or Adoption Leave and agrees to share the leave 
 

Married to child's mother between conception and birth or within 12 months from birth 

	

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth  
 

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date:

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks of SPL 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks of SPL 

 

 For fathers: Lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and birth or within 12 months from birth 
 

For employee: Served employer for at least 3 months before child’s birth  
 

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before child’s birth 

For stillbirths 

	

The date of stillbirth is between 1 Nov 2021 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Either you or the child’s mother are Singapore citizens 

	

NIL 

For adoptive child 

	

Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date between 1 Jul 2017 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Married to the adoptive mother when the election for 2013-SPL was made 
 

If child is not Singapore citizen (SC), either you or the adoptive mother has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 

	

Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks of SPL 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks of SPL 

Not the natural father or mother of child 
 

If child is not Singapore citizen (SC), either adoptive mother or father of child has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 
 

For employee: Served employer for at least 3 months before FIA date 
 

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before FIA date

title: Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
tagline: Support eligible working parents in sharing parental responsibility and caring for their infants.
text:
For SPL (2013)
 

Discuss and agree on the leave arrangement with your wife. 

Your wife must declare her intention to share her leave through the Share My Leave eService in the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal no earlier than 16 weeks before your child’s estimated delivery date. 

For employees: 

	

Notify your employer of the leave arrangement and submit the declaration form (SPL1) with necessary documents. 
 

For self-employed: 

	

Record your SPL dates and submit your claim online via the GPL Portal within 3 months after the last date of your SPL. 
 

For employers: 

	

Obtain and verify the declaration form (SPL1) and supporting documents from your employee. 

Keep the declaration form or records for 5 years from the last leave date for audit purposes. 

Verify that your employee’s wife has made a declaration to share her leave in the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal. Check all supporting documents to confirm your employee’s eligibility. 

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal no later than 3 months after the last date of your employee’s SPL. 

For SPL (from 1 Apr 2025) 

Please refer to details on the GPL Portal.

title: CPF LIFE
tagline: CPF Lifelong Income For the Elderly (CPF LIFE) is a national longevity insurance annuity scheme that provides you with monthly payouts no matter how long you live.
text:
Monthly payouts for life
Offers various plans to suit individual preferences and needs
Option to start payouts at a later age up to age 70, where monthly payouts will increase by up to 7% for each year of deferral

title: CPF LIFE
tagline: CPF Lifelong Income For the Elderly (CPF LIFE) is a national longevity insurance annuity scheme that provides you with monthly payouts no matter how long you live.
text:
Provides a lifelong monthly payout to protect against outliving one’s savings

Unlike private annuity plans, which are subject to investment market returns, CPF LIFE monies are guaranteed by the government and earn a risk-free interest of up to 6% per annum

Offers various plans to suit individual preferences and needs:

Escalating Plan

	

Increasing monthly payouts to help you maintain your lifestyle when prices increase.

Standard Plan

	

Steady monthly payouts even if you may have to keep within a fixed budget and adjust your lifestyle when prices increase.

Basic Plan

	

Payouts that will get progressively lower when balances fall below $60,000. You may have to lower your lifestyle further when prices increase.

Option to start payouts at a later age to receive up to a 7% increase in monthly payout.

title: CPF LIFE
tagline: CPF Lifelong Income For the Elderly (CPF LIFE) is a national longevity insurance annuity scheme that provides you with monthly payouts no matter how long you live.
text:
Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident born in 1958 and after, with at least $60,000 in the Retirement Account when you start your monthly payouts, will be automatically included in CPF LIFE

If you are a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident who is not automatically included in CPF LIFE, you can still choose to join CPF LIFE as long as you are a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, anytime from 65 to one month before you reach 80

You can choose to be exempted from CPF LIFE if you have a pension or private annuity plan that pays the same or higher monthly payouts than as CPF LIFE

title: CPF LIFE
tagline: CPF Lifelong Income For the Elderly (CPF LIFE) is a national longevity insurance annuity scheme that provides you with monthly payouts no matter how long you live.
text:
CPF Board will send you a notification three months before your 65th birthday on the options you have about your CPF savings and how you can make an informed decision using the Plan my monthly payouts service

If you have not instructed the CPF Board regarding your payouts by then, your payouts will automatically start when you turn 70 under the LIFE Standard Plan

text: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.

title: CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)
tagline: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.
text: Grants multiple options to invest your Ordinary Account (OA) and Special Account (SA) savings

title: CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)
tagline: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.
text: Members can invest their OA and SA savings in a wide range of products to enhance their retirement savings under the CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS).

title: CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)
tagline: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.
text:
To be eligible to invest in CPFIS, you must meet all the following criteria:

At least 18 years old

Not an undischarged bankrupt

Have more than $20,000 in OA and / or more than $40,000 in SA

Completed the CPFIS Self-Awareness Questionnaire (SAQ)

If investing in the CPFIS- OA scheme, you may only invest up to 35% of your investible savings for stocks and 10% for gold. Investible savings refers to the sum of your OA balance and the amount of CPF you have withdrawn for investment and education.

To find out how much of your OA and SA savings can be used for investment, you may:

Log in to my cpf digital services with your Singpass, then select “my cpf” > “My dashboards” > “Investment”

Log in to the CPF Mobile app with your Singpass, then select “Investment”

Visit any CPF Service Centre with your identity card

Upon reaching age 55, you can withdraw your CPFIS-OA and CPFIS-SA investments, as well as the cash balance in your Investment Account, after setting aside the Full Retirement Sum (FRS) in your Retirement Account

The FRS can be set aside fully with cash / with cash (i.e. at least the Basic Retirement Sum) and property

Once your withdrawal application is approved, your CPFIS investments will be transferred to you. The withdrawal will not result in liquidation of your investment holdings

Your agent bank and / or product providers will contact you about transferring your investments to you

title: CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)
tagline: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.
text:
New CPFIS investors must first take the Self-Awareness Questionnaire (SAQ) before they can start investing

The SAQ helps you to assess if you have basic financial knowledge and whether CPFIS is suitable for you

To start investing under CPFIS-OA, open a CPF Investment Account with one of the following CPFIS agent banks:

Overseas-Chinese Banking Corporation Ltd (OCBC)

United Overseas Bank Ltd (UOB)

DBS Bank Ltd (DBS)

After opening the account, approach product providers to buy or sell investments

For CPFIS-SA:

Approach product providers directly to buy or sell investments

title: CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)
tagline: Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.
text:
Provides CPF members an option to invest their OA and SA savings to enhance retirement funds.

Grants multiple options to invest your Ordinary Account (OA) and Special Account (SA) savings

Members can invest their OA and SA savings in a wide range of products to enhance their retirement savings under the CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS).

To be eligible to invest in CPFIS, you must meet all the following criteria:

At least 18 years old

Not an undischarged bankrupt

Have more than $20,000 in OA and / or more than $40,000 in SA

Completed the CPFIS Self-Awareness Questionnaire (SAQ)

If investing in the CPFIS- OA scheme, you may only invest up to 35% of your investible savings for stocks and 10% for gold. Investible savings refers to the sum of your OA balance and the amount of CPF you have withdrawn for investment and education.

To find out how much of your OA and SA savings can be used for investment, you may:

Log in to my cpf digital services with your Singpass, then select “my cpf” > “My dashboards” > “Investment”

Log in to the CPF Mobile app with your Singpass, then select “Investment”

Visit any CPF Service Centre with your identity card

Upon reaching age 55, you can withdraw your CPFIS-OA and CPFIS-SA investments, as well as the cash balance in your Investment Account, after setting aside the Full Retirement Sum (FRS) in your Retirement Account

The FRS can be set aside fully with cash / with cash (i.e. at least the Basic Retirement Sum) and property

Once your withdrawal application is approved, your CPFIS investments will be transferred to you. The withdrawal will not result in liquidation of your investment holdings

Your agent bank and / or product providers will contact you about transferring your investments to you

New CPFIS investors must first take the Self-Awareness Questionnaire (SAQ) before they can start investing

The SAQ helps you to assess if you have basic financial knowledge and whether CPFIS is suitable for you

To start investing under CPFIS-OA, open a CPF Investment Account with one of the following CPFIS agent banks:

Overseas-Chinese Banking Corporation Ltd (OCBC)

United Overseas Bank Ltd (UOB)

DBS Bank Ltd (DBS)

After opening the account, approach product providers to buy or sell investments

For CPFIS-SA:

Approach product providers directly to buy or sell investments

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

title: Budget 2026 CPF Top-Up

title: Budget 2026 CPF Top-Up
tagline: Provides additional support to boost the retirement adequacy of eligible Singaporeans with CPF retirement savings below prevailing Basic Retirement Sum (BRS) through a CPF top-up in 2026.
text:
Eligibility criteria

Singapore Citizen

50 years old and above in 2026

CPF LIFE/Retirement Account or Ordinary/Special Account balances below prevailing BRS in 2026 of $110,200 as at 31 December 2025

Tiered by annual value of the place of residence (as indicated on NRIC), as at 31 December 2025

Does not own more than 1 property

title: SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme
tagline: Provides temporary financial support of up to $6,000 over 6 months to involuntarily unemployed individuals, to support them in finding a job that makes better use of their skills and experiences.
text:
Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, aged 21 and above 

Earned an average gross monthly income of $5,000 or less in the past 12 months* (excluding employer CPF contributions)

Previously employed in Singapore for at least six months in the past 12 months

Unemployed due to involuntary reasons such as retrenchment, cessation of business, dismissals or termination due to illness, injury or accident

Lives in a property in Singapore with annual value of $31,000 or less

Has not received a payout from this scheme in the past three years

* Average gross monthly income includes basic salary and extra wages such as overtime pay, commissions and bonuses. This includes only the period(s) where the individual is employed and earning an income.

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
text: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
text:
Provides training allowance for trainees who self-sponsor their own short training
Provides cash award for trainees who complete sustained training
Provides absentee payroll for employers who send eligible employees for training
No application required for employees; self-employed persons and employers should submit claims online

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
text:
Training Allowance (TA): $6 per training hour (from 1 July 2026: $10.50 per training hour) for self-sponsored trainees

Training commitment award: Up to $1,000 (from 1 July 2026: up to $800) for achieving the required certifications

Absentee payroll for employers sending eligible employees for training: 95% trainee’s basic hourly wage, capped at $13 per training hour

WSS (Basic) supports short training, such as:

Workplace Literacy and Numeracy courses

Recommended courses that meet Progressive Wage Model (PWM) training requirements

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
text:
Employees

No application needed.

 

Self-employed persons

You will need to provide in the TA claim form within 120 days of course end date:

softcopy of your proof of self-employment (for example, contract for service or equivalent dated within 3 months after course end date, or ACRA business registration and business transaction documents dated within 3 months after course end date)

your course details (for example, course title, course start and end dates, and training provider’s name

Employers

You can apply online at Enterprise Portal for Jobs and Skills

title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
tagline: Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
text:
Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.

Provides training allowance for trainees who self-sponsor their own short training
Provides cash award for trainees who complete sustained training
Provides absentee payroll for employers who send eligible employees for training
No application required for employees; self-employed persons and employers should submit claims online

Training Allowance (TA): $6 per training hour (from 1 July 2026: $10.50 per training hour) for self-sponsored trainees

Training commitment award: Up to $1,000 (from 1 July 2026: up to $800) for achieving the required certifications

Absentee payroll for employers sending eligible employees for training: 95% trainee’s basic hourly wage, capped at $13 per training hour

WSS (Basic) supports short training, such as:

Workplace Literacy and Numeracy courses

Recommended courses that meet Progressive Wage Model (PWM) training requirements

Singapore Citizen;

At least 30 years old or older as of 31 December of the work year; or at least 13 years old for persons with disabilities.

Earn an average gross monthly income of not more than $3,000 for the past 12 months;

Have declared your net trade income and made MediSave contributions in full if you are self-employed;

Live in a property with an annual value of $21,000 and below as at 31 December of the preceding year;

Own not more than one property; and

If you are married:

you and your spouse together not own more than one property; and

the assessable income of your spouse does not exceed $70,000 of the preceding Year of Assessment

 

The above is similar to the Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) criteria.

Employees

No application needed.

 

Self-employed persons

You will need to provide in the TA claim form within 120 days of course end date:

softcopy of your proof of self-employment (for example, contract for service or equivalent dated within 3 months after course end date, or ACRA business registration and business transaction documents dated within 3 months after course end date)

your course details (for example, course title, course start and end dates, and training provider’s name

Employers

You can apply online at Enterprise Portal for Jobs and Skills

WSS hotline: 1800 536 8333

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.
text: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.
text:
Claim up to $7,500 per dependant
Can be applied alongside the Working Mother’s Child Relief (WMCR) scheme for additional support
Parents may share the QCR / Child Relief (Disability)

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.
text:
QCR of $4,000 per qualifying child

Child Relief (Disability) of $7,500 per child with disabilities

Eligible working mothers can also apply for Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR) in conjunction with the QCR / Child Relief (Disability) for additional support

Parents may share the QCR / Child Relief (Disability) based on an agreed apportionment

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.
text:
To be eligible for QCR, you must meet all the following conditions:

During the previous year,

Your child is unmarried and is either:

Born to you and your spouse / ex-spouse

A stepchild

Legally adopted

Your child was either:

Below 16 years old

Above 16 years old and studying full-time at any university, college or other educational institution

Your child did not have an annual income exceeding $8,000.

To be eligible for Child Relief (Disability):

Your child must be unmarried / have been unmarried in the previous year and is either:

Born to you and your spouse / ex-spouse

A stepchild

Legally adopted

Your child meets any of the following criteria:

Your child is certified by a doctor to require assistance in any of the 6 Activities of Daily Living:

Washing or Bathing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Transferring

Mobility

Your child is certified by a doctor to be impaired in any of these 3 areas:

Self-Care and Activities of Daily Living

Compliance to Psychiatric Treatment

Education or Work

Your child is attending (or is recommended by a doctor to attend) a Special Education (SPED) school

Your child is not attending a SPED school but has significant special educational needs, resulting in severe functional impairments

 

Conditions related to claiming WMCR together with QCR / Child Relief (Disability):

The total claimable relief for QCR, Child Relief (Disability) and WMCR combined is capped at $50,000 per child

When claiming both QCR / Child Relief (Disability) and WMCR, the QCR and Child Relief (Disability) claims will take precedence. WMCR claims will only be applied after.

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)
tagline: Provides tax relief to parents to recognise their efforts in supporting their children.
text:
Please refer to the following processes to apply for QCR:

For e-Filing

If claiming QCR for the first time, log in to myTax Portal using your Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA)

Navigate to "Individuals" > "File Income Tax Return"

Select "File" to File Income Tax Return

Go to "4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates"

Select “Add New” > “Child".

Fill in the relevant information and select “Update”.

If you were previously approved for relief last year, the amount will be pre-filled and granted to you automatically this year

If you would like to make changes, please follow the above e-Filing process for first-time applicants

For paper filing

Complete the "Child Relief" section in Form B1 & B

Include your child's details in Appendix 2 if it's your first time claiming

Please refer to the following processes to apply for Child Relief (Disability):

For first-time Child Relief (Disability) claims, email 1 of the following documents via myTax Mail

Completed Tax Relief Form - Application for Claim of Disability Related Tax Reliefs

Letter / supporting documents from the disability association / SPED school in respect of your child, providing details of their disabilities, to IRAS

If your child is attending / has been recommended to attend a SPED school or has significant special education needs, please refer to the “First-time Child Relief (Disability) claims” section on the Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability) page) for more details.

title: Qualifying Child Relief (QCR)/Child Relief (Disability)

text:
IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
text: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).

title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
text:
For children born or adopted before 1 Jan 2024, eligible fathers can receive up to 2 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB) , capped at $2,500 per week.
For children born or adopted on or after 1 Jan 2024, eligible fathers can receive up to 4 weeks of GPPB, capped at $2,500 per week.
For natural or adoptive fathers who do not qualify for GPPL due to their employment arrangement

title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
text:
Up to 2 weeks of income support for eligible fathers for child born/estimated delivery/FIA date before January 1, 2024, capped at $2,500 per week.

Up to 4 weeks of income support for eligible fathers for child born/estimated delivery date/FIA on or after January 1, 2024, capped at $2,500 per week.

title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
text:
For natural fathers with live births:

Your child’s date of birth/estimated delivery date is on or after 1 Jan 2021.

Your child is a Singapore Citizen or becomes one within 12 months of birth

For natural fathers with stillbirths:

The date of stillbirth is on or after November 1, 2021

Either you or the child’s mother is a Singapore citizen

For adoptive fathers:

You are not the natural father of the child

Your FIA date is on or after 1 Jan 2021

The child must be below 12 months old on FIA date

If child is not a Singapore citizen, a dependent’s pass (DP) has been issued and either you or your wife are Singapore citizens.

Applicable to all:

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before child's birth

Lawfully married to the child's mother between conception and birth or within 12 months of birth

Not eligible for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment

title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
tagline: Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
text:
Record employment or self-employment details for the 12 months before your child's birth or Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date.

Submit your claim online here within 15 months of your child's birth or FIA date.

An officer will contact you following your submission.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
Offers up to 800 structured traineeships across various sectors for 3 to 6 months
Monthly allowance ranging from S$1,800 to S$2,400, co-funded by the government and host organisations

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text: Jointly introduced by the Ministry of Manpower (MOM), Workforce Singapore (WSG), GRIT provides fresh graduates with meaningful traineeships in both private companies and public agencies, enhancing their employability and work readiness.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
All trainees must fulfil the following criteria:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident; and

Graduated or graduating in 2024, 2025 or 2026 from Universities, Polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education (ITE) and other Educational Institutions (e.g. private universities and overseas institutions); or

Graduated earlier from above institutions and completed National Service in 2024, 2025 or 2026. 

Applicants must have no prior working experience and must be prepared to commit to a full-time traineeship.

title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT)
tagline: Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
text:
Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.

Offers up to 800 structured traineeships across various sectors for 3 to 6 months
Monthly allowance ranging from S$1,800 to S$2,400, co-funded by the government and host organisations

Jointly introduced by the Ministry of Manpower (MOM), Workforce Singapore (WSG), GRIT provides fresh graduates with meaningful traineeships in both private companies and public agencies, enhancing their employability and work readiness.

All trainees must fulfil the following criteria:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident; and

Graduated or graduating in 2024, 2025 or 2026 from Universities, Polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education (ITE) and other Educational Institutions (e.g. private universities and overseas institutions); or

Graduated earlier from above institutions and completed National Service in 2024, 2025 or 2026. 

Applicants must have no prior working experience and must be prepared to commit to a full-time traineeship.

Traineeship opportunities will be progressively made available on MyCareersFuture.

WSG Hotline: 6883 5885
 Monday - Friday: 8:30am to 5:30pm
 Saturday, Sunday & Public Holidays Closed

Submit an enquiry

title: Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
tagline: Provides up to 6 days of leave for eligible working parents and legal guardians with children below 7 years old, and 2 days for those with children aged 7 to 12.
text:
Up to 6 days of Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) for parents with a Singapore citizen child below 7 years old, capped at $500 per day for the 4th to 6th day, up to $1,500 per year
2 days of Extended Childcare Leave (ECL) for parents with a Singapore citizen child aged 7 to 12, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,000 per year

title: Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
tagline: Provides up to 6 days of leave for eligible working parents and legal guardians with children below 7 years old, and 2 days for those with children aged 7 to 12.
text:
For parents with a Singapore citizen child below 7 years old:

Up to 6 days of Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL)

Employer pays for the first 3 days of GPCL

Government reimburses for the 4th to 6th day, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,500 per year

For parents with a Singapore citizen child between 7 and 12 years old:

2 days of Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)

Fully reimbursed by the Government, capped at $500 per day, up to $1,000 per year.

title: Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
tagline: Provides up to 6 days of leave for eligible working parents and legal guardians with children below 7 years old, and 2 days for those with children aged 7 to 12.
text:
Singapore Citizen child below 7 years old for GPCL or obtains Singapore citizenship within the calendar year

Singapore Citizen child aged 7 to 12 years old for ECL or obtains Singapore citizenship within the calendar year

Parent has served employer for at least 3 months or been self-employed for at least 3 months and have lost income from ceasing to be actively engaged during your GPCL/ECL period.

Adoptive parents, eligible only after Adoption Order is passed

Natural fathers not eligible if not lawfully married to the child's mother at conception and not married subsequently

Foster parents eligible until they cease providing care, protection or supervision

Legal guardians with court-appointed guardianship eligible

title: Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and Extended Childcare Leave (ECL)
tagline: Provides up to 6 days of leave for eligible working parents and legal guardians with children below 7 years old, and 2 days for those with children aged 7 to 12.
text:
For employees:

Inform your employer of your leave plans.

For foster parents, show your Letter of Identity to your employer as proof that a foster child has been placed under your care under the Children and Young Persons Act. Your employer can write in to the MSF Fostering Service at fostering@msf.gov.sg to verify your eligibility for GPCL/ECL

For legal guardians, provide a copy of your Legal Guardianship document (i.e. Order of Court) to your employer, for verification on your GPCL/ECL eligibility.

Submit a declaration form (GPCL1) with necessary documents.

Apply for leave as per your employer's procedures.

For self-employed:

Record your leave dates.

Submit your claim on the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal within 3 months after the calendar year ends.

For employers:

Collect and verify the declaration form (GPCL1) from your employee.

Submit your reimbursement claim on the GPL Portal within 3 months after the 12-month period ends.

title: Voluntary Housing Refund
tagline: Allows you to return any CPF savings used for your home purchase to your CPF account while still owning your property. Doing so reduces the refund amount you would have to make and increases the cash proceeds you would get when you eventually sell or transfer your property. This also boosts your retirement savings as you can benefit from CPF interest rates.

title: Voluntary Housing Refund
tagline: Allows you to return any CPF savings used for your home purchase to your CPF account while still owning your property. Doing so reduces the refund amount you would have to make and increases the cash proceeds you would get when you eventually sell or transfer your property. This also boosts your retirement savings as you can benefit from CPF interest rates.
text:
You have used your Ordinary Account (OA) savings to purchase your property

Please note the following conditions when making the voluntary housing refund:

You may refund up to the CPF principal amount withdrawn towards the property plus its accrued interest to your CPF account

If you are an undischarged bankrupt, you have to obtain the Official Assignee’s approval to make this housing refund

While housing grants will generally be refunded to your OA if you have an HDB flat, the grant(s) may be partially credited to your Special Account / Retirement Account and MediSave Account if you have received $30,000 or more in grant(s)

If you are 55 and above, the refunded amount will be used to meet your Full Retirement Sum (FRS), which supports your retirement needs through monthly payouts

Any refunds that exceed your cohort’s FRS will remain in your OA

You may read more on housing refunds and their processes

title: Hospital-to-Work Transition Programme
tagline: Provides case management, rehabilitation and employment support for persons with acquired disability from accidents or medical conditions who are looking to return to work.
text:
Jobseekers in the programme can get personalised career re-assessment, rehabilitation and job-matching services. Persons with disabilities who are already in employment can also benefit from job support services. A case manager will support you throughout your transition to work, providing the following services:

Rehabilitation

For clients requiring rehabilitation support, SG Enable collaborates with partner agencies such as SPD and ABLE to provide integrated rehabilitation and employment support services.

Case management

Assessing the jobseeker’s strengths, job interests and skills, and identifying job options.

Facilitate psychosocial and community support through referrals, if needed.

Recommending relevant training courses, building resume and strengthening interview skills.

Assessing the nature of the job and its tasks for suitability. 

Assessing the built environment for accessibility.

Working with employers to job redesign and recommend workplace modification, if needed.

Employment support

Vocational re-assessment, training coordination, job matching and placement, and on-site job coaching to help the new employee integrate into the workplace.

title: Hospital-to-Work Transition Programme
tagline: Provides case management, rehabilitation and employment support for persons with acquired disability from accidents or medical conditions who are looking to return to work.
text:
Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Above 16 years of age

Medically certified as fit for employment

Persons with a permanent acquired disability*^ based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least one of the six Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

Moderate Visual Impairment or worse in the better eye  

Moderate Hearing Loss or worse in the better ear 

*If your condition does not fall under the categories listed above, you may still approach SG Enable for a discussion.

^You can check if your disability status is verified on SupportGoWhere by logging with your Singpass or contacting us at H2W_SWW@sgenable.sg.

title: Hospital-to-Work Transition Programme
tagline: Provides case management, rehabilitation and employment support for persons with acquired disability from accidents or medical conditions who are looking to return to work.
text:
Preparation of documents

The following supporting documents are required before making an application:

Disability Verification Form (DVF) (To be completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional)

Fitness for Employment and Rehabilitation Form (To be completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional)

Hospital-to-Work Application Form (For email/physical submissions only)

OneMSF Omnibus Consent Form

Copy of NRIC Front and Back

 For persons with disabilities:

Apply online via SupportGoWhere, or

Email H2W_SWW@sgenable.sg with the above completed supporting documents

Approach SG Enable’s Information and Career Centre, located within the Enabling Village, for assistance.

 

For Healthcare Professionals (HCP) making referrals:

Apply online via Partner Portal, or

Email H2W_SWW@sgenable.sg with the above supporting documents filled and endorsed.

title: Counselling (Seniors)
tagline: Provides counselling to older persons aged 50 and above and/or their caregivers, to improve their mental well-being and resilience to cope with life’s challenges.
text:
Gerontological counselling provides an avenue for seniors and their caregivers to discuss their issues and concerns and help to address their personal, social or psychological challenges.

Individual and group counselling available.

Counselling can be provided at the counselling centre or at their own home if necessary.

title: Counselling (Seniors)
tagline: Provides counselling to older persons aged 50 and above and/or their caregivers, to improve their mental well-being and resilience to cope with life’s challenges.
text:
Persons aged 50 years old and above with issues and concerns such as:

Grief and loss

Loneliness/isolation

Depression

Stress and anxiety

Relationship problems

Difficulty adjusting to transitions and changes (e.g. retirement, ‘empty nest’ syndrome, onset of illnesses)

Family members/caregivers who experience stress caring for their elderly loved ones who are experiencing the above-mentioned concerns can also be supported. 

Hua Mei Centre for Successful Ageing also provides individual and group coaching in emotion management, inter-generational relationship-building and communication skills.

title: Youth Community Outreach Team (CREST-Youth)
tagline: Provides emotional support to youths in psychological distress, as your in-person First Stop for Mental Health.
text:
You can approach over 100 community mental health teams in the community islandwide such as CREST-Youth, providing the following support:

 Increase public awareness of youth mental health by organising outreach events for the youth population, as well as those in their circles of support.

Promote the recognition of early signs and symptoms of mental health conditions.

Provide coordinated person-centric care (social and health), information and support for youths and their caregivers through providing basic emotional support, follow up and service linkage.

Network and collaborate with health and community care partners and key stakeholders to enhance support for youths with mental health needs.

Involve caregivers in the recovery journey of youths.

These community mental health teams are developed under the Community Mental Health Masterplan driven by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), together with the Ministry of Health (MOH). The teams are fully funded by the government and there is no out-of-pocket cost.

title: SOS Care Text
tagline: 24/7 confidential text support for suicide prevention and crisis intervention, accessible to all but targeting at youths from the ages 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk.
text: 24/7 confidential text support for suicide prevention and crisis intervention, accessible to all but targeting at youths from the ages 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk.

title: SOS Care Text
tagline: 24/7 confidential text support for suicide prevention and crisis intervention, accessible to all but targeting at youths from the ages 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk.
text:
Helpline
Free
Referral not required

title: SOS Care Text
tagline: 24/7 confidential text support for suicide prevention and crisis intervention, accessible to all but targeting at youths from the ages 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk.
text:
Samaritans of Singapore (SOS) Care Text is dedicated to providing confidential emotional support to individuals facing a crisis, thinking about suicide or affected by suicide. This crisis intervention and suicide prevention work is done through text messaging.

This programme contributes to the identification of individuals who are suicidal or in crisis, initiation of early intervention and preventive measures, and connecting these individuals to appropriate resources where necessary

This programme is targeted at youths from the ages of 10 to 29 years old with suicide risk, but not limited to this group and anyone can access this service. Programme aims to reduce suicide risks for persons in distress through texting which is a preference for the youths. The SOS Care Text is available round-the-clock to anyone who requires emotional support and or is affected by suicide and complements the current SOS crisis hotline service. The text service is anonymous.

Clients can access care text by texting in to WhatsApp 9151 1767. 24-hour text messaging platform manned by trained volunteers and supported by professional staff.

Depending on need, clients may be encouraged to seek immediate support from the hospitals, psychiatrists, therapists, counsellors and community services or encouraged to see a counsellor at SOS.

title: Lighthouse Project
tagline: Collaboration between Limitless and KKH to provide effective and timely care for youth with mental health conditions who require higher levels of care.
text: Collaboration between Limitless and KKH to provide effective and timely care for youth with mental health conditions who require higher levels of care.

title: Lighthouse Project
tagline: Collaboration between Limitless and KKH to provide effective and timely care for youth with mental health conditions who require higher levels of care.
text:
Centre-based
Free
Referral required

title: Lighthouse Project
tagline: Collaboration between Limitless and KKH to provide effective and timely care for youth with mental health conditions who require higher levels of care.
text: The Lighthouse Project aims to be a beacon of light that attracts and correctly directs youths (aged 12-25 years old) with complex moderate to high mental health needs to accessible and appropriate help by doing the following: Providing higher-tiered support at the community level. Senior and highly skilled practitioners at Limitless provide support to youths who would otherwise never get appropriate treatment for complex mental health issues. Right-siting youths requiring higher tiered care by using the therapeutic relationship built with practitioners at Limitless to "cushion" upwards referral into primary, secondary, and tertiary care. Improving standards of care at the community level through preceptorship by senior practitioners nominated by Department of Psychological Medicine (DPM, KKH), and reduction of secondary traumatic stress in the sector through supervision and training of partners by Limitless’ psychologists.

title: Counselling
tagline: Provides counselling and psychotherapy to individuals, couples and families facing interpersonal, intrapersonal and mental health issues.
text:
The service provider will assess the request for counselling and ask your presenting issues and preferred day/time for the counselling and arrange a slot for you accordingly.

The staff will also provide information on the fees.

Counselling intake and assessment is conducted at the first appointment and the client will see the same counsellor for the subsequent sessions until the case is closed or transferred.

If counselling is not suitable, the counsellor may refer you to other appropriate services.

A counselling session takes around 1 hour.

Most issues will need more than 1 counselling session to resolve.

Fees payable for all service providers except SAMH Insight Centre.

text: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.

title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
tagline: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
text:
Centre-based, Helpline, Home-based, House-call, Online
Free
Referral required

title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
tagline: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
text: SFAT comprises a 10-session intervention, which includes 2 workshops and 8 group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool that participants can use independently with their families.      The free programme is delivered by credentialed art therapists from The Red Pencil (Singapore), in partnership with Glyph Community Ltd and Yishun Family Service Centre.

title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
tagline: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
text: The Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) programme serves children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families. Eligible families include those receiving CHAS Blue card, ComCare financial assistance, or MOE Financial Assistance Scheme.     The programme particularly supports families experiencing caregiver stress related to finances, work-life balance, and childcare arrangements, caregivers who struggle to find time for self-care and quality time with children, those with minimal understanding of their children's emotional needs, and children facing difficulties with emotional regulation.      Families can participate as a dyad of one caregiver and one child, or a caregiver may join with up to three children aged 7-14.

title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
tagline: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
text: Interested families can apply for the Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) programme through either Glyph Community Ltd or Yishun Family Service Centre under Singapore Children's Society. These partner organisations will screen potential participants for eligibility and assess needs through their existing processes before referring eligible families to the programme.

title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
tagline: Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
text: After hearing about the programme due to publicity from Glyph Community Ltd or Yishun Family Service Centre and signing up, you will first attend a pre-session where you'll be briefed about the programme, have the opportunity to ask questions, sign consent forms, and learn whether you can start immediately or join the next available programme cycle. If confirmed, you'll participate in a 10-session programme.     The programme runs for about 3 months. The programme takes place in small group settings with up to 6 families and consists of three main components: a pre-workshop introducing art therapy (1 session), group art therapy sessions (8 sessions), and a post-workshop with introduction to self-care tool (1 session).     After completing the programme, you will receive an Art-Based Self-Care Tool for independent use at home, and your family can continue receiving support from the referring organisation if needed. You will also be asked to provide feedback about the programme through evaluation forms.

text:
Home-based
Free
Referral required

title: KidSTART
tagline: Supports pregnant mothers and low-income families in early childhood development, to build strong foundations for children aged 6 and below.
text:
KidSTART Singapore Limited is a non-profit organisation and an Institution of a Public Character that specialises in supporting families in early childhood development.

Our specialist team partners with the government and community to empower low-income families in building strong foundations for their children, giving them a good start in life.

title: KidSTART
tagline: Supports pregnant mothers and low-income families in early childhood development, to build strong foundations for children aged 6 and below.
text:
You are eligible for our services if your family meets these criteria:

You have a pregnant mother and/or a child who is a Singapore Citizen and aged 6 and below

You have a monthly household income of $2,500 and below or monthly household income per capita of $650 and below

Is staying in the following areas:

Ang Mo Kio/Yishun

Bedok/Geylang Serai

title: KidSTART
tagline: Supports pregnant mothers and low-income families in early childhood development, to build strong foundations for children aged 6 and below.
text:
Supports pregnant mothers and low-income families in early childhood development, to build strong foundations for children aged 6 and below.

Home-based
Free
Referral required

KidSTART Singapore Limited is a non-profit organisation and an Institution of a Public Character that specialises in supporting families in early childhood development.

Our specialist team partners with the government and community to empower low-income families in building strong foundations for their children, giving them a good start in life.

You are eligible for our services if your family meets these criteria:

You have a pregnant mother and/or a child who is a Singapore Citizen and aged 6 and below

You have a monthly household income of $2,500 and below or monthly household income per capita of $650 and below

Is staying in the following areas:

Ang Mo Kio/Yishun

Bedok/Geylang Serai

Choa Chu Kang/Bukit Panjang/Bukit Batok

Hougang/Serangoon

Jalan Besar/Toa Payoh

Kreta Ayer/Bukit Merah

Punggol/Sengkang

Taman Jurong/Boon Lay

Woodlands/Sembawang

title: Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP)
tagline: Eligible applicants can remain in Singapore for an extended period via a Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP).
text:
The following applicant types are eligible to apply for LTVP:

Spouse of a Singapore citizen (must be sponsored by the Singapore citizen spouse)^

Foreign spouse of a Singapore Permanent Resident (must be sponsored by the Permanent Resident spouse)

Unmarried child under 21, born within a legal marriage to / legally adopted by a Singapore citizen or Permanent Resident (must be sponsored by parents)

Parent of a Singapore citizen or Permanent Resident (sponsor must be above 21)

Graduate from an Institute of Higher Learning seeking employment in Singapore

Parent or grandparent of a Student’s Pass holder studying in Singapore (requires a local sponsor above 21 years old)

Visitor seeking to give birth in Singapore (requires a local sponsor above 21 years old)

Please note that all the above criteria are not exhaustive. Eligibility is still subject to ICA’s final approval.

Singapore Citizens with foreign spouses may also be eligible for LTVP Plus (LTVP+), providing a longer period of residency and additional employment and healthcare benefits

For more information on the LTVP+ and its benefits, as well as your eligibility for it, please refer here

For LTVP+

No separate application is required. You will automatically be considered for LTVP+ (if eligible) when you apply for LTVP

^ Couples consisting of a Singapore Citizen and a Non-Resident are strongly encouraged to complete their Pre-Marriage LTVP Assessment (PMLA) before marriage. Learn more about the PMLA here and apply for PMLA here.

title: Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP)
tagline: Eligible applicants can remain in Singapore for an extended period via a Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP).
text:

Additional fees will apply if the LTVP application is granted in-principle approval

Applications are generally processed within six weeks, provided all required documents are submitted and in order

It may take longer for couples who did not complete the PMLA (up to 6 months)

You will be informed of the application outcome via email and may also check the application status online through MyICA or the e-Service

title: Epworth Literacy and Behaviour Intervention Programme
tagline: Offers literacy and behavioural intervention to support children aged 5 to 12 years old with learning difficulties.
text:
Offers literacy and behavioural intervention to support children with literacy and behavioural challenges.

Services include:
* Literacy and/or language intervention - provides children between 5 to 12 years old with skills and strategies to overcome their learning challenges and develop literacy skills.
* Behavioural intervention - supports children between 5 to 12 years old in the aspects of essential executive functioning for learning, task readiness, on-seat behaviour, and basic social competency skills.
* Parenting workshops are also offered for parents to increase their knowledge in effective parenting and understanding the needs of their children.

tagline: Support frail or homebound seniors who require assistance with activities of daily living, and/or general support with routine tasks in the community.
text:
Provided by trained care professionals to assist seniors and their caregivers with:

Personal care needs

Activities of Daily Living (e.g. showering, feeding)

Mind stimulating activities

Simple maintenance exercises prescribed by physiotherapist, occupational therapist, or speech therapist

Elder-sitting and respite for caregivers

General support with routine tasks (e.g. regular check-ins, medication reminders, help with errands)

Monitoring and responding to falls 24/7 using digital devices the client has installed at home

Household chores (e.g. sweeping floors, cleaning fans and windows, and washing toilets)

title: Community Law Centre in North West Community Law Centre @ Realm of Tranquility
tagline: This programme seeks to deliver integrated legal care in the North West District of Singapore by working collaboratively with community partners to holistically help and empower socially excluded individuals.
text:
The key target groups that the programme intends to serve include: 

(1) Socially excluded persons* residing or working in the North West District (based on CDC postal locator) who face legal and other complex issues, and who will benefit from integrated legal care; and 

(2) Community partners such as Family Service Centres, Community Development Councils, Social Service Offices and Social Service Agencies in the North West District. 

*Socially excluded persons are persons who may face significant barriers in accessing legal services due to socio-economic, mobility, psychological, psychiatric, language and technological factors. Such persons may include transnational families and the sandwich class who do not qualify for Government legal aid schemes.

title: Community Law Centre in North West Community Law Centre @ Realm of Tranquility
tagline: This programme seeks to deliver integrated legal care in the North West District of Singapore by working collaboratively with community partners to holistically help and empower socially excluded individuals.
text:
This programme seeks to deliver integrated legal care in the North West District of Singapore by working collaboratively with community partners to holistically help and empower socially excluded individuals.

Centre-based, Helpline, Home-based
Free
Referral not required

Pro Bono SG’s Community Law Centre @ Realm of Tranquility will: 

(1) Facilitate or provide legal guidance and representation to socially and virtually excluded persons; 

(2) Work closely with community partners in the North West district to provide integrated legal care to socially and virtually excluded persons in the community; 

(3) Provide training and support to community partner staff and, where relevant, their volunteers to identify potential unmet legal needs among socially and virtually excluded beneficiaries, including referring those persons for follow-up assistance at the Community Law Centre; 

(4) Provide training and support to volunteer lawyers serving at Pro Bono SG's network of community legal clinics and pro bono representation schemes; 

(5) Conduct law awareness talks for socially and virtually excluded persons/their caregivers to equip them with functional legal knowledge of their rights and protections; and 

(6) Build up a corpus of best practices and knowledge database as a centre of excellence in integrated, holistic socio-legal healing

The key target groups that the programme intends to serve include: 

(1) Socially excluded persons* residing or working in the North West District (based on CDC postal locator) who face legal and other complex issues, and who will benefit from integrated legal care; and 

(2) Community partners such as Family Service Centres, Community Development Councils, Social Service Offices and Social Service Agencies in the North West District. 

*Socially excluded persons are persons who may face significant barriers in accessing legal services due to socio-economic, mobility, psychological, psychiatric, language and technological factors. Such persons may include transnational families and the sandwich class who do not qualify for Government legal aid schemes.

Members of the public and community partners may contact the Community Law Centre @ Realm of Tranquility through the following modes: 

Address: Community Law Centre @ Realm of Tranquility, Blk 547, Woodlands Dr 16, #01-177, Singapore 730547

Office operating hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 am – 12:00 pm and 2:00 pm – 5:00 pm. Closed on weekends and public holidays.

title: Rise to Wellness - Integrated Wellness Recovery Programme (RTW-I)
tagline: RTW-I is an integrated, structured wellness recovery programme which aims to improve and sustain the mental health recovery of persons with mental health condition (PMHCs) and/or addiction issues
text: RTW-I is a 9 to 12 months programme and you will be paired with a dedicated supporter who will journey with you 1-to-1 to understand your recovery needs and recommend curated recovery resources to benefit you. Together, you'll make a personal wellness recovery plan to chart your progress, while there will be opportunities to take part in activities that support and empower your recovery, while strengthening your sense of social connection, inclusion and overall wellbeing.
