title: Severe Disability Assessment (SDA)
tagline: Provides an assessment of your disability status conducted by a Ministry of Health (MOH)-accredited severe disability assessor.
text:
SDA is required if you are:

Applying for long-term care severe disability financial assistance schemes administered by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC)

CareShield Life

ElderShield

ElderFund

Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE)

MediSave Care

Applying for supplement schemes of CareShield Life and ElderShield administered by private insurers – Great Eastern, Income and SingLife;

Undergoing a disability review to ascertain continued scheme eligibility; or

Undergoing an underwriting to enrol into selected schemes.

How to find a severe disability assessor near you:

For service providers providing centre-based and house-call, or centre-based assessments only, visit AIC's website to search by postal code, address, or your device's current location.

For service providers providing house-call assessments only, see the list here.

You are strongly encouraged to bring along any medical reports or discharge summaries when you go for your SDA appointment.

title: Severe Disability Assessment (SDA)
tagline: Provides an assessment of your disability status conducted by a Ministry of Health (MOH)-accredited severe disability assessor.
text:
For anyone who require an SDA to determine your ability to perform each of the 6 Activities of Daily Living, as certified by an MOH-accredited severe disability assessor.

These 6 activities are:

Feeding

Dressing

Toileting

Washing

Walking or moving around

Transferring from bed to chair and vice versa

title: Severe Disability Assessment (SDA)
tagline: Provides an assessment of your disability status conducted by a Ministry of Health (MOH)-accredited severe disability assessor.
text:
Assessments are strictly by appointment only. Please call ahead to make an appointment with the clinic or assessor before going to the clinic.

If your care recipient requires a mental capacity assessment to be completed to obtain certification for the purpose of applying for AIC schemes, please explicitly request for it when making the appointment. Do note that mental capacity assessments can only be completed by doctors.

A typical SDA will last about 45 minutes or longer.

The fees are as follows, if:

You visit an assessor’s clinic: $100

An assessor visits your house: $250

You will be reimbursed the assessment fee which you have paid if you are assessed to be severely disabled.

For CareShield Life claims, the assessment fee for the first disability assessment will be waived, regardless of whether you are assessed to be severely disabled, or whether the claim is successful.

text:
Active ageing programmes:

Provide and promote a variety of activities such as karaoke, arts & craft, cooking and exercising programmes

Befriending & buddying:

Provide social support to seniors through house visits and phone calls

Information and referral to care services:

Provide information on schemes, grants and support

Raise referrals to appropriate services

Management of emergency alert response calls

Social connector for social and lifestyle interventions:

Assist seniors in adherence to their recommendations in their healthplan (e.g. registering for active ageing programmes)

Community Screening:

Render support to seniors in measurement of their vital signs as required in adherence to their health plan

title: Active Ageing Centres (AAC)
tagline: A go-to point for seniors to build strong social connections, take part in recreational activities, access community health services, and contribute to the community.
text:
A go-to point for seniors to build strong social connections, take part in recreational activities, access community health services, and contribute to the community.

Free
Referral not required

Active Ageing Centres (AACs) provide a suite of services which include:

Active Ageing activities to keep seniors engaged within the community;

Befriending services for vulnerable seniors who require additional social support;

Making referrals for Care services when required;

Providing assistance through assuming the role of a social connector for social and lifestyle interventions to support seniors in adhering to their health plan recommendations; and

Supporting seniors in the measurement of their vital signs in adherence to their health plan.

Active Ageing Centre (Care), or AAC (Care), provides AAC services and additional care services, such as day care, community rehabilitation.

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents, regardless of housing types and socio-economic status.

Active ageing programmes:

Provide and promote a variety of activities such as karaoke, arts & craft, cooking and exercising programmes

Befriending & buddying:

Provide social support to seniors through house visits and phone calls

Information and referral to care services:

Provide information on schemes, grants and support

Raise referrals to appropriate services

Management of emergency alert response calls

Social connector for social and lifestyle interventions:

Assist seniors in adherence to their recommendations in their healthplan (e.g. registering for active ageing programmes)

Community Screening:

Render support to seniors in measurement of their vital signs as required in adherence to their health plan

You may drop by an AAC in your neighbourhood to sign up. Visit AIC’s website to search for AACs by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text:
Fees payable
Referral not required

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text:
Advance Care Planning (ACP) is an ongoing process of planning and preparing for your future health and personal care. It includes conversations about your:

personal beliefs

values

healthcare preferences

 

These will be shared with your loved ones and healthcare team.

 

Having a quality ACP ensures that this process is person-centred, informed and guided. In the event that you lose the capacity to make decisions or speak for yourself, the ACP will help your loved ones and healthcare team act in your best interests based on your prior expressed wishes.

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text:
For any individuals aged 21 years old* and above who has the capacity to make their own decisions.

Includes people who are healthy and living with chronic or any other medical conditions.

ACP is particularly important for people who are living with a health condition. You can consult your regular doctor, especially if you have chronic conditions (e.g. diabetes, heart disease, chronic kidney disease) or any other medical concerns.

*For individuals below 21 years old and living with a health condition, please approach your healthcare team if you are interested to find out more about ACP.

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text:
You can:

Use the myACP online service

Complete your ACP without having to speak to an ACP facilitator. You can use the myACP online service if you:

have a Singpass account

are at least 21 years old

do not have any existing serious illness

do not have a Preferred Plan of Care (PPC) ACP or Disease Specific (DS) ACP

Once you’ve completed the online form, your NHS will receive digital invitations. They’ll be notified via SMS, and must accept or reject their appointment by logging in to My Legacy with their Singpass. Your ACP will automatically be registered once all your NHS have accepted their appointments.

Book an appointment with an ACP facilitator

Visit an ACP service provider if you:

prefer to have a conversation with an ACP facilitator and be guided

have an existing serious illness*

Your ACP will be registered in the National Electronic Health Records (NEHR) upon completing all discussions with your facilitator. Some service providers may charge a nominal fee. Please check with the respective organisation for details.

* Serious illnesses include: Cancer, Stroke, End Stage Renal Failure (ESRF), Severe Liver Disease, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), Congestive Heart Failure (CHF), Dementia, and Chronic Neurological Disease.

Book an appointment or visit a ServiceSG Centre today for in-person guidance and assistance for your application. 

You may also refer to this website for more information on steps to prepare your ACP.

title: Advance Care Planning (ACP)
tagline: Helps communicate your values and how they shape your healthcare preferences to your loved ones and healthcare team.
text:
You will need to appoint a Nominated Healthcare Spokesperson (NHS) who is responsible for speaking on your behalf about your healthcare preferences when you are no longer able to do so. In such situations, the healthcare team will consult your NHS and discuss your care plan.

After completing your ACP, it will be available on the National Electronic Health Record (NEHR), where your healthcare team can access and refer to your ACP to prepare your care plan.

text: If you are a pregnant teen who needs help, know of one who needs help or need information about teenage pregnancy, please contact Babes' 24-hour helpline at 6206 6641 or text via Whatsapp at 8111 3535 to speak to the caseworkers.

title: Babes Pregnancy Crisis Support
tagline: For individuals aged 21 and below needing support with their pregnancy, so that they are aware of their options and are able to make an informed decision regarding their pregnancy.
text:
- Teenagers who require help with their pregnancy and/or anyone who needs information about teenage pregnancy may contact Babes via the 24-hour helpline (call/text).
- When an enquiry is attended to, the Helpliner will also assess if the teenager is eligible to be a case under Babes.
- Should the assessment be one that Babes can assist, Babes will arrange for a meeting with the teenager and/or her family/partner within 3-5 working days to assess the needs and provide the necessary support and guidance.
- Babes caseworker will check in with the teenager and/or family about once a week, depending on the client's needs and preferences.

title: Centre-based Nursing
tagline: Basic nursing care for seniors at a centre near their homes.
text:
You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs. 

You may also contact the service provider for a discussion. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

title: Centre-based Nursing
tagline: Basic nursing care for seniors at a centre near their homes.
text:
You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

Type of nursing procedures required and due date for change

The medical report should not be older than 1 year from your application to the service.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

title: Care Corner Learning and Special Needs Support Programme
tagline: Provides literacy, language, and numeracy intervention to children aged between 5 and 13 years old (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.
text:
The programme provides learning support to children in mainstream schools struggling with learning difficulties such as:
* Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
* Autism Spectrum Disorder (ADD)
* Central Auditory Processing Disorder
* Developmental Delay
* Dyslexia
* Dyscalculia
* Dysgraphia
* Dyspraxia
* Specific Language Impairment
* Sensory Processing Disorder

Services includes:
* KidsReady (K1 to K2) - For children attending preschool / development supporting-learning support (DS-LS) programme who need more support in English, Mathematics, handwriting and fine motor skills
* KidsLearn (P1 to P6) - For primary school children needing learning support in English & Mathematics.

title: Community Intervention Team (COMIT)

title: Community Intervention Team (COMIT)
tagline: Provides psychotherapy and psychosocial interventions such as cognitive behavioural therapy and psychoeducation support for individuals with mental health conditions or dementia, and their caregivers.
text:
Free
Referral required

title: Community Intervention Team (COMIT)
tagline: Provides psychotherapy and psychosocial interventions such as cognitive behavioural therapy and psychoeducation support for individuals with mental health conditions or dementia, and their caregivers.
text:
You can approach over 100 community mental health teams in the community islandwide such as COMIT. providing the following support:

Provide assessment, psychotherapy, psychosocial intervention such as cognitive behavioural therapy and psychoeducation, to support the reduction of symptoms for individuals to enable them to live well in the community, and better support the caregiving journey of the caregivers

Provide collaborative and coordinated care for individuals and caregivers through a case management approach.

Work in partnership with health and community care partners, and key stakeholders to co-manage and support individuals with mental health conditions and dementia in the community.

These community mental health teams are developed under the Community Mental Health Masterplan driven by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), together with the Ministry of Health (MOH). The teams are fully funded by the government and there is no out-of-pocket cost.

If you are already seeing a General Practitioner (GP) or Polyclinic doctor, you may approach them for support.

title: Community Outreach Teams (CREST)
tagline: Listens to your concerns and provides support such as therapy, as your in-person First Stop for Mental Health.
text:
You can approach over 100 community mental health teams in the community islandwide such as CREST, providing the following support:

Promote early recognition of signs and symptoms of mental health conditions and/or dementia. 

 Improve mental health conditions and/or dementia literacy by organising targeted outreach events and activities to provide mental health and/or dementia information and education to residents and caregivers in the region.

Provide psychosocial intervention such as techniques to reduce the risk of escalation of mental health needs. 

Provide collaborative and coordinated care for clients and caregivers through a case management approach.

Work in partnership with health and community care partners and key stakeholders to co-manage and support persons with mental health conditions and dementia in the community.

 

These community mental health teams are developed under the Community Mental Health Masterplan driven by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), together with the Ministry of Health (MOH). The teams are fully funded by the government and there is no out-of-pocket cost.

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text:
Centre-based
Free
Referral not required

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text:
A CPF nomination allows you to decide:

Who will receive your CPF savings (your “nominee”)

The percentage of your CPF savings each nominee should receive

CPF savings cannot be distributed by a will as CPF savings do not form part of your estate. This arrangement protects your CPF savings from creditor claims on any outstanding debts you may have and preserves your savings fully for your nominees.

Your CPF nomination will cover:

CPF savings in your Ordinary, Special, MediSave and Retirement Accounts

CPF LIFE premium balance

Your discounted Singtel shares (if any)

Your CPF nomination does not cover:

Properties bought using your CPF savings

Payout from the Dependants' Protection Scheme (DPS)

Investments under CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS)

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text:
To make a CPF nomination, you must:

Be a CPF member

Be aged 16 years and above

Have the mental capacity to make your own decisions

You can nominate any person or legal entity* to be the beneficiary of your CPF savings.

*An entity that is capable of receiving monies in its own right.

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text:
You can:

Apply online using your Singpass

Make an appointment to apply in person at any CPF service centre if you are unable to find two witnesses

Book an appointment to visit a ServiceSG Centre for in-person guidance on your online nomination application. Click here for the list of ServiceSG Centres and their operating hours

For online application

You will need to have:

Your nominees’ particulars

Full Name

NRIC or Foreign Identification Number (FIN)

Mailing Address (for foreigners)

Email Address (optional)

Two witnesses’ particulars

Full Name

NRIC

Email address and/or Singapore-registered mobile phone number

Cannot be a nominee

He/she cannot be a nominee and must be at least 21 years of age

Upon submission of your nomination, inform your witnesses to log in here within 7 calendar days to witness your nomination using their Singpass.

For in-person application

You will need to bring along and prepare:

A copy of your NRIC/passport

Your nominee(s)' NRIC number or Foreign Identification Number (FIN), email (optional), and mailing address (for foreigners)

As CPF Board's customer service executives or ServiceSG staff are acting as your witnesses, you do not need to prepare your witnesses' particulars.

title: CPF Nomination
tagline: Allows you to decide what happens to your CPF savings after your death.
text:
If your nominee falls into the following categories, here is what to expect:

Below 18 Years Old

	

The monies will be held in trust by the Public Trustee until your nominee turns 18.

Unable To Make Decisions Independently

	

The monies will be handled by their court appointed deputy.

Bankrupt

	

The monies belonging to the undischarged bankrupt nominee, will be transferred to the Official Assignee.

To allow your CPF savings to be distributed swiftly according to your wishes when you pass on, it is important to review your CPF nomination regularly, especially at key milestones (e.g.):

Starting Work

Marriage*

Childbirth

Divorce

Death of a nominee

When you reach your golden years**

*Your nomination will be revoked upon marriage, and you should make a new nomination.

**Review your nomination and consider your current family circumstances, such as which family member may need your savings more.

Programmes are aimed at helping seniors regain, maintain and optimise their ability and independence to do daily tasks which include:

Physiotherapy – assessing and treating problems with mobility due to physical disability, trauma or illness such as stroke

Occupational Therapy – assessing and treating people who need to learn, recover or maintain daily living and work skills

Speech Therapy – assessing and treating of swallowing or speaking and communication problems

Centre-based, Online
Free

title: Caregiver Training and Support Programme
tagline: Provides training and support to caregivers of persons with mental health conditions so that they can better cope with their caregiving duties.
text:
CTSP consists of the Caregivers-to-Caregivers (C2C) Programme and Engagement-to-Empowerment (E2E) Programme.

C2C aims to provide caregivers with the knowledge and skills regarding caregiving so that they can better cope with their caregiving duties. C2C provides various types of training courses that range from 8 to 12 sessions and in 3 different languages, to cover general or specific mental health conditions. Through the C2C training courses, caregiver leaders are also identified and trained to volunteer as caregiver leaders to support other caregivers in subsequent C2C classes and /or outreach and advocacy work.

E2E consists of the following components:
- Caregiver-for-Caregiver (C4C) Support Programme: 12 support group sessions over a year, with the aim of increasing support networks for caregivers. The first six sessions will be held in-person and will aim to provide support and equip caregivers with coping skills. Subsequent sessions may be a mix of online or in-person sessions, including self-run activities by caregivers with support from CAL staff, to gather fellow caregivers for social activities or a sharing session.
- Train-the-Trainer (TTT): Caregivers who indicated interest to contribute and identified by CAL staff as having potential are coached and trained as Volunteer Trainers and/or to support advocacy and outreach work through “Train-the-Trainer” modules so they can volunteer to support CAL’s programmes and activities.

title: Deputyship
tagline: For persons who lack mental capacity and have not made a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) and/or have no donee to make certain decisions on their behalf.
text:
You may apply to the court to seek a court order to be appointed as a Deputy.

Anyone above 21 years of age can apply to be a Deputy. A Deputy is usually a family member or close friend who would act in the best interest of the person who lacks capacity.

A doctor has to certify that the patient lacks mental capacity to make decisions due to an impairment of or disturbance in the functioning of the mind or brain

The Family Courts hear all deputyship applications under the Mental Capacity Act

Applicants may need to fulfil the services’ in-take criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any.

text:
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral required

title: Day Activity Centre (DAC)
tagline: Community-based facilities that provide care and skills training to persons with disabilities aged 18 and above to maximise their independence.
text:
The DACs' programme is designed to equip persons with disabilities with daily living skills and community living skills to maximise their independence.

The activities also aim to meaningfully occupy persons with disabilities with social and recreational activities.

Therapeutic and behavioural intervention services may be provided at some centres.

DACs also provide a form of respite for caregivers during the day.

title: Day Activity Centre (DAC)
tagline: Community-based facilities that provide care and skills training to persons with disabilities aged 18 and above to maximise their independence.
text:
Applicants should be:

18 years old and above*^#

Singapore citizens or Permanent Residents^^

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

A hearing impairment: Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form** completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Applicants should not be suffering from any untreated infectious disease.

 

*Students of Government and community-funded SPED schools should be encouraged to remain in school until graduation. Parents of SPED graduands may approach their School to discuss the transition plans of the children prior to graduating from School.

^Applicants who are below age 21 will need consent of parents or legal guardians for their application.

#Applicants who are aged 21 and above with no mental capacity, will need the consent of the appointed deputy(s)/donee(s) on behalf of the applicant.

^^For Permanent Residents, at least one immediate family member must be a Singapore Citizen. Immediate family members refer to spouses, parents, children or the applicant, including stepparents and stepchildren.

**From 8 September 2025, the Disability Verification Form (DVF) is the standardised proof of disability required to verify an individual’s disability status when applying for selected MSF disability schemes and services and other selected Government schemes. Applicants can check if their disability status is already verified via their Touchpoints, SupportGoWhere, or call SG Enable at 1800-8585-885

title: Day Activity Centre (DAC)
tagline: Community-based facilities that provide care and skills training to persons with disabilities aged 18 and above to maximise their independence.
text:
Please approach a social service professional from a touchpoint (e.g. Social Service Agency (SSA), hospitals etc.) for assistance in submitting a DAC application. If you are currently not receiving support from any social service or medical touchpoints, you may approach SG Enable for assistance.

Before meeting your Touchpoints, all applicants are encouraged to check if your disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere with your Singpass. If you do not have a verified disability status, the Touchpoint will guide you to the relevant registered healthcare professionals to assist with DVF.

Submission of applications to SG Enable by Social Service Professional (Online or Hardcopy)

Step 1:

All applicants should check if they have a verified disability status by logging into SupportGoWhere with their Singpass. If the applicant already has a verified disability status, a DVF is not required.

Step 2:

If the applicant does not have a verified disability status or has a new disability condition which has not been verified previously, registered Touchpoint Assessor/Admin must submit a copy of the DVF completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional together with the AD application.

Step 3:

Registered touchpoints’ Assessor/Admin may submit clients’ AD application and completed DVF online via CorpPass (recommended) or via hardcopy.

Online submission (Recommended)

Log into e-Services using your CorpPass

Hardcopy submission

Download the AD application form, print and send together with the required documents. If required, you may download sections of the AD application form and DVF below for use:

Client Assessment Form (Revised)

Medical Information Form

Declaration Form

Disability Verification Form

title: Day Activity Centre (DAC)
tagline: Community-based facilities that provide care and skills training to persons with disabilities aged 18 and above to maximise their independence.
text:
SG Enable will contact you within 10 working days upon receiving the completed application together with the required supporting documents from the touchpoint. SG Enable will also update the social worker who assisted in the application on the status of the application.

Acceptance into the programme at the preferred centre is subject to the availability of vacancies and approval of the centre management.

Referral required

The DACs' programme is designed to equip persons with disabilities with daily living skills and community living skills to maximise their independence.

The activities also aim to meaningfully occupy persons with disabilities with social and recreational activities.

Therapeutic and behavioural intervention services may be provided at some centres.

DACs also provide a form of respite for caregivers during the day.

Applicants should be:

18 years old and above*^#

Singapore citizens or Permanent Residents^^

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

A hearing impairment: Moderate hearing loss or worse in the better ear 

Intellectual disability

Autism

Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form** completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Applicants should not be suffering from any untreated infectious disease.

 

*Students of Government and community-funded SPED schools should be encouraged to remain in school until graduation. Parents of SPED graduands may approach their School to discuss the transition plans of the children prior to graduating from School.

^Applicants who are below age 21 will need consent of parents or legal guardians for their application.

#Applicants who are aged 21 and above with no mental capacity, will need the consent of the appointed deputy(s)/donee(s) on behalf of the applicant.

^^For Permanent Residents, at least one immediate family member must be a Singapore Citizen. Immediate family members refer to spouses, parents, children or the applicant, including stepparents and stepchildren.

**From 8 September 2025, the Disability Verification Form (DVF) is the standardised proof of disability required to verify an individual’s disability status when applying for selected MSF disability schemes and services and other selected Government schemes. Applicants can check if their disability status is already verified via their Touchpoints, SupportGoWhere, or call SG Enable at 1800-8585-885

SG Enable will contact you within 10 working days upon receiving the completed application together with the required supporting documents from the touchpoint. SG Enable will also update the social worker who assisted in the application on the status of the application.

Acceptance into the programme at the preferred centre is subject to the availability of vacancies and approval of the centre management.

Please approach a social service professional from a touchpoint (e.g. Social Service Agency (SSA), hospitals etc.) for assistance in submitting a DAC application. If you are currently not receiving support from any social service or medical touchpoints, you may approach SG Enable for assistance.

Before meeting your Touchpoints, all applicants are encouraged to check if your disability status has already been verified by logging into SupportGoWhere with your Singpass. If you do not have a verified disability status, the Touchpoint will guide you to the relevant registered healthcare professionals to assist with DVF.

Submission of applications to SG Enable by Social Service Professional (Online or Hardcopy)

Step 1:

All applicants should check if they have a verified disability status by logging into SupportGoWhere with their Singpass. If the applicant already has a verified disability status, a DVF is not required.

Step 2:

If the applicant does not have a verified disability status or has a new disability condition which has not been verified previously, registered Touchpoint Assessor/Admin must submit a copy of the DVF completed by a relevant registered Healthcare Professional together with the AD application.

Step 3:

Registered touchpoints’ Assessor/Admin may submit clients’ AD application and completed DVF online via CorpPass (recommended) or via hardcopy.

Online submission (Recommended)

Log into e-Services using your CorpPass

Hardcopy submission

Download the AD application form, print and send together with the required documents. If required, you may download sections of the AD application form and DVF below for use:

Client Assessment Form (Revised)

Medical Information Form

Declaration Form

Disability Verification Form

You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

The medical report should not be older than 1 year from your application to the service.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

title: Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children - P (EIPIC-P)
tagline: EIPIC-P is a programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0 to below 7 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.
text:
Children who are:

From birth to 6 years old.

Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents.

Assessed by a paediatrician to be at risk of a developmental, intellectual, sensory or physical disability, or a combination of disabilities; and

Not concurrently enrolled in any other ECDA-funded EI programmes (i.e. EIPIC, DS-LS, DS-Plus programmes).

title: Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children - P (EIPIC-P)
tagline: EIPIC-P is a programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0 to below 7 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.
text:
You may approach a doctor at any polyclinic to obtain a referral for assessment at the Department of Child Development (DCD) at KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital (KKH) or Child Development Unit (CDU) at National University Hospital (NUH).

Alternatively, you may approach a private paediatrician for an assessment.

If your child is assessed to be suitable for EIPIC, the hospital or the private paediatrician will recommend EIPIC and submit the application to SG Enable upon your consent.

More information can be found on the Enabling Guide.

title: Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children - P (EIPIC-P)
tagline: EIPIC-P is a programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0 to below 7 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.
text:
To be eligible for EIPIC-P, the child should be referred by a paediatrician to the Early Intervention (EI) Programme , with the referral made through SG Enable.

Upon receiving the referral, SG Enable will contact you within 10 working days to provide necessary information to help you make an informed decision on your choice of an EI Centre (government-funded EIPIC centre or EIPIC-P centre).

Your selected EI Centre will contact you within 2 weeks of referral to arrange an interview and assessment of your child.

Enrolment into a suitable programme at the preferred centre is subject to the availability of vacancies at the centre, and approval of the respective centre management.

title: Early Intervention Programme for Infants & Children (EIPIC)
tagline: EIPIC is a centre-based programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0-6 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.
text:
EIPIC is a centre-based programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0 to 6 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.

The continuum of EI services include:

EIPIC Under-2s

Targeted at children under 2 years old

Trains parents/caregivers so that they can effectively carry out intervention strategies in the child’s daily routines at home

EIPIC@Centre

Provides both therapy and educational intervention services, typically in small groups

DS-Plus

Provided in preschools targeted at children aged 2 to 6 years requiring low levels of EI support, and are attending preschools.

title: Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples
tagline: Provides pre- and post-marriage support for minor and young couples.
text:
Minor Couples (Mandatory to complete Marriage Preparation Programme before applying for marriage):

Either party is below 21 years old

At least one party is Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Both bride and groom are Muslims

Young Couples:

Groom is between 21 and 24 years old and bride is aged 21 years and above

At least one party is Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Both bride and groom are Muslims

title: Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples
tagline: Provides pre- and post-marriage support for minor and young couples.
text: Call, email, visit or through referrals by other agencies to apply.

title: Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples
tagline: Provides pre- and post-marriage support for minor and young couples.
text:
For minor couples

Sign up (email or call) directly with the agency for the mandatory (requried by law) complimentary Marriage Preparation Programme (MPP) before proceeding with marriage application via Registry of Muslim Marriages (ROMM) website.

Upon completion of MPP, the agency will provide casework and counselling if necessary for up to 10 years and refer couples with higher risks to FSC or SSO.

Eligible couples will also be auto-referred to KidStart. Those who prefer not to be auto-referred, can complete an opt out form instead for the agency to follow-up with them.

For young couples

Similar as above, except that the MPP is not mandatory for young couples.

They can apply e-file for marriage (OMJ) before attending MPP.

Upon completion of the MPP, the other services offered to minor couples are applicable to young couples too.

title: EQUAL Youth Programme
tagline: Provides social support to at-risk children and youth aged between 7 and 21 years old to build Habits of Mind and develop resilience through equine-facilitated psychotherapy.
text:
EQUAL Youth is a youth developmental programme which aims to provide social support and develop socio-emotional skills of at-risk children and youth through equine-assisted activities.

The outcomes of the programme are:
a) To develop socio-emotional skills of children and youth (e.g. impulsivity management, confidence and resilience building), and
b) To improve family functioning

The programme aims to help children of incarcerated parents develop resilience to cope with parental incarceration and stay connected with their incarcerated parent(s) to strengthen the parent-child relationship, as well as to support their caregivers in caring for them.

Centre-based
Free
Referral required

The following core services are provided:
- Case management
- Befriending / mentoring for the children
- Group activities for the children
- Academic support
- Caregiver support sessions
- Family bonding activities
- Milestone prison visits

Children of incarcerated parents (aged 6-21)

Caregivers of children with incarcerated parents

Incarcerated parents

An intake interview will be arranged to assess suitability.

Upon successful admission, services will be provided to the service users for the duration of the programme.

text:
Centre-based
Nominal charges may apply
Referral not required

title: Family Service Centre (FSC)
tagline: FSCs provide support for vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues, and work towards stability, self-reliance and social mobility.
text:
FSCs are community-based social services that provide support for vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues. Social Work Practitioners at the FSCs provide case management support and also partner with other agencies to coordinate holistic support for clients to help them achieve stability, self-reliance, and social mobility.

FSC services include:

Information and Referral: Providing information about social services, and linking individuals and families to appropriate social services and community resources based on the assessed needs

Casework: Providing holistic case management and individual/family counselling

Group Work: Bringing individuals and families with common needs together for purposeful group interactions to address their concerns and bring about positive change

Community Work: Tapping on community resources to help individuals and families support and empower one another.

title: Family Service Centre (FSC)
tagline: FSCs provide support for vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues, and work towards stability, self-reliance and social mobility.
text:
FSCs are open to families and individuals:

Regardless of age, race, language or religion;

At different life stages (such as couples, families with young children, adolescents, or elderly members); and

Of various family structures

title: Family Service Centre (FSC)
tagline: FSCs provide support for vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues, and work towards stability, self-reliance and social mobility.
text:
Call, walk in or email the FSCs for help on family, socio-emotional, interpersonal or other social issues. 
To find the FSC in your neighbourhood, you can call ComCare hotline at 1800 222 0000 or visit 
https://www.msf.gov.sg/our-services/directories.

title: Family Service Centre (FSC)
tagline: FSCs provide support for vulnerable individuals and families with social and emotional issues, and work towards stability, self-reliance and social mobility.
text:
Upon contact with a FSC, a FSC worker will assess the needs of the individual/family and determine the type of support needed.

If FSC services are required, the FSC will then work with the individual/family on their issues with the appropriate social work interventions.

If the FSC’s services are not required and other supportive services by other partners can help to meet these needs, the FSC worker will refer the individual/family to appropriate social services or community resources.

title: Family Strengthening Programme
tagline: Supports incarcerated parents, and their children and families, to break the cycle of incarceration through early intervention and preventive measures.
text: Supports incarcerated parents, and their children and families, to break the cycle of incarceration through early intervention and preventive measures.

title: Family Strengthening Programme
tagline: Supports incarcerated parents, and their children and families, to break the cycle of incarceration through early intervention and preventive measures.
text:
Centre-based, Home-based
Free
Referral required

title: Family Strengthening Programme
tagline: Supports incarcerated parents, and their children and families, to break the cycle of incarceration through early intervention and preventive measures.
text:
The programme supports incarcerated mothers and fathers, and their children and families through:
- Befriending (Early Reader): one-on-one befriending and reading with the children
- In-Care Prison Engagements: incarcerated mothers and fathers are involved in thematically structured groupwork sessions (separately conducted for the mothers and fathers) which promote a sense of peer helping and support among the group members; sessions for the inmates to write and record personalised stories for their children; shared parent-and-child activities (such as Family Play and face-to-face visits)
- Family Advocacy & Care: holistic care and support will be provided to meet the family’s needs, in collaboration with other agencies. Services include therapeutic care and developmental support, and case management.
- Post-Release Parenting Engagements: Desistor Network (peer support groups)
- Experiential Learning Journeys: educational field trips or activities for the children
- Caregiver Peer Support Groups
- Mama Bae: one-on-one befriending support and case management for incarcerated mothers immediately post childbirth

title: Family Strengthening Programme
tagline: Supports incarcerated parents, and their children and families, to break the cycle of incarceration through early intervention and preventive measures.
text: New Life Stories will support the families for a total of three years (typically two years with the parent in prison and one year after release from prison). Subsequently, New Life Stories will refer the families to other suitable agencies, should they continue to require additional support.

text:
Protection Specialist Centres (PSC) provide a holistic range of socio-emotional, supportive and practical services to survivors, including their families and other vulnerable members in the family in order to address the danger and likelihood of future harm posed to the survivors and meet the survivors’ needs.

Services provided include:

Casework

Counselling

Application of personal protection orders

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)
tagline: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.
text: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)
tagline: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.
text:
Centre-based
Free
Referral not required

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)
tagline: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.
text:
SPIN aims to empower single parents and lone caregivers to be resilient and self-reliant, and to help them attain increased capacity in caregiving/parenting, improve their social support, psychological well-being and increase their access to practical resources.

Services include:
* Needs assessment by social workers and co-creation of individualised care plans with single parents/lone caregivers to address identified needs.
* Counselling for single parents and children who have been assessed to be in need for emotional and mental health support.
* Befriending and peer support for single parents to build social support and trust amongst other parents and volunteers
* Tuition and homework support for the children (aged 7 to 17) of the clients
* Events and workshops for single parents to participate in that are designed to help them achieve specific outcomes in their care plans, such as social and therapeutic support, and acquisition of skills and knowledge.
* Practical support such as referrals to resource channels to support clients in meeting identified needs, disbursement of grocery vouchers and food items

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)

Single parents or lone caregivers of children below 18 years old, and these children

No income criteria

No geographical boundaries

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)
tagline: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.
text: You may (1) Approach any Family Service Centre (FSC) for referral, or (2) sign up online at https://crm.hcsa.org.sg/spin-member-application-form/ to fill up the online registration form, or (3) Call or email the programme for assistance in signing up.

title: HCSA Dayspring SPIN (Single Parents: Informed, Involved, Included)
tagline: SPIN provides support to single parents and lone caregivers in the form of informal social support network, counselling and an online portal of information and resources.
text:
A social work associate will respond to the enquiry/referral within 3-5 working days and arrange for intake assessment. 

A social worker will be assigned to the parent, and trained befrienders will be matched to the parent.  

The programme is for a maximum period of 12 months.  

After the programme ends, the parent will be invited to join the Alumni Chapter continue staying connected with a community and contribute to a supportive network of single-parent families.

title: Home Medical
tagline: Check-ups, prescriptions and consultations by a doctor for seniors who are not able to leave their homes.
text:
You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs. 

You may also contact the service provider for a discussion. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

title: Home Medical
tagline: Check-ups, prescriptions and consultations by a doctor for seniors who are not able to leave their homes.
text:
You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

The medical report should not be older than 1 year from your application to the service.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

title: Home Personal Care (HPC)
tagline: Supports frail or homebound seniors who require help with activities of daily living and/or housekeeping.
text:
Provided by trained care professionals to assist seniors and their caregivers with:

Activities of Daily Living (e.g. showering, light housekeeping)

Medication

Mind stimulating activities

Elder-sitting and respite for caregivers

Light household chores (e.g. sweeping floors, cleaning fans and windows, and washing toilets)

Note: From 1 April 2026, HPC will be enhanced to provide more tailored and frequent support for clients, including those with higher care needs. On top of the ADLs and IADLs support, enhanced HPC also provides new services such as technology-enabled monitoring to assure caregivers of the client’s safety at home. Refer to the link for more information regarding enhanced Home Personal Care.

title: Halogen SPARK!
tagline: A structured evidence-based entrepreneurship curriculum that targets Secondary 3 students who are from the G1/G2 Subject Based Banding (SBB) Stream that improves better school-focused outcomes, including their attendance and academic performance.
text:
Halogen SPARK! is an upstream intervention programme that targets Secondary students who are from the G1/G2 SBB stream. It targets pre/low/mid-risk students from the Financial Assistance Scheme (FAS) and/or come from challenging and disadvantaged backgrounds.

The programme is a structured evidence-based programme developed by NFTE (Network for Teaching Entrepreneurship), where the key objectives are to increase service users' social-emotional and academic competencies through the incorporation of English and Math in the programme curriculum. It supports the development of numeracy and literacy skills through the entrepreneurship modules, and aims to instill self-confidence and self-efficacy in youths.

title: Home Therapy
tagline: For seniors who require home-based rehabilitation to regain their functional abilities.
text:
Improves quality of life of seniors in the comfort of their own homes with:

Speech therapy – assessing and treating those with problems swallowing and speaking, or have language or communication problems

Occupational therapy – assessing and treating people who need to learn, recover or maintain daily living and work skills

Physiotherapy – assessing and treating those with injuries or other conditions to relieve pain, improve and restore functional ability

text:
Fees payable
Referral required

title: Inpatient Hospice Palliative Care Service (IHPCS)
tagline: Serves end-of-life patients who cannot be cared for at home and require inpatient care.
text:
The hospice care team of doctors, nurses, medical social workers, counsellors and therapists, bring comfort for the patient and loved ones through:

Specialist palliative care focusing on the management of symptoms such as pain, breathlessness, nausea and vomiting

Emotional, spiritual and grief support and counselling

Palliative rehabilitation, including physiotherapy and occupational therapy

Music and art therapy

title: Inpatient Hospice Palliative Care Service (IHPCS)
tagline: Serves end-of-life patients who cannot be cared for at home and require inpatient care.
text:
Patient with a life-limiting illness with prognosis of less than 3 months who:

Diagnosed by their doctor to have an advanced and progressive life-limiting illness needing specialised care, especially in the last phase of life when care at home, in nursing home or outpatient setting is not possible.

Requires time-limited care up to 28 calendar days for acute symptom exacerbation that cannot be provided in outpatient setting with an estimated prognosis of 12 months or less

Has sub-acute clinical needs and/or short-term exacerbations, which require higher skilled specialist knowledge and skills

Is a Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

title: Inpatient Hospice Palliative Care Service (IHPCS)
tagline: Serves end-of-life patients who cannot be cared for at home and require inpatient care.
text:
Application can be made by any medical doctor caring for the patient. Upon review of the application, the hospice will inform your referring doctor/institution on the application outcome. Approved applications will then be allocated a date for the hospice admission.

Financial assistance is available for those who require and qualify for additional financial aid. You may approach your medical social worker if you have further financial concerns.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

You can visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

title: Kids In Play (KIP)
tagline: The programme supports children to cope with their parent’s incarceration, children and their incarcerated parent(s) to have a positive parent-child relationship, and caregivers to be able to cope with the impact of incarceration on themselves and their families.
text:
The core services provided are as follows:
- EMPower (EMP) groupwork sessions that focus on empowering children to be resilient individuals and providing a community of support for the children
- Caregivers Connect is a support group for caregivers
- Family Bonding Programme (FBP) lets the children interact with their incarcerated parents without physical barriers
- Casework and Counselling are provided to the children and their caregivers to address the impact of incarceration

title: Kids In Play (KIP)
tagline: The programme supports children to cope with their parent’s incarceration, children and their incarcerated parent(s) to have a positive parent-child relationship, and caregivers to be able to cope with the impact of incarceration on themselves and their families.
text: The programme serves children and youth aged 5 to 18 with incarcerated parent(s). It also serves the caregivers, inmates and the family, regardless of race and religion.

title: Kids In Play (KIP)

text: Referrals are received from organisations such as the Singapore Prison Service and social service agencies, as well as self-referrals from families. You may also call 9248 5334 or email kidsinplay@smm.salvationarmy.org to find out if you are eligible.

At the point of intake and registration, the clients will be informed that the maximum time frame they can be on the programme is 3 years

title: Kids In Play (KIP)

The programme supports children to cope with their parent’s incarceration, children and their incarcerated parent(s) to have a positive parent-child relationship, and caregivers to be able to cope with the impact of incarceration on themselves and their families.

Centre-based, Home-based
Free
Referral required

The core services provided are as follows:
- EMPower (EMP) groupwork sessions that focus on empowering children to be resilient individuals and providing a community of support for the children
- Caregivers Connect is a support group for caregivers
- Family Bonding Programme (FBP) lets the children interact with their incarcerated parents without physical barriers
- Casework and Counselling are provided to the children and their caregivers to address the impact of incarceration

The programme serves children and youth aged 5 to 18 with incarcerated parent(s). It also serves the caregivers, inmates and the family, regardless of race and religion.

The case will be registered if the inmate is eligible and approved by the Singapore Prison Service, and the caregiver and child(ren) are agreeable to participate in the programme. At the point of intake and registration, the clients will be informed that the maximum time frame they can be on the programme is 3 years

Referrals are received from organisations such as the Singapore Prison Service and social service agencies, as well as self-referrals from families. You may also call 9248 5334 or email kidsinplay@smm.salvationarmy.org to find out if you are eligible.

title: SHINE Children and Youth Services - Learning and Social Support Programme
tagline: Provides literacy and language intervention to children aged between 7 and 12 years old (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.
text:
Provides literacy and language intervention to children aged 7 to 12 years (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.

Services include:
* Actualise Learning Potential and Promote Skills (ALPS) Language and literacy intervention programme - addresses the learning needs of children between 7 to 12 years old who typically lag 2 to 3 years behind their same-aged peers in language or literacy levels.
* Parenting sessions to equip and empower parents and caregivers to understand the needs of their children and better engage with them.
* Reading Odyssey - enhances the reading ability of children between 7 to 12 years old and to improve their motivation to read.

tagline: Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
text:
Centre-based, Home-based
Free
Referral required

title: Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
tagline: Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
text:
Live On! is a suicide intervention programme that targets youths aged 12 to 18 years old with suicidality, and includes their family members in the intervention.

Objectives of the programme includes:

Providing immediate support to youths with suicidal thoughts or attempts in the community

Providing continuous community support for youths and the families once they are discharged from primary care institutions

De-escalating youths' emotions and strengthening family support through individual and family counselling

title: Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
tagline: Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
text: 12 to 18 years old youths who have been discharged from IMH due to suicidality.

title: Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
tagline: Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
text:
This programme is only eligible for youths who have been discharged by IMH due to suicidality, and have been assessed by an IMH clinician to be eligible for referral.

Call 63892000 or visit IMH website for more information.

title: Live On! by TOUCH Community Services
tagline: Supports youths with high suicide risks as they transit from hospitalisation into the community with the support of the family.
text:
This programme consists of individual counselling for the youths, as well as family sessions.

Individual sessions (for the youth)

In the acute phase, the youth will meet with the social worker / counsellor / psychologist weekly for 6 to 12 sessions to de-escalate emotions

In the continuation phase, the youth will meet with the social worker / counsellor / psychologist twice a month, for another 5 to 12 sessions

After the above is completed, the youth will remain in contact with the caseworker through calls and text messages for 3 to 6 months for continuous support and monitoring

Family sessions (for the caregivers of the youth)

Meet with the social worker / counsellor twice a month from the start of the programme for 4 to 6 sessions to learn skills on how to support the youth better, which includes safety planning and crisis management

School counsellors will also be contacted, with client and parental consent.

title: Local Outreach to Suicide Survivors (LOSS)
tagline: Provides immediate and long-term emotional support to those who have lost a loved one or someone they knew to suicide.
text:
LOSS Programme comprises:

Support group for persons bereaved by suicide

SOS professional staff trained in grief work and trained volunteers will provide emotional support through email, phone calls, SMSes and counselling sessions (if necessary) to those who lost a loved one or someone they knew to suicide.

SOS also serves individuals referred from community partners (i.e. police, hospitals, SSAs) or concerned third party.

Persons bereaved by suicide

When you reach out to SOS via call, text or email, SOS staff or trained volunteer will provide emotional support and if needed, encourage you to come for grief counselling and attend support groups subsequently for longer term support.

Reach out to SOS via 24/7 hotline: 1767; CAREmail: pat@sos.org.sg; and Care Text: WhatsApp 9151 1767

title: Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA)
tagline: A legal document that allows you (the 'Donor') to appoint one or more trusted persons (the 'Donee') to make decisions on your behalf in the event you lose mental capacity.
text:
Donors must:

Be 21 years old and above

Have the mental capacity to make your own decisions

Not be an undischarged bankrupt if you wish to make an LPA for property and affairs matters

Donees must:

Be 21 years old and above

Be your spouse or partner, family member or relative, friend, or a licensed trust company for property and affair matters only

Have the mental capacity to make their own decisions

title: Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA)
tagline: A legal document that allows you (the 'Donor') to appoint one or more trusted persons (the 'Donee') to make decisions on your behalf in the event you lose mental capacity.
text:
Here are the steps on how to make an LPA:

Step 1: Decide on the type of LPA form

LPA Form 1 grants general powers to Donee(s) who will make decisions on your behalf when you lose mental capacity

LPA Form 2 is for applicants who want to appoint more than 2 Donees and/or more than 1 replacement Donee, and grant specific or customised powers that are not in the scope of LPA Form 1. You will need to engage a lawyer for LPA Form 2.

Step 2: Find an LPA certificate issuer who is:

A medical professional accredited by the OPG

A registered psychiatrist

A practising lawyer (excluding lawyers registered under section 36E of the Legal Profession Act 1966)

You and the LPA certificate issuer must digitally sign the LPA using the Singpass app. Your Donees can sign electronically by accepting their appointment. 

For your LPA to be legally valid, the certificate issuer must submit the LPA application to Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) via OPG Online (OPGO), as authorised by you.

text:
You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

The medical report should not be older than 1 year from your application to the service.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text:
Centre-based, Helpline, Online
Free
Referral not required

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text:
MSC empowers individuals by providing knowledge of their marital and maintenance rights while offering comprehensive support in collaboration with community partners.

**Maintenance Application**
* Assists in the filing of Fresh Maintenance Applications (FMO) via the Integrated Family Application Management System (iFAMS)
* Assists in the Enforcement of Maintenance Orders (EMO) through video-link filing (iFAMS)
* Appointments must be made in advance

**Free Legal Clinics**
* Offers Civil Family Law legal clinic on the first and third Monday evenings of each month
* Offers Syariah Family Law legal clinic on the third Monday of each month
* Legal clinics are open to individuals residing in Singapore with an income cap criteria

**Court Process Guidance**
* Offers legal guidance which is the explanation of legal terms and processes under the Family Law

**Counselling**
* Provides face-to-face and phone counselling for individuals who require emotional support for marital issues, post-divorce/separations, parenthood struggles, and/or similar concerns
* Counselling services are chargeable, based on clients’ financial capabilities

**MSC Talks**
** Talks conducted by guest speakers who are experts in Family and Syariah law
** These are open to staff and volunteers of community partners. They aim to educate and inform the public and social service professionals on issues regarding Family and Syariah law.

MSC also assists in responding to enquiries by sharing information or referring them to other community partners.

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text: Services are rendered to individuals residing in Singapore who face financial abuse, marital issues, or have a Singapore court order and require legal support to navigate family law matters. This includes individuals who are dealing with unpaid maintenance, divorce proceedings, and financial difficulties arising from marital conflicts.

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text:
You may apply to file Fresh or Enforcement of Maintenance application online (https://ifams.gov.sg/sop/#iFAMS). Alternatively, you may also contact the MSC programme's hotline at 6571 0185 to make an appointment.

You can register for the online legal clinic (https://forms.office.com/r/4JucwmuQfc). If you qualify, programme staff will get back to you by 3 working days on the details.

You may register for counselling service online (https://forms.office.com/r/mvdxTJcBFF) or call the hotline at 6571 0185.

title: Maintenance Support Central (MSC)
tagline: Provides support and assistance for clients who have difficulty in receiving spousal and/or child maintenance.
text:
Service users who require the programme’s services will need to complete a registration form. Staff will confirm the appointments with the service user within 3 to 5 working days of form submission.

For clients who register for legal clinic or counselling service, the processing time is 3 working days. All other services can be arranged at the time of contact.

**Counselling**

** These are open to staff and volunteers of community partners. They aim to educate and inform the public and social service professionals on issues regarding Family and Syariah law.

MSC also assists in responding to enquiries by sharing information or referring them to other community partners.

Services are rendered to individuals residing in Singapore who face financial abuse, marital issues, or have a Singapore court order and require legal support to navigate family law matters. This includes individuals who are dealing with unpaid maintenance, divorce proceedings, and financial difficulties arising from marital conflicts.

Service users who require the programme’s services will need to complete a registration form. Staff will confirm the appointments with the service user within 3 to 5 working days of form submission.

For clients who register for legal clinic or counselling service, the processing time is 3 working days. All other services can be arranged at the time of contact.

You may apply to file Fresh or Enforcement of Maintenance application online (https://ifams.gov.sg/sop/#iFAMS). Alternatively, you may also contact the MSC programme's hotline at 6571 0185 to make an appointment.

You can register for the online legal clinic (https://forms.office.com/r/4JucwmuQfc). If you qualify, programme staff will get back to you by 3 working days on the details.

You may register for counselling service online (https://forms.office.com/r/mvdxTJcBFF) or call the hotline at 6571 0185.

title: Nursing Home
tagline: A long-term residential care facility for seniors who have limited or no caregiver support, and require nursing care and assistance with most of their activities of daily living.
text:
Round-the-clock nursing services include:

Help with activities of daily living such as going to the toilet, showering, eating, etc.

Nursing care such as management of nasogastric tube, urine catheter, wound dressing, etc.

Physical and social activities such as maintenance exercises, outings, etc.

For selected nursing homes with dementia and/or psychiatric facilities, services include:

Programmes and activities for cognitive stimulation will be provided to slow down the deterioration of mental function of seniors

title: Nursing Home
tagline: A long-term residential care facility for seniors who have limited or no caregiver support, and require nursing care and assistance with most of their activities of daily living.
text:
You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

Type of nursing procedures required and due date for change

Chest X-Ray report

COVID-19 vaccination history

The medical report/discharge summary and chest X-Ray results, should dated no earlier than 6 months from the intended date of admission.

Additional documents may be required upon request to facilitate the application.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

title: Nursing Home
tagline: A long-term residential care facility for seniors who have limited or no caregiver support, and require nursing care and assistance with most of their activities of daily living.
text:
A long-term residential care facility for seniors who have limited or no caregiver support, and require nursing care and assistance with most of their activities of daily living.

Fees payable
Referral required

Round-the-clock nursing services include:

Help with activities of daily living such as going to the toilet, showering, eating, etc.

Nursing care such as management of nasogastric tube, urine catheter, wound dressing, etc.

Physical and social activities such as maintenance exercises, outings, etc.

For selected nursing homes with dementia and/or psychiatric facilities, services include:

Programmes and activities for cognitive stimulation will be provided to slow down the deterioration of mental function of seniors

Senior applying to stay in a nursing home must be:

Physically and/or mentally disabled because of illness;

Semi-mobile, using a wheelchair or is bedbound, and needs daily care and help with activities like going to the toilet or walking;

Unable to be cared for at home by family or paid domestic worker, and tried all other care options such as using day care, home medical, home nursing services, etc; and

Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

Type of nursing procedures required and due date for change

Chest X-Ray report

COVID-19 vaccination history

The medical report/discharge summary and chest X-Ray results, should dated no earlier than 6 months from the intended date of admission.

Additional documents may be required upon request to facilitate the application.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

You can visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

title: Nursing Home Respite Care
tagline: A stay-in, subsidised care option with healthcare services for seniors with high care needs if caregivers need a short break on an ad hoc basis during the day.
text:
For seniors

Need help in most of their activities of daily living and/or have daily nursing care needs

Has stable medical condition

Does not need urgent medical attention

Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

For family or caregivers

Looking for short-term respite when they want to take a break from caregiver roles, or

Caregivers are not available, e.g. when foreign domestic helper goes on home leave

title: Nursing Home Respite Care

text:
You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs.

You may also contact the service provider for a discussion. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

title: Nursing Home Respite Care
tagline: A stay-in, subsidised care option with healthcare services for seniors with high care needs if caregivers need a short break on an ad hoc basis during the day.
text:
You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

Type of nursing procedures required and due date for change

Chest X-Ray report

COVID-19 vaccination history

Supporting documents (e.g. helper's air tickets)

The medical report/discharge summary and chest X-Ray results, should dated no earlier than 6 months from the intended date of admission.

Additional documents may be required upon request to facilitate the application.

There must be a discharge care plan for your loved one at the end of the respite care period.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

Preparing earlier will ensure that you have sufficient time to work out the necessary arrangements with your preferred centre
